Pinned by `ca_5_percent_late_fee_with_both_prongs_compliant`, `ny_50_dollar_or_5_percent_lesser_cap` ($200K monthly rent → $50 cap), `ny_low_rent_5_percent_lower_than_50_dollars` ($50K rent → $25 cap from 5%), `fl_manufactured_home_park_20_percent_or_20_dollar_floor`, `fl_low_rent_20_dollar_floor_overrides_20_percent`, `tx_4_or_fewer_units_12_percent_safe_harbor`, `tx_5_or_more_units_10_percent_safe_harbor`, `default_jurisdiction_5_percent_cap`.

**California Orozco v. Casimiro two-prong validity test — Cal. Civ. Code § 1671(d)**:

| Prong | Test | Source |
|-------|------|--------|
| 1 | Impracticable OR extremely difficult to fix actual damage at lease execution | § 1671(d)(1) |
| 2 | Amount is REASONABLE ENDEAVOR to estimate FAIR AVERAGE COMPENSATION for loss sustained | § 1671(d)(2) |

BOTH prongs required. Late fee struck down is VOID IN TOTO — landlord recovers $0; tenant recovers charged amount as damages. Pinned by `ca_missing_impracticable_prong_voids_fee`, `ca_missing_reasonable_endeavor_prong_voids_fee`, `ca_void_fee_recovers_charged_amount_as_damages` ($50K charged → $50K tenant damages).

**New York mandatory 5-day grace + no-eviction rule — N.Y. Real Prop. Law § 238-a**: no late fee unless rent unpaid at least 5 days past due date; landlord CANNOT EVICT solely for unpaid late fees (warrant of eviction tracks rent + permissible fees only). Pinned by `ny_4_day_grace_period_violation` (day 4 = violation), `ny_5_day_grace_period_boundary_compliant` (day 5 = compliant), `ny_eviction_restriction_note_present`.

**Texas TREBLE damage uniquely engages — Tex. Prop. Code § 92.019(d)**: $100 PLUS TREBLE the late fee collected in violation PLUS reasonable attorney fees. Pinned by `tx_late_fee_above_cap_triggers_treble_plus_100` ($30K late fee × 3 + $100 = $90,100 = 90,100 + 10,000 cents). Pinned by `tx_uniquely_engages_treble_damages_invariant` (TX only — CA + NY + FL + Default do not).

**NY uniquely hard-caps at $50 dollars** — pinned by `ny_uniquely_hard_caps_at_50_dollars_invariant`: at $100K monthly rent, NY caps at $50 ($5,000 cents); CA + TX caps scale with rent and are higher.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/tenant-late-fee-cap`. Thirty-six tests pin: **CA 5% late fee with both prongs compliant**; **CA missing impracticable prong voids fee**; **CA missing reasonable endeavor prong voids fee**; **CA void fee recovers charged amount as damages**; **NY $50 or 5% lesser cap** ($200K rent = $50 cap); **NY low rent 5% lower than $50** ($50K rent = $25 cap); **NY late fee at $50 cap exactly compliant**; **NY late fee one cent above cap violation**; **NY 4-day grace period violation**; **NY 5-day grace period boundary compliant**; **NY eviction restriction note present**; **FL manufactured home park 20% or $20 floor**; **FL low rent $20 floor overrides 20%**; **TX 4-or-fewer units 12% safe harbor**; **TX 5-or-more units 10% safe harbor**; **TX late fee above cap triggers treble + $100**; **TX 1-day grace period violation**; **TX 2-day grace period boundary compliant**; **default jurisdiction 5% cap**; **default above cap violation cites Restatement**; **citation pins all authorities** (§ 1671(d) + Orozco + § 238-a HSTPA 2019 + § 83.808 + § 92.019 + Restatement 356); **note pins California two-prong test**; **note pins California 5-6% threshold + void in toto**; **note pins New York $50 or 5% hard cap**; **note pins New York 5-day grace + no eviction**; **note pins Florida manufactured home park $20 or 20%**; **note pins Florida general no statutory cap**; **note pins Texas 12/10 safe harbor**; **note pins Texas 2-day grace**; **note pins Texas treble + $100 remedy**; **note pins default Restatement 356**; **note pins cross-jurisdictional invariant**; **NY uniquely hard-caps at $50 invariant**; **TX uniquely engages treble damages invariant**; **jurisdiction truth table five cells**; **defensive overflow clamped with saturating mul**.

`traderview-expense::tenant_lease_guarantor_disclosure` is the **multi-jurisdictional tenant lease guarantor disclosure and restriction framework module** — trader-landlord critical because (1) lease guarantors are widely used by landlords to secure rent payments from high-risk tenants, but state law increasingly restricts both the amount a guarantor can be obligated to pay AND the procedural protections required when soliciting guarantors; (2) NY HSTPA 2019 + DHCR guidance limits ALL deposits and guarantor security to ONE MONTH'S RENT for rent-stabilized apartments; (3) Federal FCRA requirements apply when landlord pulls guarantor's consumer credit report; (4) common-law suretyship principles (Restatement (Third) of Suretyship and Guaranty) STRICTLY CONSTRUE guarantor liability + EXTINGUISH guaranty on material modifications absent consent. Companion to `tenant_data_privacy`, `rental_application_denial_disclosure`, `tenant_late_fee_cap`, `tenant_rent_receipt_requirement`.

**Four-jurisdiction framework**:

| Jurisdiction | Source | Key Restriction |
|--------------|--------|-----------------|
| **New York** | HSTPA 2019 + DHCR Operational Bulletin 2020-1 + GOL § 5-701(a)(1) + GOL § 7-108 | **ONE MONTH AGGREGATE** security + guaranty cap for rent-stabilized; NO retroactive guarantor; tenant blacklist prohibition $500-$1,000 penalty; > 12-month guaranty must be in writing |
| **California** | Cal. Civ. Code § 2787-2856 + § 2819 + § 1670.5 + § 2799 | Material modification rule (§ 2819 discharges guarantor); unconscionability (§ 1670.5); continuing guaranty revocation (§ 2799) |
| **New Jersey** | N.J.S.A. 46:8-26 + NJ Consumer Fraud Act | Landlord must provide guarantor copy of lease + EXACT MONETARY LIMIT |
| **Default** | Restatement (Third) of Suretyship and Guaranty (1996) | § 41 material modification extinguishes; § 39 novation extinguishes; strict construction |

Pinned by `ny_one_month_aggregate_compliant`, `ny_aggregate_exceeds_one_month_violation`, `ny_non_rent_stabilized_no_aggregate_cap`, `ny_retroactive_guarantor_prohibited`, `ny_blacklist_violation_500_to_1000_penalty`, `ca_material_modification_extinguishes_2819`, `default_material_modification_restatement_41`, `nj_no_copy_of_lease_violation`, `nj_no_monetary_limit_violation`, `jurisdiction_truth_table_four_cells`, `ny_uniquely_engages_blacklist_prohibition_invariant`.

**Statute of Frauds — NY GOL § 5-701(a)(1)** — lease guaranty for term LONGER THAN 12 MONTHS must be IN WRITING signed by guarantor or authorized agent. Pinned by `statute_of_frauds_24_month_oral_unenforceable`, `statute_of_frauds_12_month_oral_enforceable`, `statute_of_frauds_12_month_boundary` (12 = OK orally), `statute_of_frauds_13_month_boundary_requires_writing` (13 = writing required).

**Federal FCRA framework (15 USC § 1681 et seq.)** when landlord pulls guarantor's credit report:

| Section | Rule |
|---------|------|
| § 1681b | **Permissible purpose** required |
| § 1681e | Accuracy requirements for user |
| § 1681m | **Adverse action notice** required (CRA identity + 60-day free copy + dispute right) |
| § 1681n | **Willful violation** = actual + PUNITIVE damages + attorney fees |
| § 1681o | Negligent violation = actual damages + attorney fees |

Pinned by `fcra_adverse_action_notice_required_engages`, `fcra_no_adverse_action_no_notice_required`, `fcra_no_credit_report_no_notice_required`, `fcra_adverse_action_with_notice_no_violation`.

**Common-law suretyship principles (Restatement (Third) of Suretyship and Guaranty (1996))**:

| Section | Rule |
|---------|------|
| § 41 | MATERIAL MODIFICATION of underlying obligation without guarantor's consent EXTINGUISHES guaranty |
| § 39 | NOVATION of underlying obligation EXTINGUISHES guaranty |
| General | Guaranty STRICTLY CONSTRUED against the creditor (landlord); limited-amount guaranties enforced strictly within monetary cap |

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/tenant-lease-guarantor-disclosure`. Thirty-two tests pin: **NY one-month aggregate compliant**; **NY aggregate exceeds one-month violation**; **NY non-rent-stabilized no aggregate cap**; **NY retroactive guarantor prohibited**; **NY blacklist $500-$1,000 penalty**; **Statute of Frauds 24-month oral unenforceable**; **Statute of Frauds 12-month oral enforceable**; **Statute of Frauds 12-month boundary**; **Statute of Frauds 13-month boundary requires writing**; **CA material modification extinguishes § 2819**; **default material modification Restatement § 41**; **NJ no copy of lease violation**; **NJ no monetary limit violation**; **FCRA adverse action notice required engages**; **FCRA no adverse action no notice required**; **FCRA no credit report no notice required**; **FCRA adverse action with notice no violation**; **jurisdiction truth table four cells**; **NY uniquely engages blacklist prohibition invariant**; **citation pins all authorities** (HSTPA 2019 + DHCR + § 5-701(a)(1) + § 7-108 + § 2787-2856 + § 2819 + § 1670.5 + § 2799 + N.J.S.A. 46:8-26 + Consumer Fraud Act + § 1681 + Restatement); **note pins NY HSTPA one-month aggregate**; **note pins NY blacklist prohibition**; **note pins NY GOL § 5-701 Statute of Frauds**; **note pins CA § 2819 material modification**; **note pins CA § 1670.5 unconscionability**; **note pins CA § 2799 continuing guaranty revocation**; **note pins NJ § 46:8-26 lease copy + monetary limit**; **note pins FCRA adverse action notice**; **note pins FCRA willful punitive damages**; **note pins Restatement (Third) Suretyship 1996**; **note pins trader-landlord critical summary**; **multiple failures stack**.

`traderview-expense::security_deposit_bank_disclosure` is the **security deposit bank-location disclosure module** — when must a landlord disclose to the tenant WHERE the security deposit is held? Distinct from sibling modules `security_deposit_caps` (statutory caps on amount), `deposit_interest` (interest-payment obligation on the deposit), `deposit_return_windows` (refund timelines), and `damage_deduction_itemization` (deductions from the deposit). Four regimes ranging from NY's trust-fund + 6+ unit interest-bearing rule to Massachusetts' harsh immediate-return remedy.

**Four regimes**:

| Regime | Authority | Window | Required disclosures | Distinctive feature |
|--------|-----------|--------|----------------------|---------------------|
| New York | N.Y. Gen. Oblig. Law § 7-103 | n/a (immediate) | Bank name + address + amount | **6+ unit interest-bearing requirement**; 1% admin retention |
| New Jersey | N.J.S.A. 46:8-19 | 30 days from receipt | Bank name + address + account type + interest rate + amount | Re-notification on bank/landlord change + annual interest payment |
| Massachusetts | Mass. Gen. Laws c. 186 § 15B(3)(a) | 30 days from receipt | Bank name + location + amount + account number | **§ 15B(6) IMMEDIATE RETURN remedy** — harshest in U.S. |
| Default | None (CA/TX/FL/etc.) | n/a | None required | Lease provision controls if any |

**New York § 7-103 — trust-fund + 6+ unit interest-bearing.** Bank name + address + amount disclosure required. Trust-fund principle: deposit may NOT be commingled with landlord's personal funds. § 7-103(2) imposes INTEREST-BEARING ACCOUNT requirement at prevailing rate for buildings with SIX OR MORE family dwelling units; landlord may retain 1% per annum as administration expense; remaining interest belongs to tenant. § 7-108 added 2019 HSTPA disclosure for 6+ unit buildings. Pinned by `ny_baseline_compliant`, `ny_missing_bank_name_violation`, `ny_5_units_no_interest_bearing_requirement`, `ny_6_units_interest_bearing_required`, `ny_6_units_no_interest_bearing_account_violation`, `ny_100_units_interest_bearing_required`, and `ny_6_unit_threshold_boundary_truth_table` (4-cell sweep at units 5/6/7/100).

**New Jersey § 46:8-19 — 30-day five-component disclosure + re-notification.** Five required components within 30 days of receipt: (1) bank name; (2) bank address; (3) account type; (4) current interest rate; (5) amount. Re-notification within 30 days of (a) transfer of deposit to new landlord OR (b) move to different account/bank. Annual interest payable to tenant in cash, credited to rent on lease anniversary, OR on January 31 if landlord has provided written election notice. Pinned by `nj_baseline_compliant`, `nj_missing_account_type_violation`, `nj_missing_interest_rate_violation`, `nj_30_day_window_expired_violation`, `nj_transfer_event_30_day_expired_violation`, and `nj_annual_interest_not_paid_violation`.

**Massachusetts § 15B(3)(a) + § 15B(6) — harshest non-compliance remedy in the U.S.** 30-day receipt window. Receipt must include bank name + location + amount + account number. Annual statement required at end of each tenancy year. § 15B(6) IMMEDIATE-RETURN REMEDY: failure to comply with ANY § 15B(3) requirement entitles tenant to immediate return of the security deposit — Massachusetts imposes the strictest compliance standard in U.S. landlord-tenant law; minor procedural defects trigger full forfeiture. Pinned by `ma_baseline_compliant`, `ma_missing_account_number_engages_immediate_return`, `ma_receipt_overdue_engages_immediate_return`, `ma_30_day_boundary_exactly_compliant` (day 30 = on time; "within 30 days" reads as ≤), and `ma_missing_annual_statement_violation`.

**Default — no statutory disclosure requirement.** Most states (California Civ. Code § 1950.5, Texas Prop. Code § 92.103 + § 92.108, Florida Stat. § 83.49) regulate deposit AMOUNT + RETURN procedures but impose no LOCATION-DISCLOSURE obligation. Lease provision may still require disclosure as matter of contract. Pinned by `default_no_disclosure_required` and `default_no_violation_even_with_all_disclosure_missing` (all 6 disclosure fields off → still compliant).

**Multi-regime invariants** — `only_ma_engages_immediate_return_invariant` (4-regime sweep — only MA triggers immediate-return remedy); `only_ny_has_unit_threshold_invariant` (4-regime sweep — only NY engages 6+ unit interest-bearing); `only_nj_engages_30_day_window_invariant` (4-regime sweep — only NJ flags 30-day window in result struct); `ny_admin_retention_constant` (1% admin retention; 6-unit threshold; 30-day NJ/MA windows).

**Sibling distinction note** — every result clarifies that this module covers LOCATION DISCLOSURE specifically, referencing the four sibling deposit modules. Pinned by `sibling_distinction_note_present` (UX-text regression).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/security-deposit-bank-disclosure`. Thirty tests pin: **NY baseline compliant**; **NY missing bank name violation**; **NY 5 units no interest-bearing requirement**; **NY 6 units interest-bearing required**; **NY 6 units no interest-bearing account violation**; **NY 100 units interest-bearing required**; **NJ baseline compliant**; **NJ missing account type violation**; **NJ missing interest rate violation**; **NJ 30-day window expired violation**; **NJ transfer event 30-day expired violation** (§ 46:8-19(b) re-notification); **NJ annual interest not paid violation**; **MA baseline compliant**; **MA missing account number engages immediate return** (§ 15B(6)); **MA receipt overdue engages immediate return**; **MA 30-day boundary exactly compliant** (day 30 = on time); **MA missing annual statement violation**; **Default no disclosure required**; **Default no violation even with all disclosure missing**; **only MA engages immediate return invariant** (4-regime sweep); **only NY has unit threshold invariant** (4-regime sweep); **only NJ engages 30-day window invariant** (4-regime sweep); **NY 6-unit threshold boundary truth table** (4-cell sweep at 5/6/7/100 units); **citation pins all regime authorities** (§ 7-103 + § 7-103(2) + § 7-108 + 46:8-19 + 46:8-19(a)/(b) + § 15B(3)(a) + § 15B(6) + § 1950.5 + § 92.103 + § 92.108); **sibling distinction note present** (UX-text regression for `security_deposit_caps` + `deposit_interest` + `deposit_return_windows` + `damage_deduction_itemization` + LOCATION DISCLOSURE focus); **NY admin retention constant** (1% admin retention; 6-unit threshold; 30-day NJ/MA windows); **defensive negative units no interest-bearing required**; **defensive negative days no premature violation**; **boundary NJ at 30 days compliant**; **boundary NJ at 31 days expired**.

`traderview-expense::landlord_harassment` is the **landlord harassment liability module** — civil-penalty exposure for landlord conduct intended to cause tenant to vacate. Distinct from sibling modules `lockout_penalties` (specific lockout statute), `quiet_enjoyment` (general common-law duty + constructive eviction), and `retaliation_windows` (timing-based retaliation presumptions). Trader-critical for landlord-investors operating in California or NYC where the affirmative-harassment statutes create direct civil-penalty exposure on top of common-law claims.

**Three regimes**:

| Regime | Authority | Penalty | Distinctive feature |
|--------|-----------|---------|---------------------|
| California | Cal. Civ. Code § 1940.2 | Up to **$2,000 per violation** | 5 enumerated prohibited acts; good-faith warning exception under § 1940.2(c) |
| NYC | NYC Admin. Code § 27-2004(a)(48) + § 27-2005(d) + § 27-2115(m) | **$1,000-$10,000 per dwelling unit per violation** | Requires both (i) prohibited act AND (ii) intent or causation to vacate; **per-unit multiplier** |
| Default | Common law | Compensatory damages only | IIED, conversion, quiet enjoyment breach, constructive eviction |

**California § 1940.2 — five prohibited acts** (all requiring purpose to influence tenant to vacate):

| Subsection | Conduct |
|------------|---------|
| § 1940.2(a)(1) | Penal Code § 484 (theft) violations |
| § 1940.2(a)(2) | Penal Code § 518 (extortion) violations |
| § 1940.2(a)(3) | Force, willful threats, or menacing conduct interfering with quiet enjoyment |
| § 1940.2(a)(4) | Significant and intentional Cal. Civ. Code § 1954 entry-rules violation |
| § 1940.2(a)(5) | Threat to disclose tenant's immigration or citizenship status |

§ 1940.2(b) civil penalty: up to $2,000 per violation, recoverable in small claims or civil court by tenant who prevails. § 1940.2(c) good-faith warning exception — oral/written warning about lease violations given in good faith and in the normal course of business is NOT harassment. Pinned by `ca_force_or_threats_engages`, `ca_immigration_threat_engages`, `ca_section_1954_entry_violation_engages`, `ca_theft_or_extortion_engages`, `ca_multiple_violations_multiply_penalty` (5 × $2K = $10K), `ca_good_faith_warning_exception`, `ca_good_faith_warning_does_not_excuse_prohibited_act`, and `ca_engagement_does_not_count_unrelated_nyc_acts` (NYC-specific act categories don't engage § 1940.2).

**NYC Admin. Code § 27-2004(a)(48) + § 27-2005(d) — Tenant Harassment Prevention Act (TYPHA, 2008, amended 2017)**. "Harassment" defined as act or omission by or on behalf of owner that (i) causes or is intended to cause tenant to vacate or waive occupancy rights, AND (ii) includes one or more enumerated acts (force/threats, service interruptions, repeated buyout offers, baseless court proceedings, false statements, etc.). § 27-2115(m) civil penalty: $1,000-$10,000 per dwelling unit per violation. **Per-unit multiplier** means landlords harassing across an entire building face geometric penalty growth — 10 units × 3 violations × $10K max = $300K. Pinned by `nyc_force_with_intent_engages`, `nyc_force_without_intent_no_engagement` (intent element required), `nyc_service_interruption_with_intent_engages`, `nyc_baseless_court_proceedings_engages`, `nyc_per_unit_per_violation_multiplier` (10 units × 3 × $10K = $300K), and `nyc_requires_intent_element_truth_table` (4-cell truth table — only act+intent engages).

**Default — common-law claims only**. No statutory civil penalty. Tenant remedies are case-law-driven (intentional infliction of emotional distress, conversion, breach of implied covenant of quiet enjoyment, constructive eviction). Compensatory damages limited to actual harm; punitive damages require extreme conduct. Pinned by `default_force_or_threats_common_law_claim` ($50K actual damages → $50K compensatory; no civil penalty) and `default_no_act_no_claim`.

**Multi-regime invariants** — `only_ca_uses_2000_per_violation_invariant` (3-regime sweep); `nyc_dwelling_unit_multiplier_only_in_nyc_invariant` (CA + Default don't scale with units); `only_ca_has_good_faith_warning_exception_invariant` (3-regime sweep); `nyc_requires_intent_element_truth_table` (4-cell sweep — act × intent); `penalty_constants_invariant` (CA $2K; NYC $1K-$10K; NYC max = 5× CA penalty).

**Sibling distinction note** — every result references `lockout_penalties`, `quiet_enjoyment`, and `retaliation_windows` and clarifies that this module covers AFFIRMATIVE HARASSMENT statutes (not lockout, not general quiet enjoyment, not timing-based retaliation). Pinned by `sibling_distinction_note_present` (UX-text regression).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/landlord-harassment`. Twenty-six tests pin: **CA no prohibited act no engagement**; **CA force or threats engages**; **CA immigration threat engages**; **CA § 1954 entry violation engages**; **CA theft or extortion engages**; **CA multiple violations multiply penalty** (5 × $2K = $10K); **CA good-faith warning exception**; **CA good-faith warning does not excuse prohibited act**; **CA engagement does not count unrelated NYC acts**; **NYC no act no engagement**; **NYC force with intent engages**; **NYC force without intent no engagement** (intent element required); **NYC service interruption with intent engages**; **NYC baseless court proceedings engages**; **NYC per-unit per-violation multiplier** (10 × 3 × $10K = $300K); **default force or threats common-law claim** ($50K actual damages); **default no act no claim**; **only CA uses $2,000 per violation invariant** (3-regime sweep); **NYC dwelling-unit multiplier only in NYC invariant**; **only CA has good-faith warning exception invariant**; **NYC requires intent element truth table** (4-cell act × intent sweep); **penalty constants invariant** (CA $2K; NYC $1K-$10K; 5× ratio); **citation pins all subsections** (§ 1940.2 + (a)(1)-(5) + (b) + (c) + Penal Code § 484 + § 518 + § 1954 + NYC § 27-2004(a)(48) + § 27-2005(d) + § 27-2115(m) + 2008 + 2017); **sibling distinction note present** (UX-text regression for `lockout_penalties` + `quiet_enjoyment` + `retaliation_windows` + AFFIRMATIVE HARASSMENT + multiplier-effect references); **defensive negative violations clamped**; **defensive zero dwelling units floors at one for NYC**.

`traderview-expense::landlord_annual_rent_statement` is the **mandatory landlord-provided annual rent statement to tenant for tax-credit purposes module** — when must a residential landlord issue an annual rent-paid statement to enable the tenant to claim a state-level renter's tax credit or property tax refund? Distinct from siblings `rent_receipts` (per-payment receipts), `security_deposit_interest_statement` (annual security-deposit interest statement), and `lease_disclosures` (mandated lease content). Trader-landlord operational concern in MN, VT, ME, and other renter-tax-credit states.

**Three regimes**:

| Regime | Form | Deadline | Affidavit fallback |
|--------|------|----------|---------------------|
| Minnesota Minn. Stat. § 290A.19 | Certificate of Rent Paid (CRP) | January 31 each year | Rent Paid Affidavit via MN DOR |
| Vermont Vt. Stat. tit. 32 § 6066 | Form LRC-147 | Annual | — |
| Default | No mandate | — | — |

**Minnesota — Minn. Stat. § 290A.19 (Certificate of Rent Paid)**. Most explicit framework. Landlord MUST issue Certificate of Rent Paid (CRP) form to each tenant by **JANUARY 31** each year. CRP details total rent paid by tenant over previous calendar year and supports tenant's claim for **Property Tax Refund (Renter's Credit)** under § 290A. CRP may be provided as ELECTRONIC OR HARD COPY. **Rent Paid Affidavit fallback** — when landlord fails to provide CRP by January 31, tenant may request a "Rent Paid Affidavit" from the Minnesota Department of Revenue as alternative. Noncompliance triggers state-law penalties. Pinned by `mn_missing_january_31_deadline_violates`, `mn_affidavit_alternative_engaged_when_nonissuance`, `mn_affidavit_invocation_note_describes_audit`.

**Vermont — Vt. Stat. tit. 32 § 6066 (Renter Rebate Program)**. Landlord must provide annual statement on **Form LRC-147** to tenant for renter rebate claim. Form must include landlord name + property address + total rent paid in calendar year. No affidavit fallback (unlike MN).

**Default — no statutory obligation**. Most states have no statewide landlord annual-rent-statement mandate. Renter-tax-credit states (MA Renter Deduction, IN Renter Deduction, MI Homestead Property Tax Credit, WI School Property Tax Credit, IA Property Tax Credit, ME Renters Rebate Program, MD Homeowner's Property Tax Credit) typically have tenants claim based on their own records; landlord must produce records on tenant request but is not required to proactively issue a statement.

**MN affidavit alternative is uniquely MN's** — neither VT nor Default offer this consumer-side recourse when landlord fails. Pinned by `mn_affidavit_alternative_unique_to_mn_invariant` (3-regime sweep).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/landlord-annual-rent-statement`. Twenty-eight tests pin: **MN compliant clean path**; **MN missing January 31 deadline violates** (§ 290A.19); **MN affidavit alternative engaged when nonissuance** (Rent Paid Affidavit via Minnesota Department of Revenue); **MN affidavit invocation note describes audit** (state-law penalties on landlord); **MN missing required information violates** (landlord name + property address + total rent); **MN electronic or hard copy note present**; **MN Property Tax Refund + Renter's Credit note present**; **MN citation pins §§ 290A.19 + 290A**; **VT compliant clean path**; **VT missing deadline violates** (Form LRC-147); **VT missing required information violates**; **VT Renter Rebate note present**; **VT citation pins tit. 32 § 6066**; **VT no affidavit alternative unlike MN**; **default no obligation compliant even without statement**; **default renter-tax-credit cross-references note** (MA Renter Deduction + IN Renter Deduction + ME Renters Rebate + WI School Property Tax Credit + MI Homestead Property Tax Credit + IA Property Tax Credit + MD Homeowner's Property Tax Credit); **default records-on-request obligation note**; **default citation references no statewide mandate**; **MN unique January 31 deadline invariant**; **VT unique Form LRC-147 invariant**; **three regimes routed correctly**; **MN affidavit alternative unique to MN invariant** (3-regime sweep); **MN full compliance no affidavit alternative**; **MN both violations simultaneous when only deadline missed** (single violation due to early-return on first failure); **state penalty exposure engaged with any violation**; **state penalty exposure not engaged when compliant**; **default no state penalty exposure ever**; **VT no affidavit pathway ever**.

`traderview-expense::landlord_emergency_entry_notice` is the **mandatory landlord-paid post-entry notice after emergency entry module** — when landlord enters tenant's unit without prior notice due to true emergency (fire + water leak + gas leak), what post-entry written notice obligation attaches? Distinct from siblings `entry_notice` (general 24-hour pre-entry notice), `pesticide_application_notice` (treatment-specific), and `landlord_harassment` (unauthorized entry as harassment).

**Four regimes**:

| Regime | Authority | Specific post-entry obligation |
|--------|-----------|-----------------------------------|
| California Cal. Civ. Code §§ 1954(e) + 1940.2 | $2,000/violation for pretext | Written notice on premises + reasonable time |
| Texas Tex. Prop. Code § 92.0081 | Civil penalty $1,000 + 1 month rent | No specific written-notice obligation |
| New York N.Y. Mult. Dwell. Law § 78 + common law | Trespass + conversion | Post-entry notice + PREMISES SECURED |
| Default | Trespass + breach of quiet enjoyment | Reasonable post-entry notice |

**California — Cal. Civ. Code §§ 1954(e) + 1940.2**. Landlord may enter without prior notice "in case of emergency." Post-entry, the landlord MUST (1) leave WRITTEN NOTICE describing the date + time + purpose of entry on the premises AND (2) provide such notice within a REASONABLE TIME. Pretextual emergency entries actionable under § 1940.2 prohibited harassing acts with **$2,000 per-violation** civil penalty. Pinned by `ca_pretext_engages_harassment`, `ca_missing_written_notice_violates`, `ca_missing_reasonable_time_notice_violates`.

**Texas — Tex. Prop. Code § 92.0081**. Emergency entry permitted. Texas does NOT impose specific post-entry written-notice obligation but landlord remains liable for unauthorized entry: **actual damages + one month's rent + $1,000 + reasonable attorney fees**. Pinned by `tx_pretext_engages_civil_penalty`, `tx_no_specific_post_entry_notice_obligation_note`.

**New York — N.Y. Mult. Dwell. Law § 78 + common-law quiet enjoyment**. Emergency entry permitted but landlord must (1) provide post-entry notification to tenant AND (2) leave premises **SECURED**. Failure to secure exposes landlord to trespass + conversion claims. Pinned by `ny_missing_secured_premises_violates`, `ny_missing_post_entry_notice_violates`, `ny_pretext_engages_breach_of_quiet_enjoyment`, `ny_unique_premises_secured_requirement_invariant`.

**Default — common-law necessity doctrine + quiet enjoyment**. Most states permit emergency entry under common-law necessity doctrine. Most impose reasonable post-entry notice obligation under quiet enjoyment covenant. Failure constitutes actionable trespass + breach of quiet enjoyment + common-law harassment.

**CA uniquely requires specific written-notice content** (date + time + purpose). Pinned by `ca_uniquely_requires_specific_written_notice_invariant` (2-regime sweep).

**NY uniquely requires premises secured after entry**. Pinned by `ny_unique_premises_secured_requirement_invariant` (2-regime sweep).

**Pretext engages across all regimes** — actionable as harassment / trespass / breach of quiet enjoyment regardless of regime. Pinned by `pretext_engages_across_all_regimes` (4-regime sweep).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/landlord-emergency-entry-notice`. Twenty-five tests pin: **CA compliant passes**; **CA pretext engages harassment** ($2,000 per-violation); **CA missing written notice violates** (§ 1954(e) date + time + purpose); **CA missing reasonable time notice violates**; **CA no emergency entry no violation**; **CA citation pins §§ 1954(e) + 1940.2**; **TX compliant passes**; **TX pretext engages civil penalty** ($1,000 + month rent + attorney fees); **TX no specific post-entry notice obligation note**; **TX citation pins § 92.0081**; **NY compliant passes**; **NY missing secured premises violates** (§ 78); **NY missing post-entry notice violates**; **NY pretext engages breach of quiet enjoyment**; **NY citation pins Mult. Dwell. Law § 78**; **default compliant when actual emergency with notice**; **default pretext violates common law**; **default missing post-entry notice violates** (common-law quiet enjoyment); **NY unique premises secured requirement invariant** (2-regime sweep); **CA uniquely requires specific written notice invariant**; **pretext engages across all regimes** (4-regime sweep); **emergency exception engaged when actual emergency**; **four regimes routed correctly**; **CA clean no violations**; **NY multiple violations simultaneous** (3 violations).

`traderview-expense::landlord_mid_tenancy_rekeying` is the **mandatory landlord-paid mid-tenancy rekeying obligations module** — when tenant requests landlord to rekey locks during ongoing tenancy (lost keys, roommate departed, burglary, security upgrade) what notice, timing, and cost-allocation requirements attach? Trader-landlord operational concern: failure to comply with statutory rekeying timeframe exposes landlord to actual damages + punitive + civil penalty + one month's rent + court costs + attorney fees (TX framework). Distinct from siblings `lock_change_between_tenancies` (between-tenancy locks), `dv_survivor_lock_change` (DV-survivor locks), and `tenant_smart_lock_biometric_consent` (biometric locks).

**Three regimes** — TX maximal statutory framework versus CA limited common-law versus default reasonable-time:

| Regime | Authority | Required window | Master-key change | Interior doors | Remedies |
|--------|-----------|-----------------|-------------------|----------------|----------|
| Texas | Tex. Prop. Code §§ 92.156-92.158 + 92.164 + 92.165 | **7 days = 168 hours** after tenant request | **Landlord pays** | **EXCLUDED** | Actual + punitive + $500 + one month's rent + court costs + attorney fees |
| California | Cal. Civ. Code §§ 1954 + 1941.3 | Common-law reasonable time (~10 days = 240 hours) | Landlord pays | Not statutorily excluded | Common-law damages + § 1941.3(b) maintenance obligation |
| Default | Common-law quiet enjoyment + lease | Reasonable time | Landlord pays | Not specifically addressed | Common-law damages |

**TX uniquely explicit 7-day window** — boundary tests pin: 168 hours = compliant; 169 hours + not completed = violation. Pinned by `tx_at_168_boundary_compliant`, `tx_169_hours_violates`, and `tx_uniquely_explicit_7_day_window_invariant` (3-regime sweep: TX = 168, CA = 240, Default = 0).

**TX § 92.156 interior-door exclusion** — closet doors and other interior doors fall outside the statutory framework entirely; landlord owes no statutory rekeying duty regardless of tenant request or timing. Pinned by `tx_interior_door_excluded`.

**Cost allocation truth table** — landlord pays for master-key changes and security upgrades; tenant pays for everything else (lost keys, roommate departures, tenant-driven security concerns, burglary under TX/Default). CA additionally shifts burglary-driven rekeying to landlord under § 1941.3(b). Pinned by `rekey_reason_cost_allocation_truth_table` (6-reason sweep), `tx_landlord_pays_for_master_key_change`, `tx_landlord_pays_for_security_upgrade`, `tx_tenant_pays_for_key_lost`, `ca_burglary_landlord_pays`, `default_tenant_pays_for_key_lost`, and `default_landlord_pays_for_security_upgrade`.

**TX unique remedies stack** — actual damages + punitive damages + $500 civil penalty + ONE MONTH's rent + court costs + attorney fees is uniquely cumulative; CA + Default rely on common-law damages without statutory multipliers. Pinned by `tx_remedies_note_describes_one_month_rent_and_500` and `tx_unique_remedies_invariant` (TX engages full remedies stack; CA does NOT).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/landlord-mid-tenancy-rekeying`. Twenty-six tests pin: **TX compliant passes**; **TX tenant pays for key lost**; **TX landlord pays for master key change**; **TX landlord pays for security upgrade**; **TX past 7-day window violates**; **TX at 168 boundary compliant**; **TX 169 hours violates**; **TX no new keys violates**; **TX interior door excluded**; **TX remedies note describes one month rent and $500**; **TX citation pins Subchapter D sections**; **CA compliant within reasonable time**; **CA burglary landlord pays**; **CA past reasonable time violates**; **CA citation pins subsections**; **default compliant always**; **default tenant pays for key lost**; **default landlord pays for security upgrade**; **three regimes routed correctly**; **TX unique remedies invariant**; **rekey reason cost allocation truth table** (6-reason sweep); **no tenant request no engagement**; **TX clean no violations**; **CA clean no violations**; **default clean no violations**; **TX uniquely explicit 7-day window invariant** (3-regime sweep).

`traderview-expense::landlord_possession_delivery` is the **landlord-possession-delivery module** — when must a landlord deliver ACTUAL possession (not just legal right) of the leased premises to the tenant at lease commencement, and what are the tenant's remedies if a prior tenant is holding over or squatters remain? Trader-critical for landlord-investors whose new acquisitions come with unanticipated occupants. Distinct from sibling modules `holdover_tenant_damages` (prior tenant's liability for remaining past lease end), `quiet_enjoyment` (post-delivery covenant against landlord interference), `habitability_remedies` (post-delivery uninhabitable-condition remedies), and `eviction_notices` (procedures landlord must follow to evict).

**Three regimes** — the structural English-vs-American divergence with URLTA codification:

| Regime | Authority | Duty | Tenant remedies | Lease cancellation |
|--------|-----------|------|-----------------|---------------------|
| URLTA states | URLTA § 2.103 + § 4.102 | **Yes — actual possession** | **Greater of 3 months' rent OR 3× actual damages** + attorney's fees + injunctive relief | Yes |
| English Rule | Restatement (2d) of Property § 6.2 + modern majority | **Yes — actual possession** | Actual damages + rent abatement for delay period (common law) | Yes |
| American Rule | Hannan v. Dusch, 153 S.E. 824 (Va. 1930) | **No — only legal right** | Tenant must evict holdover party + recover from THAT party | No (lease remains in force) |

**URLTA § 4.102 statutory remedies — greater of 3 months' rent OR threefold actual damages**. The "whichever is greater" clause means the 3-months floor protects tenants with low actual out-of-pocket damages; the triple-actual multiplier scales penalty with actual harm when damages are large. Pinned by `urlta_3_months_rent_greater_than_triple_damages` ($1K actual × 3 = $3K vs 3 mo × $2K = $6K → $6K wins), `urlta_triple_damages_greater_than_3_months` ($5K × 3 = $15K vs $6K → $15K wins), `urlta_no_actual_damages_3_months_floor` ($0 actual → $6K floor), `urlta_no_breach_no_damages`, and `urlta_dollar_for_dollar_test_with_3_months_rent_versus_actual_damages_truth_table` (5-cell sweep).

**English Rule actual damages + rent abatement**. Common-law rule provides actual damages with rent abatement for the delay period. Module uses `monthly_rent × days / 30` (multiply-before-divide) to preserve precision. 30 days delay at $2K/month = $2,000 exact abatement. Pinned by `english_rule_failure_actual_damages_plus_abatement` ($5K actual + $2K abatement = $7K), `english_rule_no_actual_damages_abatement_only` ($2K abatement only), `english_rule_no_breach_no_damages`, and `english_rule_long_delay_large_abatement` (365 days × $2K / 30 = $2,433,333 abatement).

**American Rule shifts burden to tenant**. Landlord delivers only LEGAL possession. New tenant has standing to evict holdover prior tenant or squatter directly and recover damages from that party — NOT the landlord. Lease remains in force; rent continues to accrue. Tenant cannot cancel. Pinned by `american_rule_no_landlord_duty`, `american_rule_no_statutory_remedies`, and `american_rule_holdover_party_remedy_note` (note must reference burden-shifting to tenant for eviction proceeding).

**Multi-regime invariants** — `only_urlta_has_statutory_remedies_invariant` (3-regime sweep — only URLTA states get § 4.102 statutory remedies + attorney-fee shifting); `only_american_rule_shifts_burden_to_tenant_invariant` (only American rule sets `tenant_must_evict_prior_tenant_party = true`); `american_rule_only_regime_without_duty_invariant` (URLTA + English impose duty; American does not); `urlta_constants_invariant` (3-multiplier, 3-months floor).

**Damages calculation method enum** — `DamagesCalculationMethod::UrltaTripleActualOrThreeMonths` for URLTA; `EnglishRuleActualWithAbatement` for English rule; `NoLandlordDamages` for American rule. Pinned by 3 dedicated enum-coverage tests.

**Sibling distinction note** — every result references related modules `holdover_tenant_damages`, `quiet_enjoyment`, `habitability_remedies`, and `eviction_notices`, and clarifies that this module covers LEASE-COMMENCEMENT DELIVERY DUTY specifically. Pinned by `sibling_distinction_note_present` (UX-text regression).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/landlord-possession-delivery`. Twenty-five tests pin: **URLTA landlord failure triggers statutory remedies**; **URLTA 3 months rent greater than triple damages**; **URLTA triple damages greater than 3 months**; **URLTA no actual damages 3 months floor**; **URLTA no breach no damages**; **English rule failure actual damages plus abatement** ($5K actual + $2K abatement = $7K); **English rule no actual damages abatement only** ($2K); **English rule no breach no damages**; **English rule long delay large abatement** (365 days × $2K / 30 = $2,433,333); **American rule no landlord duty**; **American rule no statutory remedies**; **American rule holdover party remedy note**; **URLTA uses URLTA triple actual or three months enum**; **English rule uses actual with abatement enum**; **American rule uses no landlord damages enum**; **only URLTA has statutory remedies invariant** (3-regime sweep); **only American rule shifts burden to tenant invariant** (3-regime sweep); **American rule only regime without duty invariant** (3-regime sweep); **URLTA dollar-for-dollar test truth table** (5-cell sweep over rent × damages combinations); **URLTA constants invariant** (3× multiplier, 3 months floor); **citation pins all authorities** (Restatement § 6.2 + URLTA § 2.103 + § 4.102 + Hannan v. Dusch); **sibling distinction note present** (UX-text regression for `holdover_tenant_damages` + `quiet_enjoyment` + `habitability_remedies` + `eviction_notices` + LEASE-COMMENCEMENT DELIVERY DUTY); **defensive negative rent clamped**; **defensive negative damages clamped**; **defensive negative days no abatement**.

`traderview-expense::lease_waiver_enforceability` is the **lease waiver clause enforceability module** — when may a residential or commercial lease validly waive tenant rights or landlord liability via contract language? Distinct from sibling modules `landlord_harassment` (affirmative-harassment penalties), `habitability_remedies` (post-delivery habitability obligations), `quiet_enjoyment` (general post-delivery covenant), and `plain_language_lease` (statutory clarity requirement). This module focuses on the LEASE-DRAFTED WAIVER PROVISION itself — when the contract clause attempting to extinguish a right is void as a matter of statute or public policy.

**Three regimes**:

| Regime | Authority | Scope | Lease type |
|--------|-----------|-------|------------|
| New York | N.Y. Gen. Oblig. Law § 5-321 | **Narrow** — only landlord-negligence exculpatory clauses void | Both residential AND commercial |
| California | Cal. Civ. Code § 1953 | **Broad** — voids 6 categories of tenant rights waivers | Residential only |
| Default | Common law + Restatement (Second) of Contracts § 178 | Knowing + voluntary + public policy test | Both |

**New York § 5-321 — narrow scope, broad application.** Voids any clause "exempting the lessor from liability for damages for injuries to person or property caused by or resulting from the negligence of the lessor." Applies to BOTH residential and commercial leases. INDEMNIFICATION clauses remain enforceable (Great N. Ins. Co. v. Interior Constr. Corp., 7 N.Y.3d 412 (2006)) — landlord may still seek indemnity from tenant for liability paid to injured third parties; only the clause exempting LANDLORD from liability to tenant for landlord's own negligence is void. Pinned by `ny_landlord_negligence_exculpatory_void`, `ny_negligence_exculpatory_applies_to_commercial`, `ny_jury_trial_waiver_outside_5_321_scope` (jury waiver enforceable in NY with knowing consent), `ny_jury_trial_waiver_without_consent_unenforceable`, and `ny_indemnity_distinction_note_when_5_321_engages` (UX-text regression for indemnity-vs-exculpation distinction).

**California § 1953 — broadest residential protection in the U.S.** Six categories of waivers automatically void in residential leases:

| Subsection | Voided waiver category |
|------------|-------------------------|
| § 1953(a)(1) | § 1950.5 (security deposit) or § 1954 (landlord entry) rights |
| § 1953(a)(2) | Right to assert cause of action against lessor |
| § 1953(a)(3) | Right to notice or hearing required by law |
| § 1953(a)(4) | Procedural rights in litigation (including jury trial waiver) |
| § 1953(a)(5) | Right to have lessor exercise duty of care to prevent personal injury or property damage |
| § 1953(a)(6) | Cumulative remedies under the lease |

§ 1953 applies ONLY to residential leases. Commercial waivers in California fall under common-law analysis. Pinned by `ca_residential_jury_trial_waiver_void`, `ca_residential_deposit_rights_waiver_void`, `ca_residential_future_cause_of_action_void`, `ca_residential_notice_hearing_void`, `ca_residential_habitability_void`, `ca_residential_cumulative_remedies_void`, `ca_commercial_lease_outside_1953_scope` (commercial waiver enforceable with consent), `ca_1953_applies_only_to_residential_invariant` (residential vs commercial head-to-head), and `ca_residential_all_seven_waiver_types_void` (7-prong sweep — all enum types void in CA residential).

**Default common-law analysis — knowing/voluntary + public policy**. Waiver enforceable if BOTH (a) knowing and voluntary consent (no adhesion-contract procedural unconscionability) AND (b) waiver does not violate public policy. Public-policy-protected categories (habitability, duty of care, landlord negligence exculpation) are void REGARDLESS of consent. Pinned by `default_knowing_voluntary_enforceable`, `default_not_knowing_voluntary_unenforceable`, `default_habitability_waiver_void_regardless_of_consent`, `default_negligence_exculpatory_void_regardless_of_consent`, and `default_public_policy_voids_regardless_of_consent_truth_table` (4-cell sweep — public-policy-protected category voids regardless of consent; non-protected category requires knowing consent).

**Multi-regime invariants** — `only_california_1953_engages_residential_only_protection` (3-regime sweep); `ny_5_321_applies_to_both_residential_and_commercial_invariant` (both residential and commercial coverage); `ca_1953_applies_only_to_residential_invariant` (residential vs commercial divergence). `ca_residential_all_seven_waiver_types_void` confirms all enum waiver types are void in CA residential.

**No-waiver-clause short circuit** — when `waiver_in_lease_agreement` is false, no analysis is performed; result returned immediately as compliant with explanatory note. Pinned by `no_waiver_clause_no_analysis`.

**Sibling distinction note** — every result references `landlord_harassment` (affirmative-harassment penalties), `habitability_remedies` (post-delivery habitability obligations), `quiet_enjoyment` (general post-delivery covenant), and `plain_language_lease` (statutory clarity requirement), and clarifies that this module covers LEASE-DRAFTED WAIVER PROVISIONS specifically. Pinned by `sibling_distinction_note_present` (UX-text regression).

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/lease-waiver-enforceability`. Twenty-four tests pin: **no waiver clause no analysis**; **NY landlord-negligence exculpatory void**; **NY negligence exculpatory applies to commercial**; **NY jury trial waiver outside § 5-321 scope**; **NY jury trial waiver without consent unenforceable**; **CA residential jury trial waiver void** (§ 1953(a)(4)); **CA residential deposit rights waiver void** (§ 1953(a)(1)); **CA residential future cause of action void** (§ 1953(a)(2)); **CA residential notice/hearing void** (§ 1953(a)(3)); **CA residential habitability void** (§ 1953(a)(5)); **CA residential cumulative remedies void** (§ 1953(a)(6)); **CA commercial lease outside § 1953 scope** (residential-only protection); **default knowing voluntary enforceable**; **default not knowing voluntary unenforceable**; **default habitability waiver void regardless of consent**; **default negligence exculpatory void regardless of consent**; **only California § 1953 engages residential-only protection** (3-regime sweep); **NY § 5-321 applies to both residential and commercial invariant**; **CA § 1953 applies only to residential invariant**; **CA residential all seven waiver types void**; **default public policy voids regardless of consent truth table** (4-cell sweep); **citation pins all subsections** (§ 5-321 + § 1953 + (a)(1)-(6) + § 1950.5 + § 1954 + Restatement § 178 + Great N. Ins. Co. 7 N.Y.3d 412); **sibling distinction note present** (UX-text regression for `landlord_harassment` + `habitability_remedies` + `quiet_enjoyment` + `plain_language_lease` + LEASE-DRAFTED WAIVER PROVISIONS); **NY indemnity distinction note when § 5-321 engages** (UX-text regression — indemnification clauses remain enforceable per Great N. Ins. Co.).

`traderview-expense::residential_lease_arbitration_clause` is the **pre-dispute arbitration clause enforceability in residential leases** module — when can a landlord include a binding arbitration provision in a residential lease that requires the tenant to arbitrate disputes (habitability, eviction, deposit, fee challenges) rather than file court action? Distinct from `lease_waiver_enforceability` (general lease-drafted waiver framework) and `prevailing_party_attorney_fees` (fee-shifting). This module focuses ONLY on the ARBITRATION / CLASS-ACTION WAIVER question.

**Three regimes**:

| Regime | Authority | Default rule |
|--------|-----------|--------------|
| **California** | Cal. Civ. Code § 1953(a)(4); Brooks v. Greystar Real Estate Partners (S.D. Cal. 2024) | Traditionally voids procedural-rights waivers; **eroded by Brooks** under FAA preemption; CONTESTED status for habitability / unlawful detainer / deposit disputes |
| **New Jersey** | N.J.S.A. 2A:23B-1 + Atalese v. U.S. Legal Services Group (220 N.J. 220, 2014) | Requires EXPLICIT judicial-forum-waiver language in clear, unambiguous terms; boilerplate clauses fail Atalese |
| **Default** | 9 U.S.C. §§ 1/2/4 Federal Arbitration Act + AT&T Mobility v. Concepcion (563 U.S. 333, 2011) + Epic Systems v. Lewis (584 U.S. 497, 2018) | FAA enforces; § 2 savings clause permits state defenses applied neutrally; class-action waivers enforceable under Epic Systems |

**Universal Speak Out Act of 2022 (117 Stat. 2192) carve-out for sexual harassment / assault.** Pre-dispute arbitration clauses are UNENFORCEABLE for sexual harassment and sexual assault claims regardless of state regime. Pinned by `sexual_harassment_pre_dispute_arbitration_unenforceable_universal` (3-regime sweep confirms all three regimes bar arbitration), `sexual_harassment_disables_class_action_waiver_too`, `note_speak_out_act_references_117_stat_2192`.

**§ 2 FAA savings clause permits unconscionability + duress defenses.** State-law contract defenses (unconscionability, duress, misrepresentation, fraud) apply through FAA § 2 if applied NEUTRALLY (not specifically against arbitration agreements) per AT&T Mobility v. Concepcion (563 U.S. 333, 2011). Pinned by `unconscionability_voids_arbitration_universal`, `duress_or_misrepresentation_voids_arbitration`, `unconscionability_disables_class_action_waiver`, `note_unconscionability_references_concepcion`.

**California § 1953(a)(4) status is CONTESTED post-Brooks v. Greystar (S.D. Cal. 2024).** The federal court ruled that FAA preemption analysis applies to residential lease arbitration clauses, eroding the traditional § 1953(a)(4) void rule. Module marks all CA habitability / unlawful detainer / deposit disputes as "CONTESTED status — FAA savings clause analysis required" rather than absolute void. Pinned by `ca_habitability_now_contested_under_brooks`, `ca_traditional_void_pathway_includes_brooks_caveat`, `ca_traditional_void_categories_three_specific_disputes_only` (3-dispute sweep confirms only habitability/UD/deposit fall in traditional void category — Other disputes arbitrable).

**NJ Atalese v. U.S. Legal Services Group (220 N.J. 220, 2014) requires EXPLICIT judicial-forum-waiver language.** Boilerplate arbitration clauses fail because they lack clear, unambiguous waiver of the right to a judicial forum. Pinned by `nj_explicit_judicial_waiver_satisfies_atalese`, `nj_missing_explicit_waiver_violates_atalese`, `nj_pre_atalese_boilerplate_clause_unenforceable`, `nj_unique_atalese_requirement_invariant` (CA/Default do not require Atalese explicit waiver).

**Class-action waivers enforceable under Epic Systems v. Lewis (584 U.S. 497, 2018) when underlying arbitration is enforceable.** When the underlying arbitration clause fails (no clause, sexual harassment exception, unconscionability, duress, Atalese failure), the class-action waiver also fails. Pinned by `class_action_waiver_enforceable_under_epic_systems`, `class_action_waiver_voids_when_underlying_arbitration_voided`, `no_class_action_waiver_no_enforcement_note`, `no_clause_in_lease_disables_class_action_waiver`.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/residential-lease-arbitration-clause`. Twenty-eight tests pin: **no arbitration clause no enforcement**; **sexual harassment pre-dispute arbitration unenforceable universal** (3-regime Speak Out Act sweep); **unconscionability voids arbitration universal** (Concepcion savings clause); **duress or misrepresentation voids arbitration**; **CA habitability now contested under Brooks** (S.D. Cal. 2024); **CA unlawful detainer traditional § 1953 void**; **CA security deposit traditional § 1953 void**; **CA other dispute arbitrable under FAA**; **NJ explicit judicial waiver satisfies Atalese**; **NJ missing explicit waiver violates Atalese** (220 N.J. 220, 2014); **default FAA general enforceability**; **class-action waiver enforceable under Epic Systems** (584 U.S. 497, 2018); **no class-action waiver no enforcement note**; **citation CA pins § 1953(a)(4) + Brooks + Concepcion + Epic + Speak Out Act**; **citation NJ pins Atalese + N.J.S.A. 2A:23B-1 + 220 N.J. 220 (2014)**; **citation Default pins FAA 9 U.S.C. §§ 1/2/4 + Concepcion + Epic + Speak Out Act**; **sexual harassment disables class-action waiver too**; **unconscionability disables class-action waiver**; **no clause in lease disables class-action waiver**; **CA habitability arbitrable post-Brooks but marked contested**; **NJ unique Atalese requirement invariant** (CA/Default do not require explicit waiver); **5 dispute types routed correctly** (Habitability/UnlawfulDetainer/SecurityDeposit/SexualHarassment/Other); **CA traditional void categories 3 specific disputes only**; **note Speak Out Act references 117 Stat. 2192**; **note unconscionability references Concepcion**; **CA traditional void pathway includes Brooks caveat**; **NJ pre-Atalese boilerplate clause unenforceable**; **class-action waiver voids when underlying arbitration voided** (cascade rule).

`traderview-expense::rental_bedroom_egress_window` is the **rental property bedroom egress window requirement compliance module** — when must a trader-landlord ensure every bedroom in a residential rental unit has an emergency escape and rescue opening (EERO) meeting International Residential Code (IRC) § R310 minimum standards? Trader-landlord critical for any rental owner: failure to provide compliant egress window breaches implied warranty of habitability + creates fire-safety liability exposure + violates state building code adoption (CA + NY + 49 other state IRC adoptions). Distinct from siblings `detector_requirements` (smoke + CO detector hardware), `fire_sprinkler_disclosure` (suppression system), `tenant_fire_safety_plan_disclosure` (HPD signage), and `window_guard_requirements` (NYC HMC § 27-2046.1 child-safety).

**Two regimes** — IRC R310 (49 state-adopting jurisdictions) vs Default:

| Regime | Authority | Net opening | Height | Width | Sill height |
|--------|-----------|-------------|--------|-------|--------------|
| IRC R310 | IRC § R310.2.1 + § R310.2.2 + Cal. Residential Code § R310 + 2020 NY State Residential Code § R310 | **≥ 5.7 sq ft** (5.0 sq ft grade exception) | **≥ 24 in** | **≥ 20 in** | **≤ 44 in** above finished floor |
| Default | Local building code + common-law habitability | N/A statewide | N/A | N/A | N/A |

**IRC § R310.2.1 four-element simultaneous requirement** — every sleeping room must have at least one EERO meeting ALL FOUR dimensions simultaneously. Net clear opening of 5.7 sq ft (821 sq in) standard; 5.0 sq ft (720 sq in) grade-floor exception when sill ≤ 44 inches above or below adjacent ground. Pinned by `ircr310_compliant_passes`, `ircr310_opening_at_5_7_sqft_boundary_compliant` (exactly 821 sq in = compliant), `ircr310_opening_below_5_7_sqft_violates` (820 fails), `ircr310_grade_floor_5_0_sqft_compliant` (720 = compliant at grade), `ircr310_grade_floor_below_5_0_sqft_violates`, `ircr310_height_at_24_in_boundary_compliant`, `ircr310_height_below_24_in_violates`, `ircr310_width_at_20_in_boundary_compliant`, `ircr310_width_below_20_in_violates`, `ircr310_sill_at_44_in_boundary_compliant`, `ircr310_sill_above_44_in_violates`, `ircr310_all_four_violations_stack` (4 violations), `ircr310_grade_floor_uniquely_lower_threshold_invariant` (5.0 grade vs 5.7 standard), and `ircr310_note_pins_four_dimensions`.

**IRC § R310.2.3 window well requirements** (if EERO below adjacent ground level):
- Minimum horizontal area = **9 sq ft** (1,296 sq in)
- Minimum horizontal projection = **36 inches** from exterior face of wall
- Permanently affixed ladder/steps required if well depth **> 44 inches**

Pinned by `ircr310_below_grade_well_required`, `ircr310_below_grade_well_below_9_sqft_violates` (1,200 sq in violates 1,296 minimum), `ircr310_well_projection_below_36_in_violates` (35 in violates), `ircr310_well_depth_over_44_in_requires_ladder` (48 in depth without ladder violates), `ircr310_well_depth_over_44_in_with_ladder_compliant`, `ircr310_well_depth_at_44_in_boundary_no_ladder_required` (exactly 44 in = no ladder), `ircr310_well_required_only_below_grade_invariant`, `ircr310_ladder_required_only_when_well_depth_over_44_invariant` (44 in = no ladder; 45 in = ladder required), and `ircr310_note_pins_window_well_requirements`.

**Default fallback** — no statewide IRC adoption; local building code or common-law warranty of habitability governs. Most U.S. jurisdictions adopt IRC; some legacy areas use older codes. Pinned by `default_no_violations`, `default_citation_pins_local_code`, and `two_regimes_routed_correctly`.

**Habitability breach engages with any violation** — pinned by `ircr310_habitability_breach_engages_with_any_violation`.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-bedroom-egress-window`. Twenty-nine tests pin: **IRC R310 compliant passes**; **IRC R310 opening at 5.7 sq ft boundary compliant** (821 sq in); **IRC R310 opening below 5.7 sq ft violates** (820 fails); **IRC R310 grade floor 5.0 sq ft compliant** (720 sq in); **IRC R310 grade floor below 5.0 sq ft violates**; **IRC R310 height at 24 in boundary compliant**; **IRC R310 height below 24 in violates**; **IRC R310 width at 20 in boundary compliant**; **IRC R310 width below 20 in violates**; **IRC R310 sill at 44 in boundary compliant**; **IRC R310 sill above 44 in violates** (§ R310.2.2); **IRC R310 below grade well required**; **IRC R310 below grade well below 9 sq ft violates** (§ R310.2.3); **IRC R310 well projection below 36 in violates**; **IRC R310 well depth over 44 in requires ladder**; **IRC R310 well depth over 44 in with ladder compliant**; **IRC R310 well depth at 44 in boundary no ladder required**; **IRC R310 all four violations stack**; **IRC R310 citation pins authorities** (§ R310 + § R310.2.1 + § R310.2.2 + § R310.2.3 + Cal. Residential Code + 2020 NY State Residential Code); **IRC R310 note pins four dimensions**; **IRC R310 note pins window well requirements**; **IRC R310 note pins state adoption** (ALL FOUR); **default no violations**; **default citation pins local code**; **two regimes routed correctly**; **IRC R310 grade floor uniquely lower threshold invariant** (5.0 grade vs 5.7 standard); **IRC R310 well required only below grade invariant**; **IRC R310 ladder required only when well depth over 44 invariant**; **IRC R310 habitability breach engages with any violation**.

`traderview-expense::rental_application_denial_disclosure` is the **rental application denial reason written disclosure compliance module** — when a trader-landlord rejects a tenant applicant, what statutory written-disclosure obligations attach? Trader-landlord operational concern: incomplete or untimely denial-reason disclosure exposes landlord to statutory damages + applicant claim for discriminatory denial + reputational risk. Distinct from siblings `adverse_action_notice` (FCRA-only consumer-report denial notice), `fair_chance_housing` (criminal-background screening framework), `application_fees` (caps on application fee itself), and `credit_check_authorization` (pre-screening consent).

**Four regimes** — three jurisdictions with state-statutory protection vs federal FCRA baseline:

| Regime | Authority | Pre-fee disclosure | Individualized assessment | Federal FCRA only |
|--------|-----------|---------------------|----------------------------|---------------------|
| California | Cal. Civ. Code §§ 1950.6 + 1786.40 (ICRAA) | No | No | Statutory protection engaged |
| New Jersey | N.J.S.A. 46:8-52 et seq. (NJ Fair Chance in Housing Act) | **YES** (criminal history) | **YES** | Statutory protection engaged |
| New York City | NYC Local Law 24 of 2023 (Fair Chance for Housing Law eff. Jan 1, 2025) + NYC FARE Act (eff. June 11, 2025) | No | **YES** | Statutory protection engaged |
| Default | 15 USC § 1681m (FCRA § 615(a)) | No | No | Federal baseline only |

**California § 1950.6 + § 1786.40 ICRAA** — written notice required when credit score or history was reason for denial; specific written reason for denial required. Pinned by `ca_credit_denial_with_notice_compliant`, `ca_credit_denial_without_written_notice_violates`, `ca_no_specific_reason_violates`, `ca_citation_pins_authorities` (§§ 1950.6 + 1786.40 ICRAA), and `ca_note_pins_icraa` (Investigative Consumer Reporting Agencies Act).

**New Jersey Fair Chance in Housing Act (N.J.S.A. 46:8-52 et seq.)** — uniquely requires PRE-FEE written disclosure of criminal-history consideration before accepting application fee + written statement allowing applicants to provide rehabilitation evidence + individualized assessment for criminal-background-based denials. Pinned by `nj_criminal_denial_compliant`, `nj_no_pre_fee_disclosure_violates`, `nj_no_rehabilitation_right_disclosed_violates`, `nj_no_individualized_assessment_violates`, `nj_non_criminal_basis_no_pre_fee_required` (non-criminal denials don't trigger pre-fee requirement), `nj_citation_pins_fcha`, `nj_note_pins_before_accepting_fee`, `nj_uniquely_requires_pre_fee_disclosure_invariant` (4-regime sweep: only NJ requires pre-fee), and `denial_basis_truth_table_nj` (5-cell sweep: only CriminalBackground triggers pre-fee).

**NYC Local Law 24 of 2023 + FARE Act** — conditional offer + lookback + individualized assessment framework for criminal-history denials; written notice with specific reason and appeal rights required (effective January 1, 2025). NYC FARE Act prohibits broker fees from being charged to tenants (effective June 11, 2025); landlords/agents must disclose other fees in listings and rental agreements. Pinned by `nyc_criminal_denial_compliant`, `nyc_no_individualized_assessment_violates`, `nyc_no_rehabilitation_evidence_violates`, `nyc_no_written_notice_violates`, `nyc_citation_pins_local_law_24_and_fare_act`, and `nyc_note_pins_january_1_2025_and_june_11_2025`.

**Default federal FCRA baseline (15 USC § 1681m)** — adverse action notice required when consumer report was basis for denial; must include CRA contact info + dispute right + free-copy right + statement that landlord made decision. Less stringent than CA / NJ / NYC. Pinned by `default_credit_denial_with_cra_notice_compliant`, `default_no_cra_contact_violates_fcra`, `default_citation_pins_fcra_baseline`, `default_note_pins_fcra_less_stringent`, and `statutory_protection_engaged_for_ca_nj_nyc_only_invariant` (CA + NJ + NYC engage statutory protection; Default does not).

**NJ + NYC uniquely require individualized assessment invariant** — both NJ FCHA and NYC FCHA require individualized assessment for criminal-history-based denials (blanket criminal-record bar unlawful in both). CA + Default do NOT impose this requirement. Pinned by `nj_and_nyc_uniquely_require_individualized_assessment_invariant`.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-application-denial-disclosure`. Twenty-seven tests pin: **CA credit denial with notice compliant**; **CA credit denial without written notice violates** (§ 1950.6); **CA no specific reason violates** (§§ 1950.6 + 1786.40 ICRAA); **CA citation pins authorities**; **NJ criminal denial compliant**; **NJ no pre-fee disclosure violates** (46:8-52 BEFORE); **NJ no rehabilitation right disclosed violates**; **NJ no individualized assessment violates** (blanket bar prohibition); **NJ non-criminal basis no pre-fee required**; **NJ citation pins FCHA**; **NYC criminal denial compliant**; **NYC no individualized assessment violates** (Local Law 24 + blanket); **NYC no rehabilitation evidence violates**; **NYC no written notice violates**; **NYC citation pins Local Law 24 and FARE Act** (June 11, 2025); **default credit denial with CRA notice compliant**; **default no CRA contact violates FCRA** (§ 1681m); **default citation pins FCRA baseline**; **four regimes routed correctly**; **NJ uniquely requires pre-fee disclosure invariant** (4-regime sweep); **NJ + NYC uniquely require individualized assessment invariant**; **statutory protection engaged for CA + NJ + NYC only invariant**; **CA note pins ICRAA**; **NJ note pins before accepting fee**; **NYC note pins January 1, 2025 and June 11, 2025** (Local Law 24 + FARE Act); **default note pins FCRA less stringent**; **denial basis truth table NJ** (5-cell sweep: only CriminalBackground triggers pre-fee).

`traderview-expense::rental_bed_bug_disclosure` is the **rental property bed bug infestation pre-lease disclosure compliance module** — when must a trader-landlord disclose bed bug infestation history to prospective tenants before lease signing? Trader-landlord operational concern: missed bed bug disclosure exposes landlord to statutory damages + applicant constructive eviction claim + reputational risk in competitive multifamily markets. Distinct from siblings `rental_application_denial_disclosure` (FCRA-style adverse action), `rental_hot_water_temperature` (habitability minimums), `tenant_fire_safety_plan_disclosure` (fire safety), `rental_bedroom_egress_window` (structural).

**Four regimes** — California AB 551 vs NYC Multiple Dwelling Law vs Arizona tenant-request vs Maine strictest single-unit rule:

**California — Cal. Civ. Code § 1954.603 (AB 551 of 2015, effective January 1, 2017 new tenants / July 1, 2017 existing tenants)** — written notice to prospective tenants BEFORE initiating new tenancy in at least 10-point type describing bed bug appearance, behavior, lifecycle, infestation indicators; landlord may NOT show, rent, or lease vacant unit known to have current infestation (§ 1954.603(b)); 2-business-day post-inspection notification to tenant (§ 1954.603(c)).

**New York City — NYC Multiple Dwelling Law § 27-2018.1** — annual building-wide bed bug report (NYC Form RA-89) filed with HPD between December 1 and December 31 each year; prior year's filing provided to every new tenant before lease signing; $250 civil penalty for failure to file annual report; tenant remedies include rent abatement and constructive eviction.

**Arizona — A.R.S. § 33-1319** — tenant-request-only ADHS educational materials disclosure; no proactive pre-lease disclosure required; landlord may not place tenant in unit known to be currently infested; least stringent regime among comparators.

**Maine — 14 M.R.S. § 6021-A (strictest single-unit rule)** — pre-rental written disclosure of any bed bug infestation in unit OR adjacent unit within prior 12 months; landlord must inspect within 5 days of tenant written notice of suspected infestation; 24-hour written disclosure of inspection results; treatment at landlord's expense unless tenant caused infestation; $250 to $1,500 per-violation civil penalty.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-bed-bug-disclosure`. Thirty-five tests pin: **CA clean disclosure compliant**; **CA no pre-lease disclosure violation** (§ 1954.603(a)); **CA missing educational content violation** (10-point type); **CA renting known infested unit violation** (§ 1954.603(b)); **CA known infested not rented no violation**; **NYC clean disclosure compliant**; **NYC missing annual HPD filing violation** (§ 27-2018.1 Form RA-89); **NYC missing prior year disclosure violation**; **AZ no tenant request no disclosure required**; **AZ tenant requested disclosure required** (ADHS materials); **AZ tenant requested disclosure provided compliant**; **AZ renting known infested violation**; **ME clean disclosure compliant**; **ME prior 12-month infestation without disclosure violation** (§ 6021-A(2)); **ME inspection within 5 days compliant** (boundary); **ME inspection at 6 days violation**; **ME 24-hour disclosure compliant** (boundary); **ME 25-hour disclosure violation**; **ME penalty at minimum in range** ($250); **ME penalty at maximum in range** ($1,500); **ME penalty below minimum out of range violation**; **ME penalty above maximum out of range violation**; **citation pins CA / NYC / AZ / ME authorities** (all four); **NYC strictest annual report invariant** (only NYC requires annual HPD filing); **AZ least stringent no pre-lease default** (only AZ skips); **ME strictest inspection and disclosure dual deadlines invariant** (5-day + 24-hour stack); **known infestation truth table all regimes** (4-regime sweep — all four prohibit renting known infested); **note pins CA AB 551 effective dates** (January 1, 2017); **note pins NYC December filing window**; **note pins AZ tenant-request-only model**; **note pins ME civil penalty range**; **defensive Maine penalty zero treated as compliant**.

`traderview-expense::rental_basement_water_intrusion_disclosure` is the **rental property basement water intrusion / mold disclosure compliance module** — when must a trader-landlord disclose water intrusion, flood history, visible mold, and remediation history to prospective and existing tenants? Trader-landlord operational concern: undisclosed water intrusion creates breach of warranty of habitability + constructive eviction + per-tenant statutory damages + multi-tenant class action exposure when mold-related health claims arise. Distinct from siblings `mold_disclosure` (general mold framework), `flood_disclosure` (flood zone), `rental_bedroom_egress_window` (structural), `rental_hot_water_temperature` (habitability minimums).

**Four regimes** — Maryland (Tenant Mold Protection Act) vs Virginia (move-in inspection) vs New York (PCDS + NYC LL55) vs Default:

**Maryland — Maryland Tenant Mold Protection Act of 2024 (eff. July 1, 2025) + MD Real Property § 8-211 + § 8-211.1** — landlord must disclose **visible mold + water intrusion + documented remediation history in writing BEFORE move-in**; **mold information pamphlet** required at lease signing; **15-day mold assessment deadline** from receipt of tenant written notice; **45-day remediation completion deadline** from assessment; breach of warranty of habitability + tenant rescission right + statutory damages for violations.

**Virginia — Va. Code § 55.1-1220 + § 8.01-226.12 + § 55.1-1216** — landlord must provide **itemized move-in inspection report within 5 days** of move-in disclosing **visible mold** in readily accessible interior areas; § 55.1-1220 requires landlord to take steps to **prevent moisture accumulation and mold growth**; if report discloses mold, tenant may terminate OR stay — if stay, landlord must **remediate within 5 days** of tenant decision.

**New York — Property Condition Disclosure Act + NY GOL § 5-905 + NYC Local Law 55 of 2018 + NYC Admin Code § 27-2017** — Property Condition Disclosure Act requires past/present mold problem disclosure on PCDS; **natural flood event history** required under NY GOL § 5-905 (plumbing/mechanical floods NOT voluntarily required); NYC Local Law 55 of 2018 — buildings with **more than one unit** must keep dwellings free of allergens including mold; **annual inspection required**; indoor allergen hazard reduction protocols under § 27-2017.

**Default — common-law implied warranty of habitability + EPA Mold Remediation Guidance + CDC Stachybotrys + Fair Housing Act + ADA** — no specific statewide water intrusion / mold disclosure mandate; general implied warranty of habitability covers uninhabitable conditions; federal Fair Housing Act + ADA may apply for tenants with mold-induced respiratory conditions; EPA 402-K-01-001 mold remediation best practices.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-basement-water-intrusion-disclosure`. Thirty-four tests pin: **MD clean compliant**; **MD no pre-move-in disclosure violation** (Mold Protection Act); **MD no mold pamphlet violation**; **MD assessment at 15-day boundary compliant**; **MD assessment at 16 days without completion violation**; **MD remediation at 45-day boundary compliant**; **MD remediation at 46 days without completion violation**; **VA clean compliant**; **VA no move-in report violation** (§ 55.1-1216 5-day); **VA mold present but not disclosed violation** (§ 8.01-226.12); **VA mold present disclosed compliant**; **VA tenant stay election 5-day remediation compliant**; **VA tenant stay election 6-day remediation violation**; **NY clean compliant**; **NY no PCDS mold disclosure violation** (Property Condition Disclosure Act); **NY no flood history disclosure violation** (§ 5-905); **NYC LL55 multi-unit no annual inspection violation**; **NYC LL55 single-unit no annual inspection no violation** (out of scope); **default no violations**; **citation pins MD / VA / NY / default authorities** (all four); **note pins MD July 2025 effective date**; **note pins MD 15-day + 45-day deadlines**; **note pins VA § 55.1-1216 5-day move-in report**; **note pins VA 5-day remediation after tenant stay**; **note pins NY natural-vs-mechanical flood distinction**; **note pins NYC LL55 multi-unit annual inspection**; **note pins default EPA Mold Remediation Guidance** (EPA 402-K-01-001); **MD uniquely requires mold pamphlet invariant**; **VA 5-day remediation uniquely shorter than MD 45 invariant**; **NYC LL55 uniquely requires annual inspection invariant**; **multiple MD violations stack** (3+ violations).

`traderview-expense::rental_carbon_monoxide_detector` is the **rental property carbon monoxide (CO) detector compliance module** — when must a trader-landlord install, maintain, and certify CO alarms in dwelling units with fossil-fuel-burning appliances or attached garages? Trader-landlord operational concern: missing or non-functional CO alarms create criminal-misdemeanor exposure (IL Class B), civil penalties (CA $100/violation), and wrongful-death liability when CO poisoning occurs. Distinct from siblings `rental_bed_bug_disclosure` (lease disclosure), `rental_hot_water_temperature` (habitability minimums), `tenant_fire_safety_plan_disclosure` (fire safety), `rental_bedroom_egress_window` (structural), `rental_gas_appliance_ban` (electrification).

**Four regimes** — California SB 183 vs New York Amanda's Law vs Illinois CO Alarm Detector Act vs Massachusetts Nicole's Law (strictest):

**California — SB 183 of 2010 (Cal. Health & Safety Code §§ 13260-13263, Carbon Monoxide Poisoning Prevention Act)** — CO alarm required in all dwelling units intended for human occupancy with fossil-fuel-burning appliance OR attached garage; **State Fire Marshal-certified device required**; installed outside each sleeping area AND on every level including basements; compliance deadlines — single-family fossil-fuel/garage by July 1, 2011; multifamily by January 1, 2013; $100/violation statutory damages under § 13263.

**New York — Amanda's Law (NY Exec. Law § 378(5-a), effective February 22, 2010)** — named for Amanda Hansen who died from CO poisoning at a 2009 sleepover; CO alarm required in every one-and-two-family dwelling and multiple dwelling where carbon-monoxide source is present; **alarm within 15 feet of each sleeping area entrance**; UL 2034 listed.

**Illinois — Carbon Monoxide Alarm Detector Act (430 ILCS 135/1 et seq., effective January 1, 2007)** — CO detector within 15 feet of every sleeping room in dwelling unit with fossil-fuel-burning appliance OR attached garage; **failure to install is a Class B misdemeanor** (430 ILCS 135/15); UL 2034 listed.

**Massachusetts — Nicole's Law (M.G.L. c. 148 § 26F½, effective March 31, 2006)** — strictest among comparators; named for Nicole Garofalo who died of CO poisoning in 2005; **interconnected (hardwired or wireless) CO alarms required on every level**; within 10 feet of each bedroom door (stricter than NY/IL 15-foot rule); **certificate of compliance from local fire department REQUIRED BEFORE selling or renting** the dwelling; UL 2034 listed.

Mounted at `POST /api/rental/rental-carbon-monoxide-detector`. Thirty-five tests pin: **CA clean compliant**; **CA no alarm with CO source violation** (SB 183); **CA no CO source no alarm compliant**; **CA missing State Fire Marshal certification violation** (§ 13261); **CA not on every level violation** (§ 13262); **NY clean compliant**; **NY no alarm with CO source violation** (Amanda's Law § 378(5-a)); **NY not UL 2034 listed violation**; **NY 15-foot boundary compliant**; **NY 16-foot distance violation**; **IL clean compliant**; **IL no alarm Class B misdemeanor violation** (430 ILCS 135/15); **IL 15-foot boundary compliant**; **IL 16-foot distance violation**; **MA clean compliant**; **MA battery-only not interconnected violation**; **MA hardwired-not-interconnected violation**; **MA interconnected wireless compliant**; **MA 10-foot boundary compliant**; **MA 11-foot violation**; **MA missing fire department certificate violation**; **MA strictest 10-foot distance invariant** (11 ft NY ok, 11 ft MA fails); **MA uniquely requires interconnection invariant** (CA battery ok, MA battery fails); **MA uniquely requires fire department certificate invariant**; **citation pins CA / NY / IL / MA authorities** (all four); **note pins CA July 2011 + January 2013 deadlines**; **note pins NY Amanda Hansen origin**; **note pins IL Class B misdemeanor**; **note pins MA Nicole Garofalo origin**; **no CO source no violations all regimes** (4-regime sweep); **power source truth table for MA** (4-cell — only interconnected variants pass); **multiple MA violations stack** (4+ violations).

`traderview-expense::rental_elevator_safety_inspection` is the **rental property elevator safety inspection compliance module** — when must a trader-landlord operating a multifamily building with elevators comply with state-specific periodic inspection, testing, and certification requirements grounded in ASME A17.1 Safety Code for Elevators and Escalators? Trader-landlord operational concern: expired elevator certification creates strict-liability exposure for elevator injury claims + per-day civil penalty + emergency department shutdown authority + tort liability multiplier. Distinct from siblings `rental_swimming_pool_drain_safety` (VGB Act), `rental_carbon_monoxide_detector`, `rental_bedroom_egress_window`, `soft_story_seismic_retrofit`.

**Three regimes** — California (Cal/OSHA + Form 80) vs NYC (DOB Category 1/3/5 + PVT-A) vs Default (ASME A17.1-2025):

**California — Cal. Labor Code §§ 7300-7324.2 + Title 8 Subchapter 6 Elevator Safety Orders** — Cal/OSHA Elevator Unit at California DIR issues elevator permits. **Annual inspection required**; permit valid 1 year. § 7317 — inspectors must have at least **4 years mechanical/electrical experience** including 1 year elevator-specific. § 7300.1 adopts ASME A17.1 by reference. § 7320 — **Form 80 permit-to-operate** must be posted in elevator; **$200/day civil penalty** for operation without valid permit.
