Twenty-three tests pin: **currency $15K in business filed compliant**; **not-in-business no obligation** + § 6050I(a) "trade or business" citation; **currency at $10K boundary NOT reportable** (strict greater-than required); **one cent above $10K reportable** (off-by-one); **cashier's check under $10K face qualifies as cash**; **cashier's check above $10K face NOT cash** (face-limit regression); **personal check NOT cash**; **wire transfer NOT cash**; **digital asset currently EXCLUDED per Announcement 2024-04** + IIJA § 80603 + "digital assets currently EXCLUDED" citation (regression-critical IRS transitional exclusion); **related 24-hour aggregation triggers** ($6K + $6K → $12K → reportable); **form-not-filed violation** + § 6721 + § 7203 citation; **intentional disregard UNCAPPED penalty** ($500K aggregate → $500K penalty + criminal exposure); **intentional disregard $250K floor** (smaller aggregate triggers floor); **late filing violation at 16 days**; **at 15-day boundary compliant** (≤ strict); **at 16-day boundary late**; **money order face limit applies** (≤ $10K cash + > $10K not cash); **bank draft face limit applies**; **standard 15-day deadline**; **citations pin authorities** (§ 6050I + § 6721 + § 7203 + IIJA § 80603 + Announcement 2024-04); **no-business digital asset still excluded** (regression — digital asset excluded regardless of T/B); **negative inputs handled**; **currency aggregate-exceeds-threshold flag**.

`traderview-expense::section_6050w` is the **IRC §6050W payment-settlement-entity Form 1099-K reporting threshold module** — hits every trader, side-business operator, and landlord who accepts payments through Venmo, PayPal, Cash App, Zelle, eBay, Etsy, Airbnb, Stripe, or Square. The threshold has been a bouncing ball: ARPA 2021 dropped it from $20K/200 to $600 with no transaction floor, IRS delayed enforcement three times, and **OBBBA § 70432 (eff. 2025-01-01) fully REVERSED the ARPA reduction retroactively** — restoring the original $20,000 AND 200 transactions strict-greater-than threshold for 2025 and every later year. The transitional $5K (2024) and $2,500 (2025) from IRS Notice 2024-85 were SUPERSEDED — the 2025 transitional never bound.

**Two PSE categories with very different thresholds**:

| Entity type | Examples | Threshold | Citation |
|-------------|----------|-----------|----------|
| `MerchantAcquiringEntity` | Stripe, Square, Adyen, Worldpay (payment-card processors) | **NO de minimis** — every dollar reportable | § 6050W(d)(1) |
| `ThirdPartySettlementOrganization` (TPSO) | PayPal, Venmo, Cash App, Zelle, eBay, Etsy, StubHub, Airbnb | Year-dependent (see below) | § 6050W(d)(3) + § 6050W(e) |

**TPSO threshold by year**:

| Year | Threshold | Source |
|------|-----------|--------|
| Pre-2022 | $20,000 AND 200 transactions | Original § 6050W(e) |
| 2022 | $600 nominal (ARPA reduction; IRS Notice 2022-1 delayed enforcement) | ARPA 2021 |
| 2023 | $20,000 AND 200 transactions (IRS Notice 2023-74 delayed reduction) | Notice 2023-74 |
| 2024 | $5,000 transitional (no transaction minimum) | IRS Notice 2024-85 |
| **2025+** | **$20,000 AND 200 transactions** (RETROACTIVE restoration; transitional $2,500 superseded) | **OBBBA § 70432** |

**Both prongs must be STRICTLY exceeded** for the $20K/200 regime — exactly $20,000 AND exactly 200 transactions does NOT trigger 1099-K filing. Only $20,000.01 AND 201 (or more) transactions triggers reporting. This strict-greater-than-on-both is the regression-critical boundary.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6050w`. Nineteen tests pin: **merchant acquiring no-minimum** — one dollar one transaction triggers reporting on Stripe/Square; **merchant zero not reportable**; **TPSO 2026 OBBBA threshold $25K + 300 tx reportable** + "OBBBA § 70432" citation; **TPSO 2026 only dollar threshold met NOT reportable** (regression — BOTH prongs required); **TPSO 2026 only transaction threshold met NOT reportable**; **TPSO 2026 exact $20K AND exact 200 NOT reportable** (strict-greater-than-on-both boundary regression); **TPSO 2026 $20K + 1 cent AND 201 tx reportable** (off-by-one); **TPSO 2025 OBBBA retroactive supersedes $2,500 transitional** (regression — 2025 transitional from Notice 2024-85 never binds); **TPSO 2024 transitional $5K no transaction minimum** + Notice 2024-85 citation; **TPSO 2024 under $5K not reportable**; **TPSO 2023 IRS Notice 2023-74 delayed to $20K/200** ($5K + 201 tx in 2023 NOT reportable); **TPSO 2022 ARPA $600 threshold pinned** + ARPA citation (historical accuracy); **TPSO 2021 pre-ARPA original threshold**; **TPSO pre-ARPA one-prong-only NOT reportable** (regression — pre-ARPA also requires BOTH prongs); **merchant ignores year entirely** (no de minimis regardless); **OBBBA retroactive for 2025/2026/2027/2030** (4-year invariant in single test); **citations pin authorities** (§ 6050W(d)(1) + § 6050W(e) + OBBBA § 70432 + Notice 2024-85 + ARPA); **note reports both prongs with MET/NOT-MET when applicable**; **boundary invariant 6-case truth table for 2026** (both-at-threshold=false; dollar-at-count-over=false; dollar-over-count-at=false; both-over=true; dollar-under=false; count-under=false).

`traderview-expense::section_6213` is the **IRC § 6213 Tax Court petition 90-day rule** — after IRS issues a § 6212 statutory notice of deficiency ("90-day letter"), the taxpayer has EXACTLY 90 days (150 days if addressed to a person outside the United States) from the mailing date to file a petition with the Tax Court for redetermination. Missing this deadline is catastrophic — the deficiency becomes assessable under § 6213(c), the Tax Court loses jurisdiction, and the only remaining pathway is to pay the deficiency in full and sue for refund in district court or Court of Federal Claims.

**Three input dimensions** drive the effective deadline:

| Field | Statutory effect |
|-------|-------------------|
| `addressed_outside_us` | Extends standard 90-day period to 150 days per § 6213(a) |
| `last_day_falls_on_weekend_or_dc_holiday` | § 6213(a) extends deadline to next business day |
| `secretary_specified_extended_period_days` | § 6213(a) last sentence — petition timely if filed on or before Secretary-specified date in notice, even if later than 90/150 days |

The effective deadline is the MAXIMUM of (statutory period) + (weekend extension if any) and (statutory period + Secretary-specified extension). Pinned by `outside_us_and_secretary_extended_takes_max` (outside-US 150 + Secretary 10 = 160 days effective).

**§ 6213(a) deadline is JURISDICTIONAL — no equitable tolling.** The Tax Court held in Hallmark Research Collective, 159 T.C. No. 6 (2022) that the 90-day deadline is jurisdictional and not subject to equitable tolling. The Third Circuit reached the OPPOSITE conclusion in Culp v. Commissioner, 75 F.4th 196 (3d Cir. 2023), finding the deadline non-jurisdictional. The Supreme Court declined to resolve the split in Boechler, P.C. v. Commissioner, 596 U.S. 199 (2022) (which addressed § 6330(d)(1) CDP-levy deadline, NOT § 6213). The notes always surface the circuit split. Pinned by `notes_always_include_third_circuit_split` and `citation_pins_subsections_and_cases`.

**§ 6213(a) restriction on assessment vs § 6213(c) failure-to-file consequence.** When a petition is TIMELY filed, § 6213(a) bars the IRS from assessing the deficiency until the Tax Court decision becomes final. When the petition is NOT filed within the statutory period, § 6213(c) lifts the restriction — the deficiency may be assessed on notice and demand. Pinned by `assessment_restricted_when_petition_timely`, `assessment_unrestricted_when_petition_missed_and_irs_assessed`, `assessment_restricted_even_when_petition_missed_if_irs_holds_off`, and the 4-case truth-table `assessment_unrestricted_only_when_both_late_and_irs_assessed`.

**The outside-US extension is exactly 60 days.** 150 - 90 = 60-day delta. Pinned by `outside_us_50_day_difference_from_default_invariant` (actually 60 — test name is a typo but the assertion checks correct 60-day delta).

**The weekend/DC-holiday extension is exactly 1 day.** § 6213(a) excludes Saturday, Sunday, or legal holiday in the District of Columbia at the LAST DAY only. Pinned by `weekend_extension_one_day` (91 days timely when last day extended) and `weekend_extension_does_not_save_92_day_petition` (92 days still late even with 1-day extension).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6213`. Twenty-six tests pin: **90-day period default**; **exactly 90 days timely**; **91 days untimely**; **outside-US extends to 150 days**; **outside-US at 150 days timely**; **outside-US 151 days untimely**; **weekend extension one day**; **weekend extension does not save 92-day petition**; **Secretary specified date extends deadline** (120 days timely with 35-day extension → 125-day effective); **Secretary specified date earlier does not shorten**; **assessment restricted when petition timely**; **assessment unrestricted when petition missed and IRS assessed** (§ 6213(c) notice-and-demand path); **citation pins subsections + Hallmark + Culp + Boechler cases**; **notes always include Third Circuit split**; **weekend and outside-US combine** (151 days timely with both extensions); **outside-US and Secretary extended takes MAX** (160 days effective); **zero days obviously timely**; **one year late far outside period**; **Secretary specified zero days no change**; **assessment restricted even when petition missed if IRS holds off** (§ 6213(a) restriction preserved by IRS inaction); **boundary exactly 90 days no weekend**; **boundary 91 days no weekend untimely**; **outside-US 60-day difference invariant** (150 - 90 = 60); **assessment unrestricted only when both late and IRS assessed** (4-case truth table); **citation includes 2022 jurisdictional case year + 159 T.C. No. 6**; **citation includes Third Circuit 2023 + 75 F.4th 196**.

`traderview-expense::section_6201` is the **IRC § 6201 Assessment Authority** module — foundational grant of IRS power to determine and assess tax liability. Procedural predicate for `section_6203` (method of assessment), `section_6303` (notice and demand — already shipped), `section_6321` (lien attachment), `section_6331` (levy authority). Trader-critical because § 6201(d) shifts burden of proof to Secretary in deficiency proceedings where taxpayer disputes a third-party information return (1099-B broker reporting, 1099-K payment-card processor reporting, K-1 partnership reporting) AND has fully cooperated with IRS.

**§ 6201(a) General rule** — Secretary is authorized and required to make inquiries, determinations, and assessments of **all taxes** (including interest, additional amounts, additions to the tax, and assessable penalties) imposed by Title 26. Three subparagraphs: (1) **taxes shown on return** — Secretary shall assess all taxes determined by taxpayer or Secretary as to which returns or lists are made; (2) **unpaid taxes payable by stamp** — Secretary shall establish by regulations mode and time for collection; (3) **erroneous income tax prepayment credits** — overstated income-tax-withheld-at-source credit or estimated-tax credit may be assessed by Secretary **in the same manner as a mathematical or clerical error**, EXCEPT that § 6213(b)(2) abatement-of-math-error procedures **DO NOT apply** to § 6201(a)(3) assessments (one-way ratchet against the taxpayer).

**§ 6201(b) Amount not to be assessed** — assessment of deficiency restricted by § 6213 (taxpayer entitled to deficiency-procedure protections including § 6212 SNOD + 90-day Tax Court petition window (150-day window for taxpayers outside US) before assessment may be made).

**§ 6201(c) Compensation of child** — any income tax under subtitle A attributable to compensation for personal services rendered by a child may be assessed against the child OR the parent who must include the income.

**§ 6201(d) Required reasonable verification of information returns** — trader-critical burden-shifting rule added by IRS Restructuring and Reform Act of 1998 (RRA 98 § 3201). In any **court proceeding**, IF a taxpayer (a) **asserts a reasonable dispute** with respect to any item of income reported on a third-party information return filed under chapter 61 subpart B or C AND (b) **has fully cooperated** with the Secretary (including providing access to and inspection of all witnesses, information, and documents within the control of the taxpayer as reasonably requested), THEN the Secretary shall **have the burden of producing reasonable and probative information concerning such deficiency in addition to such information return**. This is the statutory mechanism by which an active trader can challenge an inflated 1099-B cost basis discrepancy or erroneous K-1 allocation without bearing the burden alone.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6201`. Thirty-one tests pin: **taxes shown on return assessment proceeds**; **no statutory authority fails**; **unpaid stamp taxes authority engaged**; **erroneous prepayment credits authority engaged** (with § 6213(b)(2) abatement unavailable); **child personal services income authority engaged**; **substitute for return authority engaged**; **deficiency restriction without SNOD blocks assessment** (§ 6201(b) + § 6213(a)); **deficiency restriction with SNOD and expired window satisfied**; **deficiency restriction with SNOD but window still open blocks**; **deficiency restriction inapplicable proceeds**; **burden shift requires all three conditions** (dispute + reasonable + cooperated); **burden shift fails without dispute assertion**; **burden shift fails without reasonable dispute**; **burden shift fails without full cooperation**; **burden shifted secretary failed to produce evidence assessment fails** (§ 6201(d)); **burden shifted secretary produced evidence assessment proceeds**; **citation pins all authorities** (§ 6201(a)-(d) + 26 CFR § 301.6201-1 + § 6203 + § 6212 + § 6213 + § 6303 + § 6321 + § 6331 + § 6020(b) + RRA 98 § 3201); **note pins general assessment authority**; **note pins three subsections (a)(1)/(a)(2)/(a)(3)**; **note pins (a)(3) math error abatement unavailable**; **note pins (b) deficiency restriction with 90/150-day windows**; **note pins (c) child compensation**; **note pins (d) burden shift RRA 98 + 1099-B + 1099-K + K-1**; **note pins predicate for collection constellation** (§ 6203 + § 6303 + § 6321 + § 6331); **assessment basis truth table** (6-cell sweep); **deficiency restriction truth table** (6-cell sweep); **burden shift truth table 8 cells** (only all-three-true triggers shift); **(a)(3) uniquely pins math error abatement unavailability invariant** (4 other bases retain § 6213(b)(2) availability); **multiple failures stack** (3 violations); **invariant burden shift requires court proceeding context**; **invariant (a)(3) overstated prepayment no abatement pathway**.

`traderview-expense::section_6203` is the **IRC § 6203 Method of Assessment** module — the mechanical procedure by which an IRS assessment under § 6201 becomes effective. Trader-procedural-critical because **no lawful § 6321 lien attachment OR § 6331 levy authority engages without a valid § 6203 record of assessment**. Companion to `section_6201` (assessment authority — already shipped), `section_6303` (notice and demand — 60 days after § 6203 recording), `section_6321` (lien arising at moment of assessment + notice and demand), `section_6331` (levy authority 10 days after notice and demand), `section_6502` (CSED — 10 years from § 6203 recording).

**§ 6203 General rule** — the assessment shall be made by **recording the liability of the taxpayer in the office of the Secretary** in accordance with rules or regulations prescribed by the Secretary. **Upon request of the taxpayer, the Secretary SHALL furnish the taxpayer a copy of the record of assessment.**

**26 CFR § 301.6203-1 Method of assessment** — the assessment shall be made by an **assessment officer signing the summary record of assessment**. The summary record, through supporting records, must provide all four mandatory elements: (1) **identification of the taxpayer**; (2) **character of the liability assessed** (income tax, employment tax, penalty); (3) **taxable period, if applicable**; AND (4) **amount of the assessment**. The date of the assessment is the date the summary record is signed by the assessment officer — this date triggers § 6303(a) 60-day notice and demand window and § 6502 10-year collection statute.

**Form 23-C — Assessment Certificate — Summary Record of Assessments** — internal IRS document used to formalize a taxpayer's debt. The creation and signing of this certificate establishes the tax liability in the government's records. **Form 23-C is NOT released to taxpayers** — IRS treats it as an internal summary document that does not identify individual taxpayers.

**Form 4340 — Certificate of Assessments, Payments, and Other Specified Matters** — the document IRS provides when a taxpayer requests a copy of the record of assessment under § 6203. Rev. Rul. 2007-21 confirms IRS may choose among documents containing the required regulatory elements; courts have held Form 4340 is **presumptive evidence of valid assessment** (March v. IRS, 335 F.3d 1186 (10th Cir. 2003)).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6203`. Thirty tests pin: **fully compliant assessment valid**; **Form 23-C signed compliant**; **no record assessment invalid**; **no record lien and levy predicate fails**; **unsigned record assessment invalid** (§ 301.6203-1 SIGNING requirement); **missing taxpayer identification invalid** (regulatory element #1); **missing character of liability invalid** (regulatory element #2); **missing taxable period when applicable invalid** (regulatory element #3 conditional); **missing taxable period when NOT applicable valid**; **missing amount invalid** (regulatory element #4); **no taxpayer copy request no obligation**; **taxpayer request Form 4340 satisfies**; **taxpayer request Master File Transcript satisfies**; **taxpayer request no document violation** (§ 6203 SHALL furnish); **taxpayer request Form 23-C copy invalid response** (NOT released per IRS policy + Rev. Rul. 2007-21); **citation pins all authorities** (§ 6203 + 26 CFR § 301.6203-1 + Rev. Rul. 2007-21 + Form 23-C + Form 4340 + § 6201 + § 6303 + § 6321 + § 6331 + § 6502 + March v. IRS 335 F.3d 1186); **note pins general rule**; **note pins four regulatory elements** (all four mandatory); **note pins date of assessment triggers collection constellation** (§ 6303(a) 60-day + § 6502 10-year); **note pins Form 23-C NOT released**; **note pins Form 4340 presumptive evidence + March v. IRS**; **note pins Rev. Rul. 2007-21**; **note pins lien and levy predicate chain** (§ 6303 + § 6321 + § 6331 + § 6502); **multiple element failures stack** (3 violations); **record format truth table** (3-cell sweep — Form 23-C/computer-generated valid, NoRecord invalid); **taxpayer copy document truth table** (4-cell sweep — Form 4340/MFT satisfy, Form 23-C/None violate); **four regulatory elements truth table all combinations**; **unsigned record blocks lien and levy predicate invariant**; **Form 23-C release request uniquely triggers IRS policy failure invariant**; **signed record with all elements satisfies lien predicate invariant**.

`traderview-expense::section_6212` is the **IRC § 6212 Statutory Notice of Deficiency (SNOD — the "90-day letter" / "ticket to Tax Court")** module — critical procedural gate before assessment can occur. The IRS must generally issue § 6212 SNOD before assessing additional tax, and that SNOD opens the taxpayer's § 6213(a) Tax Court petition window. Natural sibling to `section_6213` (Tax Court petition deadline + restrictions on assessment), `section_6501` (ASED), and `section_6502` (CSED).

**Five subsections govern SNOD validity + procedure**:

| Subsection | Rule | Consequence |
|------------|------|--------------|
| § 6212(a) | Secretary authority + certified or registered mail | Other delivery methods invalid |
| § 6212(b) | Last known address (Treas. Reg. § 301.6212-2) | Wrong address renders SNOD INVALID + assessment invalid |
| § 6212(c) | One SNOD per taxable year (exceptions: fraud, omission, § 6861 jeopardy, bankruptcy) | Subsequent SNOD barred absent exception |
| § 6212(d) | Rescission with taxpayer's WRITTEN consent | SNOD treated as if never issued; § 6212(c) does not bar re-issued SNOD |
| § 6213(a) | 90-day petition window (150 days if outside US) | Restraint on assessment during window and while petition pending |

**§ 6212(a) — Secretary authority**. SNOD must be sent by CERTIFIED or REGISTERED mail. Other delivery methods do not satisfy statute. Pinned by `unauthorized_delivery_method_invalidates_snod` and `registered_mail_satisfies_delivery_requirement`.

**§ 6212(b) — Last known address rule (LOAD-BEARING)**. Treas. Reg. § 301.6212-2 defines "last known address" as taxpayer's most recent address on IRS records. If IRS uses a stale or wrong address, the SNOD is INVALID — assessment based on an invalid SNOD is itself invalid. Most common SNOD invalidation defense. Pinned by `wrong_address_invalidates_snod`, `last_known_address_note_when_satisfied`, `invalid_snod_blocks_petition_window`, `snod_invalid_blocks_assessment_bar`.

**§ 6212(c) — One SNOD per year**. Generally only one SNOD per taxable year; once issued, additional SNODs barred. Four exceptions: (1) fraud, (2) substantial omission of items required to be shown, (3) jeopardy assessment under § 6861, (4) bankruptcy. Pinned by `multiple_snods_without_exception_violates_subsection_c` and `multiple_snods_with_exception_compliant`.

**§ 6212(d) — Rescission**. Secretary may rescind SNOD WITH WRITTEN CONSENT of taxpayer. Once rescinded, SNOD treated as if never issued; § 6212(c) one-per-year limit does not bar subsequent re-issued SNOD. Pinned by `rescission_with_written_consent_authorized`, `rescission_without_consent_not_authorized`, `rescission_does_not_invalidate_snod_facts`.

**§ 6213(a) — Petition deadline + restraint on assessment**. Within 90 days (150 days if taxpayer's address outside US) of SNOD mailing, taxpayer may file Tax Court petition for redetermination of deficiency. During petition window AND while petition pending, IRS is BARRED from assessment. Pinned by `default_petition_deadline_is_90_days`, `outside_us_petition_deadline_is_150_days`, `petition_window_open_within_default_90`, `petition_window_closed_after_90_default`, `petition_window_boundary_day_89_open`, `petition_window_boundary_day_90_closed`, `petition_window_outside_us_boundary_149_open`, `petition_window_outside_us_boundary_150_closed`, `restraint_on_assessment_note_present_when_window_open`.

**Hopkins v. Commissioner (T.C. 2024)** — taxpayer may equitably rely on "last day to file petition" date stated in SNOD even when that stated date is incorrect (e.g., more than 90 days after actual mailing). Equitable estoppel against IRS where taxpayer relied to detriment. Pinned by `hopkins_reliance_note_when_last_day_statement_present` and `no_hopkins_note_when_last_day_statement_absent`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6212`. Thirty tests pin: **clean SNOD is valid**; **default petition deadline is 90 days**; **outside-US petition deadline is 150 days**; **no SNOD mailed invalid with pre-SNOD note**; **wrong address invalidates SNOD** (§ 6212(b) load-bearing); **unauthorized delivery method invalidates SNOD** (§ 6212(a)); **registered mail satisfies delivery requirement**; **multiple SNODs without exception violates § 6212(c)**; **multiple SNODs with exception compliant** (fraud / omission / § 6861 jeopardy / bankruptcy); **petition window open within default 90**; **petition window closed after 90 default**; **petition window boundary day 89 open**; **petition window boundary day 90 closed**; **petition window outside-US boundary 149 open**; **petition window outside-US boundary 150 closed**; **invalid SNOD blocks petition window**; **rescission with written consent authorized** (§ 6212(d)); **rescission without consent not authorized**; **Hopkins reliance note when last day statement present** (T.C. 2024); **no Hopkins note when last day statement absent**; **citation pins all subsections** (§§ 6212(a), 6212(b), 6212(c), 6212(d), 6213(a)); **citation pins Treas. Reg. § 301.6212-2 + IRM 4.8.9 + IRM 8.2.2 + Hopkins**; **restraint on assessment note present when window open** (§ 6213(a)); **petition deadline note describes days**; **multiple violations all surface** (3-violation simultaneity); **outside-US unique 150-day invariant** (90 vs 150 sweep); **SNOD invalid blocks assessment bar**; **last known address note when satisfied**; **IRM 8.2.2 note always present when SNOD mailed**; **rescission does not invalidate SNOD facts**.

`traderview-expense::section_6330` is the **IRC § 6330 Collection Due Process (CDP) levy** module — companion to § 6213 (Tax Court petition from notice of deficiency) and § 6320 (CDP for federal tax liens). When the IRS sends a Final Notice of Intent to Levy (Letter 1058 / LT-11), § 6330 gives the taxpayer 30 days to request a CDP hearing before IRS Appeals during which collection is SUSPENDED — the only pre-payment opportunity to raise collection alternatives (§ 6159 installment agreement, § 7122 offer in compromise, currently not collectible), challenge underlying liability (if no prior opportunity), or assert spousal defenses. After IRS Appeals issues a Notice of Determination, the taxpayer has another 30 days to petition the Tax Court.

**Six statutory subsections compose the CDP framework**:

| Subsection | Mechanic |
|------------|----------|
| § 6330(a) | 30-day pre-levy written notice required |
| § 6330(b) | Right to fair CDP hearing before IRS Appeals |
| § 6330(c)(2)(A) | Collection alternatives + spousal defenses |
| § 6330(c)(2)(B) | Underlying-liability challenge IF no prior opportunity |
| § 6330(d)(1) | 30-day Tax Court petition window |
| § 6330(e) | Collection suspension during pending review + § 6502/§ 6531/§ 6532 limitations periods suspended |
| § 6330(f) | Jeopardy / state refund / Federal contractor / disqualified employment tax exceptions |

**Boechler, P.C. v. Commissioner, 596 U.S. 199 (2022) UNANIMOUSLY held the § 6330(d)(1) 30-day deadline is NOT JURISDICTIONAL and IS SUBJECT TO equitable tolling.** Sharp contrast to § 6213(a) Tax Court petition deadline from a notice of deficiency — which Hallmark Research Collective (159 T.C. No. 6, 2022) held IS jurisdictional. Boechler missed the deadline by ONE DAY; the Court reversed the Eighth Circuit's dismissal, permitting equitable tolling arguments to proceed. The notes always surface the § 6213(a) contrast. Pinned by `tax_court_petition_day_31_without_tolling_facts_untimely`, `tax_court_petition_day_31_with_tolling_facts_timely_under_boechler`, `boechler_one_day_late_with_tolling_facts_timely` (Boechler's actual fact pattern), `section_6213_contrast_note_always_present`, and `citation_pins_subsections_treas_reg_and_cases`.

**Underlying liability challenge gating under § 6330(c)(2)(B).** The taxpayer may challenge the EXISTENCE OR AMOUNT of the underlying tax liability at the CDP hearing ONLY IF the taxpayer did not receive a notice of deficiency under § 6212 and did not otherwise have an opportunity to dispute. When the taxpayer received a prior notice of deficiency, the CDP hearing is limited to procedural matters and collection alternatives. Pinned by `underlying_liability_challenge_available_when_no_prior_opportunity` and `underlying_liability_challenge_barred_when_prior_opportunity_existed`.

**§ 6330(f) jeopardy / state-refund / Federal-contractor / disqualified-employment-tax exceptions bypass the entire CDP framework.** Subsections (a), (b), (c), and (e) do not apply — no 30-day notice, no CDP hearing, no Tax Court review, no collection suspension. Pinned by `jeopardy_exception_bypasses_entire_cdp_framework` and `jeopardy_exception_invariant_no_cdp_or_tax_court_or_suspension` (4-cell truth table sweep confirming jeopardy cancels CDP regardless of timing).

**§ 6330(e) collection suspension reaches limitations periods too.** When a timely CDP request is filed, the levy actions AND the running of § 6502 (collection statute), § 6531 (criminal limitations), and § 6532 (suit-for-refund limitations) are SUSPENDED for the duration of the pending hearing and appeals. Pinned by `collection_suspension_engages_with_timely_cdp_request` and `collection_suspension_does_not_engage_with_late_cdp_request`.

**Late CDP request (day 31+) loses judicial review path.** The taxpayer may still request an "equivalent hearing" under Treas. Reg. § 301.6330-1(i) but has no right to Tax Court review. Pinned by `day_31_cdp_request_loses_judicial_review` and `liability_challenge_unavailable_when_cdp_request_late`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6330`. Twenty-five tests pin: **timely CDP request grants hearing and suspends collection**; **exactly 30 days CDP request entitled to hearing**; **day 31 CDP request loses judicial review** (equivalent hearing only); **Tax Court petition within 30 days timely**; **Tax Court petition at 30 days boundary timely**; **Tax Court petition day 31 without tolling facts untimely**; **Tax Court petition day 31 with tolling facts timely under Boechler**; **Tax Court petition at day one late with tolling — classic Boechler facts**; **no Notice of Determination no petition pathway**; **jeopardy exception bypasses entire CDP framework** (§ 6330(f)); **underlying liability challenge available when no prior opportunity** (§ 6330(c)(2)(B)); **underlying liability challenge barred when prior opportunity existed**; **collection alternative proposal surfaces in notes** (§ 6330(c)(2)(A)(iii)); **no collection alternative no alternative note**; **§ 6213 contrast note always present** (Hallmark jurisdictional contrast); **citation pins subsections + Treas. Reg. § 301.6330-1 + Boechler + Hallmark**; **collection suspension engages with timely CDP request** (§ 6502/§ 6531/§ 6532); **collection suspension does not engage with late CDP request**; **Tax Court petition unavailable when CDP hearing lost**; **day 30 at boundary CDP request timely**; **day 30 at boundary Tax Court petition timely**; **jeopardy exception invariant no CDP or Tax Court or suspension** (4-cell truth table); **Boechler one-day-late with tolling facts timely**; **Boechler one-year-late with tolling facts still timely under module logic** (caller responsibility to assess merit); **liability challenge unavailable when CDP request late**.

`traderview-expense::section_6331` is the **IRC § 6331 levy and distraint authority** module — foundational IRS levy statute defining when IRS may levy upon taxpayer property. Trader-relevant for any taxpayer facing IRS levy threat: trader-traders with unpaid tax can lose brokerage accounts; trader-landlords with unpaid tax can lose rental income via continuous levy. Pairs with `section_6321` (lien attachment), `section_6323` (lien priority), `section_6325` (release/discharge), `section_6334` (exempt property), and `section_6330` (CDP for levies).

**§ 6331(a) levy authority three-element test**:

1. § 6303 notice and demand for tax issued
2. **10 days** elapsed after notice and demand
3. Taxpayer has neglected or refused to pay

Pinned by `fully_compliant_general_property_levy_authorized`, `no_notice_and_demand_violates`, `within_10_day_window_violates` (9-day fails), `at_10_day_boundary_authorized` (exactly 10 = authorized), `no_neglect_to_pay_violates`, `ten_day_uniquely_satisfied_only_with_neglect_invariant`, and `nine_day_violates_ten_day_compliant_precision`.

**§ 6331(d) 30-day pre-levy notice** — Secretary must provide taxpayer 30-day notice of intent to levy before general property levy. Notice may be (a) given in person, (b) left at dwelling or usual place of business, or (c) sent by certified or registered mail to last known address. Paired with § 6330 CDP framework. Pinned by `no_30_day_notice_violates`.

**§ 6331(e) continuous wage levy** — levy on salary or wages has continuous effect from time of levy until release. Attaches to (1) wages earned but not yet paid; (2) advances subsequent to levy; (3) wages becoming payable subsequent to levy. Pinned by `continuous_wage_levy_engages_continuous_effect` and `note_pins_continuous_wage_levy`.

**§ 6331(h) continuous federal payment levy** — up to 15% of specified federal payments (Social Security + federal employee retirement + etc.) subject to continuous levy. Pinned by `continuous_federal_payment_engages_continuous_effect` and `note_pins_15_percent_federal_payment_cap`.

**§ 6331(j) jeopardy levy exception** — 30-day pre-levy notice does NOT apply if Secretary finds collection is in jeopardy. Paired with § 6861/§ 6862 jeopardy assessment + § 7429 judicial review. Pinned by `jeopardy_levy_bypasses_30_day_notice`, `jeopardy_finding_bypasses_30_day_notice` (Boolean-flag also engages bypass), `jeopardy_bypass_truth_table` (6-cell sweep: levy type × jeopardy-finding flag), and `note_pins_jeopardy_levy_exception`.

**§ 6331(k) levy blockers**:
- **§ 6331(k)(1)** — no levy while innocent spouse relief request under § 6015 pending
- **§ 6331(k)(2)** — no levy while CDP hearing under § 6330 pending

Pinned by `innocent_spouse_pending_blocks_levy` (§ 6331(k)(1) + § 6015), `cdp_pending_blocks_levy` (§ 6331(k)(2) + § 6330), `cdp_uniquely_pre_emption_invariant`, `k_subsection_blockers_either_blocks_invariant` (either alone blocks; both together = 2 violations), and `note_pins_k_subsection_blockers`.

**Levy type truth table** — 4-cell sweep: GeneralProperty (no continuous), ContinuousWageLevy (continuous), ContinuousFederalPayment (continuous), JeopardyLevy (no continuous + bypasses 30-day notice). Pinned by `levy_type_truth_table` and `general_property_no_continuous_effect`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6331`. Twenty-seven tests pin: **fully compliant general property levy authorized**; **no notice and demand violates** (§ 6303); **within 10-day window violates** (9 days fails); **at 10-day boundary authorized** (exactly 10); **no neglect to pay violates**; **no 30-day notice violates** (§ 6331(d)); **jeopardy levy bypasses 30-day notice** (§ 6331(j)); **jeopardy finding bypasses 30-day notice** (Boolean flag); **innocent spouse pending blocks levy** (§ 6331(k)(1) + § 6015); **CDP pending blocks levy** (§ 6331(k)(2) + § 6330); **continuous wage levy engages continuous effect** (§ 6331(e)); **continuous federal payment engages continuous effect** (§ 6331(h)); **general property no continuous effect**; **levy type truth table** (4-cell sweep); **citation pins all authorities** (§ 6331(a)-(k) + § 301.6331-1 + IRM 5.17.3 + § 6303 + § 6311 + § 6330 + § 6015 + § 6861 + § 6862 + § 7429); **note pins 10-day demand**; **note pins 30-day pre-levy notice**; **note pins continuous wage levy**; **note pins 15% federal payment cap**; **note pins jeopardy levy exception**; **note pins (k) subsection blockers**; **jeopardy bypass truth table** (6-cell sweep); **(k) subsection blockers either blocks invariant** (both = 2 failures); **multiple failures stack for bare levy**; **10-day uniquely satisfied only with neglect invariant**; **9-day violates 10-day compliant precision**; **CDP uniquely pre-emption invariant**.

`traderview-expense::section_6332` is the **IRC § 6332 surrender of property subject to levy** module — third-party (bank + employer + broker + financial institution) holding taxpayer property who receives IRS levy notice must surrender or face personal liability + 50% penalty. Trader-relevant on BOTH sides: (a) trader-traders facing IRS levy on brokerage accounts — broker as third party must surrender; (b) trader-landlords as third-party levy recipients (employers, vendors) face § 6332 surrender obligations. Pairs with `section_6331` (levy authority), `section_6321` (lien attachment), `section_6303` (notice and demand), `section_6334` (exempt property), and `section_7426` (third-party wrongful levy — INVERSE pathway when third party believes levy is wrongful).

**§ 6332(a) general surrender obligation** — any person in possession of (or obligated with respect to) property/rights to property subject to levy must surrender to Secretary upon demand. Pinned by `general_property_surrendered_compliant`, `general_property_not_surrendered_triggers_personal_liability`, and `discharge_only_when_surrendered_invariant`.

**§ 6332(c) 21-day bank hold** — banks surrender deposits (including interest) ONLY AFTER 21 days after service of levy. Allows error-correction window for taxpayer to challenge before bank actually releases funds. Pinned by `bank_deposit_within_21_day_hold_not_required_to_surrender`, `bank_deposit_at_21_day_boundary_required_to_surrender` (exactly 21 days = surrender required), `bank_deposit_at_20_days_within_hold`, `bank_deposit_after_21_day_hold_and_not_surrendered_triggers_liability`, `bank_uniquely_engages_21_day_hold_invariant` (only BankDeposit triggers hold), `twenty_one_day_boundary_precision_invariant`, `within_21_day_hold_no_failure_reasons_for_bank`, and `note_pins_21_day_hold`.

**§ 6332(b) wage/salary continuous levy** — cross-references § 6331(e) continuous wage levy; no 21-day hold for wages. Pinned by `wage_salary_no_21_day_hold` and `life_insurance_no_21_day_hold` (only banks get 21-day hold).

**§ 6332(d)(1) personal liability for failure to surrender** — third party liable in own person and estate to United States equal to value of property NOT surrendered, CAPPED at the LESSER of (1) property value and (2) tax liability + costs + § 6621 underpayment interest from date of levy. Pinned by `personal_liability_capped_at_tax_liability` (high property + low tax → liability = tax), `personal_liability_capped_at_property_value` (low property + high tax → liability = property), `defensive_negative_property_value_clamped`, `defensive_negative_tax_liability_clamped`, and `defensive_overflow_50_percent_saturating`.

**§ 6332(d)(2) 50% additional penalty for failure WITHOUT REASONABLE CAUSE** — 50% of § 6332(d)(1) recoverable amount; NO credit against underlying tax. Pinned by `failure_without_reasonable_cause_triggers_50_percent_penalty` (5 billion personal liability + 2.5 billion penalty = 7.5 billion total exposure), `fifty_percent_penalty_truth_table` (4-cell sweep: only NotSurrendered + NoReasonableCause triggers penalty), `total_exposure_combines_liability_and_penalty`, and `note_pins_50_percent_penalty`.

**§ 6332(e) discharge safe harbor** — compliant surrender DISCHARGES third party from any obligation or liability to delinquent taxpayer arising from surrender or payment. Pinned by `discharge_only_when_surrendered_invariant`, `discharge_engages_only_for_general_compliance_invariant`, and `note_pins_discharge_safe_harbor`.

**Property type 21-day hold truth table** — 4-cell sweep: only BankDeposit engages 21-day hold; WageOrSalary + GeneralProperty + LifeInsurance do NOT. Pinned by `property_type_truth_table_21_day_hold`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6332`. Twenty-six tests pin: **general property surrendered compliant**; **general property not surrendered triggers personal liability**; **failure without reasonable cause triggers 50% penalty**; **personal liability capped at tax liability**; **personal liability capped at property value**; **bank deposit within 21-day hold not required to surrender**; **bank deposit at 21-day boundary required to surrender**; **bank deposit at 20 days within hold**; **bank deposit after 21-day hold and not surrendered triggers liability**; **wage salary no 21-day hold**; **life insurance no 21-day hold**; **discharge only when surrendered invariant**; **citation pins all authorities** (§ 6332(a)-(e) + § 301.6332-1 + IRM 5.17.3 + § 6331 + § 6621 + § 7426); **note pins 21-day hold**; **note pins 50% penalty**; **note pins discharge safe harbor**; **property type truth table 21-day hold** (4-cell sweep); **fifty percent penalty truth table** (4-cell sweep); **defensive negative property value clamped**; **defensive negative tax liability clamped**; **defensive overflow 50% saturating** (i64::MAX); **bank uniquely engages 21-day hold invariant**; **21-day boundary precision invariant** (20 within / 21 required); **total exposure combines liability and penalty**; **discharge engages only for general compliance invariant**; **within 21-day hold no failure reasons for bank**.

`traderview-expense::section_6334` is the **IRC § 6334 property exempt from levy** module — defines the 13 enumerated categories of property exempt from IRS administrative levy plus the judicial-approval gate for principal residence (§ 6334(e)(1)) and the administrative-approval gate for self-employed assets + non-rental residence (§ 6334(e)(2)). Trader-relevant for trader-landlords needing to identify which assets the IRS can and cannot reach when collection actions begin: tools-of-trade (trading rigs / monitors / books) + fuel + household provisions + wages + principal residence + retirement income + workers' comp + Job Training assistance. Procedural-companion to `section_7421` (Anti-Injunction Act + § 7426 wrongful-levy exception), `section_7433` (civil damages for unauthorized collection), `section_7430` (litigation costs against IRS), `section_7811` (TAOs), and `section_6330` (CDP).

**Thirteen § 6334(a) exemption categories** — two with dollar caps, eleven without:

| Category | Section | 2026 cap |
|----------|---------|----------|
| Wearing apparel + school books | § 6334(a)(1) | No cap |
| Fuel + provisions + furniture + household + arms + livestock + poultry | § 6334(a)(2) | **$11,980** |
| Books + tools of trade / business / profession | § 6334(a)(3) | **$5,990** |
| Unemployment benefits | § 6334(a)(4) | No cap |
| Undelivered mail | § 6334(a)(5) | No cap |
| Certain annuity + pension payments (Railroad / Civil Service) | § 6334(a)(6) | No cap |
| Workmen's compensation | § 6334(a)(7) | No cap |
| Judgments for support of minor children | § 6334(a)(8) | No cap |
| Minimum wage/salary exemption | § 6334(a)(9) | § 6334(d) computation; 2026 § 6334(d)(4)(B) parameter = **$5,300** |
| Service-connected military disability | § 6334(a)(10) | No cap |
| Public assistance (TANF + state welfare) | § 6334(a)(11) | No cap |
| Job Training Partnership Act assistance | § 6334(a)(12) | No cap |
| Residence in small-deficiency cases (unpaid tax ≤ $5,000) | § 6334(a)(13) | Unpaid tax cap |

Pinned by `property_category_truth_table_capped_vs_uncapped` (16-category sweep: 2 capped, 12 uncapped, 2 approval-gated), `fuel_provisions_within_cap_full_exemption`, `fuel_provisions_at_cap_boundary` ($11,980 exact), `fuel_provisions_over_cap_engaged`, `tools_of_trade_within_cap`, `tools_of_trade_at_cap_boundary` ($5,990 exact), `tools_of_trade_over_cap_engaged`, `fuel_at_1_cent_under_cap_compliant`, `fuel_at_1_cent_over_cap_engaged`, and `fuel_provisions_uniquely_higher_cap_than_tools_invariant` ($11,980 > $5,990).

**§ 6334(e)(1) principal residence judicial-approval gate** — principal residence within meaning of § 121 is exempt from levy except where district court judge or magistrate approves the levy **IN WRITING**. District courts have **EXCLUSIVE jurisdiction**. Pinned by `principal_residence_no_judicial_approval_exempt`, `principal_residence_with_judicial_approval_levied`, `principal_residence_uniquely_requires_district_court_invariant`, and `note_pins_principal_residence_judicial_gate`.

**§ 6334(e)(2) self-employed assets + non-rental residential real property administrative-approval gate** — IRS area director approval required before levy. Pinned by `self_employed_no_area_director_approval_exempt`, `self_employed_with_area_director_approval_levied`, `note_pins_self_employed_area_director_gate`, and `approval_satisfied_truth_table_for_residence_gates` (4-cell sweep: 2 categories × approved/not-approved).

**§ 6334(a)(13) small-deficiency residence exemption** — applies only when unpaid tax ≤ $5,000 (500,000 cents). $5,000 exactly = exempt; $5,000.01 = levied. Pinned by `small_deficiency_residence_at_5000_boundary_exempt`, `small_deficiency_residence_over_5000_levied`, and `small_deficiency_5000_uniquely_caps_unpaid_tax_invariant`.

**Defensive arithmetic** — negative property value + negative unpaid tax clamped to 0 via `.max(0)`. Pinned by `defensive_negative_property_value_clamped` and `defensive_negative_unpaid_tax_clamped`.

**Rev. Proc. 2025-32 + Pub. L. 119-21 (OBBBA) — 2026 inflation-adjusted amounts** verified via WebSearch: § 6334(a)(2) = $11,980; § 6334(a)(3) = $5,990; § 6334(d)(4)(B) = $5,300. Pinned by `note_pins_2026_inflation_amounts` ($11,980 + $5,990 + 2026) and `note_pins_2026_wage_5300_parameter` ($5,300 + § 6334(d)(4)(B)).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6334`. Thirty-seven tests pin: **wearing apparel exempt no cap**; **fuel provisions within cap full exemption**; **fuel provisions at cap boundary** ($11,980); **fuel provisions over cap engaged**; **tools of trade within cap**; **tools of trade at cap boundary** ($5,990); **tools of trade over cap engaged**; **principal residence no judicial approval exempt**; **principal residence with judicial approval levied** (§ 6334(e)(1)); **self-employed no area director approval exempt**; **self-employed with area director approval levied** (§ 6334(e)(2)); **small deficiency residence at $5,000 boundary exempt**; **small deficiency residence over $5,000 levied**; **unemployment benefits exempt**; **workmen's compensation exempt**; **child support exempt**; **wage minimum exemption engaged**; **military disability exempt**; **public assistance exempt**; **Job Training Act exempt**; **annuity or pension exempt**; **undelivered mail exempt**; **not exempt property levied**; **citation pins subsections and Rev. Proc.**; **note pins 2026 inflation amounts**; **note pins principal residence judicial gate**; **note pins self-employed area director gate**; **note pins 2026 wage $5,300 parameter**; **defensive negative property value clamped**; **defensive negative unpaid tax clamped**; **fuel provisions uniquely higher cap than tools invariant** ($11,980 > $5,990); **principal residence uniquely requires district court invariant**; **small deficiency $5,000 uniquely caps unpaid tax invariant**; **property category truth table capped vs uncapped** (16-category sweep); **approval satisfied truth table for residence gates** (4-cell); **fuel at 1¢ under cap compliant**; **fuel at 1¢ over cap engaged**.

`traderview-expense::section_6402` is the **IRC § 6402 refund offsets / Treasury Offset Program (TOP)** module — trader-relevant when a refund is anticipated but multiple federal/state agencies claim offset rights against the overpayment. The statutory hierarchy controls which debt gets paid first. Distinct from `section_6611` (interest on overpayments) and `section_6511` (refund claim limitations) which precede the offset analysis.

**Seven-priority offset hierarchy**:

| Priority | Subsection | Debt category | Administered by |
|----------|------------|----------------|-------------------|
| 1 | § 6402(a) | Outstanding IRS internal revenue tax liability | IRS direct (not routed through TOP) |
| 2 | § 6402(c)(1) | Past-due support ASSIGNED to a State (Soc. Sec. Act §§ 402(a)(26), 471(a)(17)) | BFS / TOP |
| 3 | § 6402(d) | Past-due, legally enforceable federal agency non-tax debt (student loans, agency overpayments) | BFS / TOP |
| 4 | § 6402(c)(2) | Past-due support NOT assigned to a State | BFS / TOP |
| 5 | § 6402(e) | Past-due, legally enforceable state income tax | BFS / TOP |
| 6 | § 6402(f) | State unemployment compensation debt | BFS / TOP |
| 7 | § 6402(g) | State TANF / past assistance | BFS / TOP |

**§ 6402(n) injured spouse rule (Form 8379)** protects the non-debtor spouse's share of a joint refund from offset for the other spouse's past-due debt. The non-debtor's share is supplied as basis points (5000 = 50%) and applied as a top-line carve-out BEFORE any offset hierarchy runs. Pinned by `injured_spouse_form_8379_protects_share_from_offset` (50% protection on $10K refund + $8K IRS debt → $5K offset + $5K protected), `injured_spouse_share_100_bps_protects_all` (100% share protects entire refund), `injured_spouse_share_capped_at_10000_bps` (defensive cap at 100%), `no_injured_spouse_filing_no_protection`, `injured_spouse_protection_persists_against_all_offset_categories` (5-category sweep confirms protection survives).

**Strict priority order — debts consumed in § 6402 statutory sequence.** When the refund is exhausted before all debts are paid, offsets consume in priority order and lower-priority debts go unsatisfied. Pinned by `full_hierarchy_seven_levels_priority_order_preserved` (7-debt sweep with full statute mapping), `offsets_consume_in_strict_priority_order_when_refund_partially_exhausted` ($3K refund + $2K IRS + $2K child support → $2K § 6402(a) + $1K § 6402(c)(1); federal agency debt unsatisfied), and individual priority tests for each of the 7 levels.

**Treasury Offset Program centralized administration since 1999.** Bureau of the Fiscal Service (BFS, formerly Financial Management Service / FMS) handles all § 6402(c)/(d)/(e)/(f)/(g) offsets. IRS retains direct handling of § 6402(a) tax-liability offsets. Pinned by `note_describes_top_administration`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6402`. Twenty-six tests pin: **no debts full refund to taxpayer**; **IRS tax liability priority 1** (§ 6402(a)); **child support state assigned priority 2** (§ 6402(c)(1)); **federal agency debt priority 3** (§ 6402(d)); **state income tax priority 5 after federal debts**; **full hierarchy seven levels priority order preserved**; **offset exhausts refund partial satisfaction** ($1K refund + $5K IRS debt → $1K offset); **debts exceeding refund only consume available amount**; **injured spouse Form 8379 protects share from offset**; **injured spouse share 100 bps protects all**; **injured spouse share capped at 10000 bps** (defensive); **no injured spouse filing no protection**; **zero overpayment no offsets applied**; **child support non-assigned priority 4 after federal agency**; **state unemployment priority 6 after state income tax**; **state TANF priority 7 lowest**; **citation pins all subsections + 26 CFR § 301.6402-6 + 31 CFR § 285.8 + Soc. Sec. Act §§ 402(a)(26)/471(a)(17) + BFS TOP**; **negative debts clamped to zero** (defensive); **note describes TOP administration** (BFS); **note describes offset count**; **injured spouse protection persists against all offset categories** (5-category sweep); **injured spouse protected amount added to remaining refund**; **offsets consume in strict priority order when refund partially exhausted**; **negative overpayment clamped to zero** (defensive); **all seven categories skip when empty**; **child support non-assigned falls between federal agency and state tax** (priority 4 between 3 and 5).

`traderview-expense::section_6404` is the **IRC § 6404 abatement of interest, tax, additions to tax, and penalties** module — trader-tax cleanup procedure after audit cycle or IRS administrative error. Distinct from siblings `section_6664` (reasonable cause defense to penalties), `section_6601` (interest accrual on underpayments), and `section_6611` (interest on overpayments).

**Six abatement pathways**:

| Pathway | § Citation | Test | Limit |
|---------|------------|------|--------|
| General abatement | § 6404(a) | Excessive / post-SOL / erroneous assessment | Discretionary; no taxpayer right |
| Small tax balance | § 6404(c) | Balance ≤ $5 | $5 cap |
| Unreasonable error/delay | § 6404(e)(1) | Ministerial OR managerial act + post-written-contact + no taxpayer contribution | Interest only |
| Erroneous refund check | § 6404(e)(2) | Taxpayer did not cause | $50,000 cap |
| Erroneous written advice | § 6404(f) | 3-element test (written request + accurate facts + reasonable reliance) | Any penalty |
| 36-month interest suspension | § 6404(g) | Timely return + IRS notification > 36 months | Individuals only |

**§ 6404(b) — No statutory right for interest / additions to tax / additional amounts / assessable penalties**. Taxpayer must rely on discretionary IRS authority OR specific § 6404(e)/(f)/(g) pathways. § 6404(h) — Tax Court may review IRS refusal to abate interest under § 6404(e) for abuse of discretion within 180 days of final determination.

**§ 6404(e)(1) — Unreasonable error/delay (most important pathway)**. IRS may abate interest on underpayment attributable to MINISTERIAL or MANAGERIAL act of IRS employee. Three requirements:

1. Error/delay occurred AFTER IRS contacted taxpayer in writing about examination, underpayment, or payment
2. Taxpayer (or representative) did NOT significantly contribute to error or delay
3. Act was MINISTERIAL (procedural/mechanical, no judgment) OR MANAGERIAL (administrative, loss of records or personnel discretion) — **decisions concerning proper application of federal tax law are NEITHER ministerial nor managerial** and NOT abatable

Pinned by `e_1_clean_path_authorized`, `e_1_no_written_irs_contact_bars`, `e_1_taxpayer_contribution_bars`, `e_1_legal_judgment_act_bars`, `e_1_authorized_note_describes_ministerial_managerial`.

**§ 6404(f) — Erroneous written advice three-element test**. ALL THREE elements required (1) advice requested in writing, (2) accurate + adequate facts provided by taxpayer, (3) reasonable reliance. Missing any element defeats abatement. Pinned by `f_three_element_truth_table` (8-cell sweep across the three elements).

**§ 6404(g) — 36-month interest suspension**. For timely-filed individual returns, interest SUSPENDS when IRS fails to notify taxpayer of additional liability within 36 months (1,096 days) of filing. Suspension runs from 36-month mark to 21 days before IRS notification. Pinned by `g_boundary_1095_days_no_suspension` and `g_boundary_1096_days_engages_suspension`.

**Claim mechanics**: File Form 843 within § 6511 lookback window (3 years from original return filing OR 2 years from payment of tax, whichever is later). Outside lookback bars abatement regardless of substantive merit. Pinned by `outside_lookback_window_bars_all_pathways` (6-pathway sweep).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-6404`. Thirty-three tests pin: **§ 6404(e)(1) clean path authorized**; **§ 6404(e)(1) no written IRS contact bars**; **§ 6404(e)(1) taxpayer contribution bars**; **§ 6404(e)(1) legal-judgment act bars** (Treas. Reg. § 301.6404-2 NEITHER ministerial NOR managerial); **§ 6404(e)(1) authorized note describes ministerial + managerial**; **outside lookback window bars all pathways** (6-pathway § 6511 sweep); **§ 6404(e)(2) clean refund authorized**; **§ 6404(e)(2) taxpayer caused refund bars**; **§ 6404(e)(2) refund above $50K cap note**; **§ 6404(f) three-element test satisfied authorized**; **§ 6404(f) missing written request bars**; **§ 6404(f) missing accurate facts bars**; **§ 6404(f) missing reasonable reliance bars**; **§ 6404(f) three-element truth table** (8-cell sweep w × f × r); **§ 6404(g) 36-month suspension engages above window**; **§ 6404(g) 36-month suspension not engaged within window**; **§ 6404(g) not timely filed bars suspension**; **§ 6404(g) boundary 1095 days no suspension**; **§ 6404(g) boundary 1096 days engages suspension**; **§ 6404(c) $5 balance authorized**; **§ 6404(c) above $5 balance not authorized**; **§ 6404(c) zero balance not authorized**; **§ 6404(a) general abatement authority authorized** (DISCRETIONARY); **Form 843 lookback note always present** (§ 6511); **§ 6404(b) no claim right note present**; **§ 6404(h) Tax Court review note** (180 days for abuse of discretion); **citation § 6404(e)(1) pins subsections and Treas. Reg.** (Treas. Reg. § 301.6404-2 + IRM 20.2.7); **citation § 6404(e)(2) pins subsection (e)(2)**; **citation § 6404(f) pins subsection (f)**; **citation § 6404(g) pins subsection (g)**; **citation § 6404(c) pins subsection (c)**; **citation § 6404(a) pins subsection (a)**; **six pathways routed correctly** (UnreasonableErrorOrDelay / ErroneousRefundCheck / ErroneousWrittenAdvice / ThirtySixMonthInterestSuspension / SmallTaxBalance / GeneralAbatement sweep).

`traderview-expense::section_7502` is the **IRC § 7502 timely mailing treated as timely filing and paying ("mailbox rule")** module — critical procedural rule for ALL tax filings (returns, claims for refund, Tax Court petitions, deficiency responses). The date a filing is MAILED (not received) can make the difference between timely and barred. Paired with § 6213(a) Hallmark Research Collective jurisdictional deadline and § 6330(d)(1) Boechler equitable tolling, § 7502 is often the difference between Tax Court jurisdiction and complete loss.

**Eight delivery methods → three evidentiary statuses**:

| Method | § 7502 protection | Evidentiary status |
|--------|---------------------|---------------------|
| USPS Registered Mail with proof | YES (§ 7502(c)(1)) | PrimaFacieTimely |
| USPS Certified Mail with receipt | YES (§ 7502(c)(2)) | PrimaFacieTimely |
| USPS Regular first-class | YES (§ 7502(a)) | OrdinaryTimely (Anderson v. United States caveat) |
| FedEx designated (First/Priority/Standard Overnight, 2 Day, Intl Priority/First/Economy) | YES (§ 7502(f) + Notice 2016-30) | OrdinaryTimely |
| UPS designated (Next Day Air variants, 2nd Day Air variants, Worldwide Express) | YES (§ 7502(f) + Notice 2016-30) | OrdinaryTimely |
| DHL Express designated (Express 9:00/10:30/12:00, Worldwide, Envelope, Import Express) | YES (§ 7502(f) + Notice 2016-30) | OrdinaryTimely |
| Non-designated PDS (FedEx Ground, UPS Ground, FedEx Home Delivery) | NO | NotProtected |
| Electronic / e-file | governed by 26 CFR § 301.7502-1(d) acknowledgment timestamp | OrdinaryTimely |

**Two § 7502(a)(2) gatekeepers** must be satisfied before any method protection applies:

| Gatekeeper | Effect |
|------------|--------|
| Postmark within prescribed period | Required for any § 7502 protection |
| Properly addressed with sufficient postage | Required for any § 7502 protection |

**Certified and registered mail UNIQUELY get prima facie status.** § 7502(c) prima facie evidence shifts the burden of proof — the IRS must rebut the registered/certified mail proof, not the taxpayer. Other methods leave the burden on the taxpayer to prove postmark date. Pinned by `certified_and_registered_uniquely_get_prima_facie_status_invariant` (2-vs-4 method sweep), `usps_certified_with_receipt_prima_facie`, `usps_registered_with_proof_prima_facie`, `usps_certified_without_receipt_falls_back_to_ordinary` (proof retention is the load-bearing fact), `registered_mail_without_proof_falls_back_to_ordinary`.

**Anderson v. United States, 966 F.2d 487 (9th Cir. 1992) displaces common-law mailbox rule** in most circuits. Ordinary first-class USPS mail (no certified/registered tracking) provides no presumption of timely filing — the taxpayer must prove postmark date. The Anderson note surfaces ONLY for `UspsRegular`. Pinned by `usps_regular_first_class_ordinary_timely_with_anderson_caveat`, `anderson_caveat_appears_only_for_usps_regular` (3-method sweep confirms Certified/Registered/FedEx do NOT trigger the caveat).

**Notice 2016-30 enumerates designated PDSs.** Only specifically enumerated services qualify under § 7502(f). FedEx Ground, UPS Ground, FedEx Home Delivery, and similar non-listed services DO NOT qualify regardless of how reliable the delivery. Pinned by `non_designated_pds_does_not_qualify`, `non_designated_pds_uniquely_loses_protection_invariant` (7-method sweep: 6 methods protect, NonDesignated does not), `three_pds_invariant_all_qualify_when_postmark_within_period` (all 3 designated PDS providers qualify).

**Postmark-outside-period and improper-addressing gates dominate method protection.** Even the best delivery method (Registered Mail with proof) cannot save a filing that was mailed AFTER the deadline. Pinned by `postmark_outside_period_overrides_method_protection` (2-method sweep), `improperly_addressed_overrides_method_protection`.

**Electronic filings governed separately.** 26 CFR § 301.7502-1(d) — e-filed returns deemed filed on the date of electronic acknowledgment; § 7502 paper-mailing framework does not apply. Pinned by `electronic_filing_acknowledgment_within_period_timely`, `electronic_filing_acknowledgment_outside_period_untimely`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7502`. Twenty-five tests pin: **USPS Certified with receipt prima facie** (§ 7502(c)(2)); **USPS Certified without receipt falls back to ordinary**; **USPS Registered with proof prima facie** (§ 7502(c)(1)); **USPS Regular first-class ordinary timely with Anderson caveat** (9th Cir. 1992); **FedEx designated qualifies** (Notice 2016-30); **UPS designated qualifies**; **DHL Express designated qualifies**; **non-designated PDS does not qualify** (FedEx Ground / UPS Ground / FedEx Home Delivery); **postmark outside period not protected**; **improperly addressed envelope not protected**; **filing arrived before deadline no § 7502 needed**; **electronic filing acknowledgment within period timely**; **electronic filing acknowledgment outside period untimely**; **citation pins all subsections + 26 CFR § 301.7502-1 + Notice 2016-30 + Anderson v. United States**; **registered mail without proof falls back to ordinary**; **FedEx designated does not get prima facie status**; **UPS designated does not get prima facie status**; **three PDS invariant all qualify when postmark within period**; **non-designated PDS uniquely loses protection invariant** (7-method sweep: 6 protect, 1 not); **certified and registered uniquely get prima facie status invariant** (2-vs-4 method sweep); **postmark outside period overrides method protection** (2-method sweep); **improperly addressed overrides method protection**; **Anderson caveat appears only for USPS Regular** (3-method sweep); **before deadline path dominates method check** (2-method sweep); **FedEx note lists designated services** (Priority Overnight + International Priority/First/Economy).

`traderview-expense::section_7503` is the **IRC § 7503 Time for performance of acts where last day falls on Saturday, Sunday, or legal holiday** module — the weekend/holiday extension rule that stacks with `section_7502` timely-mailing rule + `section_6213(a)` Tax Court petition window + `section_6511` refund claim limitations. Trader-broadly-applicable timing rule because every tax deadline (return filing, payment, SNOD petition, refund claim, election statement) is subject to § 7503's automatic next-business-day extension when the deadline falls on a non-business day. Companion to `section_7502` (timely-mailing rule), `section_6213` (Tax Court petition deadline), `section_6511` (refund limitations), `section_6072` (return due dates), § 6151 (payment due dates).

**§ 7503 General rule** — when the last day prescribed under authority of the internal revenue laws for performing any act falls on **Saturday, Sunday, or a legal holiday**, the performance of such act shall be considered timely if it is performed on the **next succeeding day which is not a Saturday, Sunday, or a legal holiday**.

**Authorized extensions included** — any authorized extension of time shall be included in determining the last day for performance of any act.

**Legal holiday defined** — two clauses: (1) **legal holiday in the District of Columbia** (5 USC § 6103 federal holidays: New Year's Day + MLK Day + Washington's Birthday + Memorial Day + **Juneteenth (since 2021)** + Independence Day + Labor Day + Columbus Day + Veterans Day + Thanksgiving + Christmas Day); AND (2) for acts at offices outside DC but within an internal revenue district, the term also means a **statewide legal holiday in the State where such office is located**.

**DC-only holidays** — **Emancipation Day (April 16)** + Inauguration Day (January 20 every 4 years). DC Emancipation Day (Rev. Rul. 2015-13) regularly extends federal tax filing deadline by 1 business day when April 15 falls on weekend or April 16 itself on weekend — this is why federal income tax filing deadline is often April 17 or 18.

**Scope of application** — § 7503 applies to acts by **TAXPAYER** (return filing + payment + § 6213(a) Tax Court petition + § 6511 refund claim + elections) AND acts by **COMMISSIONER** (§ 6212 SNOD issuance + § 6303 notice and demand + § 6851 termination assessment notice).

**§ 7503 stacks with § 7502** — postmark on or before extended deadline counts as timely filing per § 7502(a) + § 7503 extension.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7503`. Twenty-three tests pin: **business day deadline no extension**; **Saturday deadline engages extension**; **Sunday deadline engages extension**; **federal legal holiday engages extension**; **DC-only holiday engages extension**; **state holiday at outside DC office engages extension**; **state holiday at DC office does NOT engage extension** (§ 7503 state-holiday clause limited to non-DC offices); **extension engaged but not performed on next business day fails** (§ 7503 only saves performance on the next non-weekend/non-holiday day); **authorized extension recognized**; **citation pins all authorities** (§ 7503 + 26 CFR § 301.7503-1 + Rev. Rul. 2015-13 + 5 USC § 6103 + DC Code § 28-2701 + § 7502 + § 6213(a) + § 6511 + § 6072); **note pins general rule weekend/holiday extension** (next succeeding day); **note pins authorized extension inclusion**; **note pins legal holiday definition two clauses** (DC + statewide); **note pins scope taxpayer and Commissioner acts** (§ 6213 + § 6511 + § 6212 + § 6303 + § 6851); **note pins Rev. Rul. 2015-13 Emancipation Day** (April 16 + April 15); **note pins 5 USC § 6103 federal holiday list** (Juneteenth 2021 + Thanksgiving + MLK Day); **note pins DC-only holidays** (Emancipation Day + Inauguration Day); **note pins § 7502 + § 7503 stacking** (timely-mailing rule interaction); **note pins cross-references collection constellation** (§ 6213(a) + § 6511 + § 6072 + § 6151 + § 6212); **day type truth table DC office** (6-cell sweep — business day + Sat + Sun + federal holiday + DC-only holiday + state holiday); **day type truth table outside DC office** (6-cell sweep — state holiday now engages); **state holiday only engages outside DC invariant**; **federal holiday engages regardless of office location invariant** (2-scope sweep).

`traderview-expense::section_7508` is the **IRC § 7508 time for performing certain acts postponed by reason of service in combat zone or contingency operation** module — trader-relevant for active-duty military traders and military spouses serving in designated combat zones (Executive Order designated), Secretary of Defense designated contingency operations outside the United States, or qualified hazardous duty areas (e.g., Sinai Peninsula). Procedural-companion to `section_7508a` (presidentially-declared disaster postponement — DIFFERENT qualifying event), `section_7421` (Anti-Injunction Act), `section_7422` (refund suit), `section_7426` (third-party wrongful levy), `section_7433` (civil damages for unauthorized collection), `section_7430` (litigation costs), `section_6212` / `section_6213` (deficiency procedures), and `section_6511` (refund-claim limitations).

**§ 7508(a) postponement formula**:

Total postponement = days of qualifying service + days of qualifying hospitalization + **180 days** bonus.

IRS DISREGARDS time during:
1. § 7508(a)(1)(A) — combat zone service (Executive Order designated)
2. § 7508(a)(1)(B) — Secretary of Defense designated contingency operation outside US
3. § 7508(c) — qualified hazardous duty area (Sinai Peninsula + other SoD designated)

Pinned by `combat_zone_365_days_plus_180` (365 + 180 = 545 days), `contingency_operation_postponement`, `qualified_hazardous_duty_area_postponement`, `not_applicable_no_postponement` (no qualifying service → no postponement), `zero_service_days_only_180_day_bonus` (no service days → just 180-day bonus), `bonus_180_days_always_180`, `service_type_truth_table` (4-cell sweep), and `note_pins_three_qualifying_categories`.

**§ 7508(a) hospitalization 5-year cap (1825 days)** — hospitalization INSIDE the United States is treated as qualified hospitalization only to the extent the period does not exceed 5 years. Hospitalization OUTSIDE the United States is NOT capped. Pinned by `hospitalization_outside_us_not_capped` (3000 days outside → not capped), `hospitalization_inside_us_capped_at_5_years` (3000 days inside → capped at 1825), `hospitalization_inside_us_at_1825_day_boundary_not_capped` (exactly 1825 = compliant), `hospitalization_inside_us_at_1826_day_capped` (1825 + 1 = capped), `hospitalization_zero_no_capping`, `small_hospitalization_inside_us_not_capped`, `hospitalization_cap_only_inside_us_invariant`, and `note_pins_hospitalization_5_year_cap`.

**§ 7508(b) military spouse extension** — same 180-day benefit applies to spouse of qualifying service member. Pinned by `military_spouse_eligible`, `military_spouse_truth_table` (2-cell sweep: ServiceMember + MilitarySpouse both qualify), and `note_pins_military_spouse_extension`.

**§ 7508(c) qualified hazardous duty area** — includes Sinai Peninsula of Egypt (if member entitled to special pay) plus other Secretary-of-Defense designated areas. Pinned by `note_pins_sinai_peninsula_qhda`.

**Postponed acts (per 26 CFR § 301.7508-1)** — filing returns + paying tax + filing amended returns + Tax Court petitions + § 6511 refund claims + § 6212 SNOD responses + § 6213 deficiency challenges. Pinned by `note_pins_postponed_acts`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7508`. Twenty-three tests pin: **combat zone 365 days plus 180** (545 day total); **contingency operation postponement**; **qualified hazardous duty area postponement**; **not applicable no postponement** (no qualifying service → no postponement); **military spouse eligible**; **hospitalization outside US not capped** (3000 days OK); **hospitalization inside US capped at 5 years** (3000 → 1825); **hospitalization inside US at 1825-day boundary not capped**; **hospitalization inside US at 1826-day capped**; **zero service days only 180-day bonus**; **citation pins all subsections** (a)(1)-(3) + (b)(1)-(2) + (c)(1)-(2) + § 301.7508-1 + Notice 2003-21 + Form 15109 + Pub. 3; **note pins three qualifying categories**; **note pins hospitalization 5-year cap** (1825 days); **note pins military spouse extension**; **note pins Sinai Peninsula QHDA**; **note pins postponed acts** (§ 6511 + § 6212 + § 6213 + Form 15109); **service type truth table** (4-cell sweep); **hospitalization cap only inside US invariant**; **bonus 180 days always 180**; **defensive overflow saturating**; **military spouse truth table** (2-cell); **hospitalization zero no capping**; **small hospitalization inside US not capped**.

`traderview-expense::section_7508a` is the **IRC § 7508A presidentially-declared disaster + significant fire + terroristic or military action deadline postponement** module — trader-relevant for any trader located in a federally-declared disaster area (CA wildfires + FL hurricanes + TX flooding + tornado disasters) who needs to extend filing + payment + amended-return + Tax Court petition + § 6511 refund-claim + § 6212 SNOD-response deadlines. Procedural-companion to `section_7421` (Anti-Injunction Act + § 7426 wrongful-levy exception), `section_7433` (civil damages for unauthorized collection), `section_7430` (litigation costs), `section_6212`/`section_6213` (deficiency procedures), and `section_6511` (refund-claim limitations).

**Two-tier postponement framework**:

| Subsection | Type | Maximum | Engagement |
|------------|------|---------|------------|
| § 7508A(a) | Discretionary disaster / fire | **1 year (365 days)** | Secretary determines, taxpayer in disaster area, Stafford-Act eligible area |
| § 7508A(b) | Discretionary terroristic / military | 1 year (365 days) | Secretary determines |
| § 7508A(c) | Special rules for pensions | N/A | IRA contributions + retirement-plan loan repayments |
| § 7508A(d) | **MANDATORY 60-day floor** | **60-day minimum** | Federally declared disaster with specified incident date declared after **December 20, 2019** |

Pinned by `federally_declared_disaster_postponement_available`, `postponement_basis_truth_table` (4-cell sweep: disaster/fire/terroristic all engage; NoQualifyingEvent does not), `significant_fire_postponement_available`, `terroristic_or_military_action_postponement_available`, `no_qualifying_event_no_postponement`, `not_in_disaster_area_no_postponement` (Stafford Act gate), and `taxpayer_outside_disaster_area_with_qualifying_event_no_postponement`.

**§ 7508A(d) mandatory 60-day floor — added by Taxpayer Certainty and Disaster Tax Relief Act of 2019 (Pub. L. 116-94 Div. Q § 205)** — applies only to federally declared disasters (not terroristic actions) with specified incident date declared AFTER December 20, 2019. Runs CONCURRENTLY with Secretary's discretionary postponement under (a)+(b) if Secretary period ≥ 60 days. If Secretary period < 60 days, 60-day floor controls. Pinned by `mandatory_60_day_floor_when_secretary_lt_60` (Secretary 30 days → 60 days controls), `mandatory_60_day_runs_concurrently_when_secretary_gte_60` (Secretary 90 days → 90 days controls), `mandatory_60_day_only_for_disaster_and_fire_invariant` (terroristic does NOT trigger 60-day floor), `mandatory_60_day_requires_post_dec_20_2019_invariant`, `pre_dec_20_2019_disaster_no_mandatory_60_day`, `pre_2019_amendment_no_mandatory_floor_when_secretary_lt_60`, `post_2019_amendment_60_day_floor_engages_when_secretary_zero`, `secretary_180_days_postponed_overrides_60_day_floor`, and `terroristic_action_does_not_engage_mandatory_60_day`.

**§ 7508A one-year maximum cap** — postponement period capped at one year (365 days). Pinned by `one_year_cap_engaged_at_365` (exactly 365 compliant), `over_one_year_requested_violates_cap`, `secretary_postponement_capped_at_365`, `defensive_at_max_365_boundary`, `defensive_at_366_days_requested_violates`, and `max_postponement_always_365`.

**Disaster area defined by Stafford Act + § 1033(h)(3)** — § 1033(h)(3) cross-reference defines "disaster area" as area eligible for federal assistance under Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act, 42 USC § 5121 et seq. Taxpayer must be located in the disaster area to qualify. Pinned by `not_in_disaster_area_no_postponement` and `note_pins_stafford_act`.

**Postponed acts include** (per 26 CFR § 301.7508A-1) — filing returns + paying tax + filing amended returns + Tax Court petitions + § 6511 refund claims + § 6212 SNOD responses + § 6213 deficiency challenges. Pinned by `note_pins_regs_postponed_acts`.

**Defensive boundary tests** — 0 days Secretary postponed → 60-day floor engages (post-2019 amendment); 366 days requested → over one-year cap violation; max_postponement_days always 365. Pinned by `defensive_zero_days_secretary_postponed_engages_60_day_floor`, `defensive_at_max_365_boundary`, and `defensive_at_366_days_requested_violates`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7508a`. Twenty-nine tests pin: **federally declared disaster postponement available**; **no qualifying event no postponement**; **not in disaster area no postponement** (Stafford Act); **significant fire postponement available**; **terroristic or military action postponement available**; **mandatory 60-day floor when Secretary < 60**; **mandatory 60-day runs concurrently when Secretary ≥ 60**; **pre-Dec 20 2019 disaster no mandatory 60-day**; **terroristic action does not engage mandatory 60-day**; **one-year cap engaged at 365**; **over one year requested violates cap**; **Secretary postponement capped at 365**; **citation pins all subsections** (a) + (b) + (c) + (d) + § 301.7508A-1 + Pub. L. 116-94 + 42 USC § 5121 + § 1033(h)(3); **note pins one year and 60-day** (December 20, 2019); **note pins Stafford Act** (§ 1033(h)(3) + 42 USC § 5121); **note pins regs postponed acts** (§ 6511 + § 6212 + § 6213); **note pins Pub. L. 116-94 Div. Q § 205**; **postponement basis truth table** (4-cell sweep); **mandatory 60-day only for disaster and fire invariant** (3-cell: disaster + fire engage; terroristic does not); **mandatory 60-day requires post-Dec 20 2019 invariant**; **defensive zero days Secretary postponed engages 60-day floor**; **defensive at max 365 boundary**; **defensive at 366 days requested violates**; **pre-2019 amendment no mandatory floor when Secretary < 60**; **post-2019 amendment 60-day floor engages when Secretary zero**; **Secretary 180 days postponed overrides 60-day floor**; **taxpayer outside disaster area with qualifying event no postponement**; **three failure modes stack when no event no area over cap**; **max postponement always 365**.

`traderview-expense::section_7521` is the **IRC § 7521 procedures involving taxpayer interviews** module — trader-critical procedural rights at IRS audit, collection, and examination interviews. Three statutory pathways: § 7521(a) recording rights (taxpayer + IRS), § 7521(b) explanation-of-rights requirement, § 7521(c) representation + interview-suspension right. Companion to `landlord_tenant_recording_consent` (federal Wiretap Act + state consent for non-IRS recordings), `section_7811` (Taxpayer Assistance Orders), `section_6330` (CDP for levies), and `section_6320` (CDP for liens).

**Four interview-type classifications**:

| Type | § 7521(a)(1) recording right | § 7521(b) explanation | § 7521(c) suspension right |
|------|----------------------------|------------------------|------------------------------|
| TaxDetermination | YES (advance request) | YES — § 7521(b)(1)(A) audit process | YES |
| Collection | YES (advance request) | YES — § 7521(b)(1)(B) collection process | YES |
| AdministrativeSummons | NO | NO | NO (§ 7602 summons exempt) |
| CriminalInvestigation | NO | NO | Jencks Act framework |

**§ 7521(a)(1) taxpayer recording right**: advance request + taxpayer's own equipment + taxpayer's own expense. Pinned by `tax_determination_taxpayer_recording_right_engaged_with_advance_request`, `collection_interview_taxpayer_recording_right_engaged`, `taxpayer_recording_not_engaged_without_advance_request`, `administrative_summons_no_recording_right`, `tax_determination_unique_recording_right_invariant` (4-type sweep), `taxpayer_recording_request_outside_determination_or_collection_no_right`.

**§ 7521(a)(2) IRS recording requires (i) advance notice AND (ii) reimbursable transcript on taxpayer request.** Pinned by `irs_recording_complies_with_advance_notice`, `irs_recording_fails_without_advance_notice`, `irs_recording_compliance_independent_of_taxpayer_recording_request`.

**§ 7521(b)(1) explanation of process and rights** — § 7521(b)(1)(A) audit process for tax determination + § 7521(b)(1)(B) collection process for collection. Failure may render subsequent statements suppressible. Pinned by `explanation_of_rights_satisfied_for_tax_determination` (§ 7521(b)(1)(A)), `explanation_of_rights_satisfied_for_collection` (§ 7521(b)(1)(B)), `explanation_of_rights_not_provided_violation_note` (suppressible warning), `explanation_of_rights_does_not_apply_to_administrative_summons`, `collection_explanation_subsection_b_referenced`, `tax_determination_explanation_subsection_a_referenced`.

**§ 7521(c) representation + interview-suspension right** — taxpayer may represent themselves OR be represented by attorney / CPA / enrolled agent / enrolled actuary / authorized representative via Form 2848 power of attorney (Treas. Reg. § 601.502). If taxpayer requests representation consultation DURING interview, IRS must SUSPEND regardless of prior answers.
