Pinned by `representation_engaged_with_power_of_attorney`, `suspension_required_when_taxpayer_requests_representation`, `suspension_satisfied_when_irs_suspends`, `failure_to_suspend_violation_note_warns_about_suppression`, `poa_alone_engages_representation_right`, `representation_consultation_request_alone_engages_representation_right`.

**§ 7521(c) administrative-summons exception bars suspension right.** Interview initiated by § 7602 administrative summons is exempt from the suspension rule. Pinned by `administrative_summons_bars_suspension_right`.

**§ 7521(c) unreasonable-delay bypass with Immediate Supervisor consent.** If IRS officer determines representative is responsible for unreasonable delay and obtains supervisor approval, suspension right is overridden. Pinned by `unreasonable_delay_bypass_with_supervisor_consent_no_suspension`, `suspension_required_truth_table_4_cells` (4-cell sweep over taxpayer-request × interview-type × bypass-approved).

**Criminal investigation interview warning surfaces Jencks Act framework.** § 7521 procedural rights do not apply in same manner; consult criminal defense counsel. Pinned by `criminal_investigation_warning_note_engaged`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7521`. Twenty-nine tests pin: **tax determination taxpayer recording right engaged with advance request** (§ 7521(a)(1)); **collection interview taxpayer recording right engaged**; **taxpayer recording not engaged without advance request**; **administrative summons no recording right**; **IRS recording complies with advance notice** (§ 7521(a)(2)); **IRS recording fails without advance notice**; **explanation of rights satisfied for tax determination** (§ 7521(b)(1)(A) audit process); **explanation of rights satisfied for collection** (§ 7521(b)(1)(B) collection process); **explanation of rights not provided violation note** (suppressible warning); **explanation of rights does not apply to administrative summons**; **representation engaged with power of attorney** (Form 2848 + § 601.502); **suspension required when taxpayer requests representation** (§ 7521(c)); **suspension satisfied when IRS suspends** (regardless of prior answers); **administrative summons bars suspension right** (§ 7521(c) carve-out); **unreasonable delay bypass with supervisor consent no suspension** (Immediate Supervisor approval); **criminal investigation warning note engaged** (Jencks Act); **citation pins all subsections + § 7602 + § 601.502 + cross-reference § 7811 + § 6330 + § 6320**; **four interview types routed correctly**; **tax determination unique recording right invariant** (4-type sweep: 2 engage + 2 do not); **suspension required truth table 4 cells** (taxpayer-request × type × bypass); **POA alone engages representation right**; **representation consultation request alone engages representation right**; **collection explanation subsection (B) referenced**; **tax determination explanation subsection (A) referenced**; **failure to suspend violation note warns about suppression**; **no representation request no suspension required**; **taxpayer recording request outside determination or collection no right** (Summons + Criminal both barred); **IRS recording compliance independent of taxpayer recording request**; **full compliance path all rights satisfied** (all five flags green simultaneously).

`traderview-expense::section_7522` is the **IRC § 7522 Content of tax due, deficiency, and other notices** module — added by Taxpayer Bill of Rights of 1988 (TBOR 1, Pub. L. 100-647 § 6233) to require IRS notices to describe the BASIS for, and identify the AMOUNTS of, every component of liability. Trader-procedural-critical because § 7522 is the taxpayer's statutory entitlement to know what the IRS is asserting and why — covering § 6303 notice and demand (CP14), § 6212 SNOD (Letter 3171/5071C 90-day letter), CP2000 Automated Underreporter notices generated from 1099-B + 1099-K + K-1 matching, and the first 30-day Appeals-eligible proposed-deficiency letter (Letter 525). Companion to `section_6201` (assessment authority), `section_6203` (method of assessment), `section_6212` (SNOD), `section_6303` (notice and demand), `section_7491` (burden of proof).

**§ 7522(a) General rule** — any notice to which this section applies shall **describe the basis for, and identify the amounts (if any) of**, any (1) tax due, (2) interest, (3) additional amounts, (4) additions to the tax, and (5) assessable penalties included in such notice. **An inadequate description shall NOT INVALIDATE such notice** — statutory safe harbor; § 7522(a) is a notice-content DUTY but inadequate notice is curable rather than voidable.

**§ 7522(b) Notices to which section applies** — three categories: (1) **§ 7522(b)(1)** any tax due notice or deficiency notice described in **§ 6155, § 6212, or § 6303** (CP14 notice and demand, Letter 1058 Final Notice of Intent to Levy, Letter 3171/5071C SNOD); (2) **§ 7522(b)(2)** any notice generated out of any **information return matching program** (CP2000 Automated Underreporter — matches 1099-B broker reporting + 1099-K payment-card processor reporting + K-1 partnership reporting against tax return; response deadline 30 days from notice date / 60 days if taxpayer outside US); (3) **§ 7522(b)(3)** the first letter of proposed deficiency which allows the taxpayer an opportunity for administrative review in the **IRS Independent Office of Appeals** (Letter 525 30-day letter; Taxpayer First Act of 2019 § 1001 redesignated as "Independent Office of Appeals").

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7522`. Twenty-eight tests pin: **fully compliant SNOD passes**; **§ 6303 notice covered**; **§ 6155 payment notice covered**; **CP2000 information matching covered**; **Letter 525 30-day letter covered**; **not covered notice section not engaged**; **missing basis description violation with safe harbor**; **missing tax amount violation**; **missing interest amount violation**; **missing additional amounts violation**; **missing additions to tax violation**; **missing assessable penalties violation**; **safe harbor preserves notice validity** (inadequate description does NOT invalidate); **no liability component no amount required**; **citation pins all authorities** (§ 7522 + TBOR 1 + Pub. L. 100-647 § 6233 + Taxpayer First Act of 2019 § 1001 + 26 CFR § 301.7522-1 + IRM 4.19.3 CP2000 + IRM 4.10.7 Letter 525 + § 6155 + § 6212 + § 6303 + § 7491 + IRS Publication 1); **note pins general rule describe basis and amounts**; **note pins safe harbor** (NOT INVALIDATE + curable); **note pins § 7522(b)(1) covered notices** (§ 6155 + § 6212 + § 6303 + CP14 + Letter 1058); **note pins § 7522(b)(2) CP2000 with third party info returns** (1099-B + 1099-K + K-1 + 30 days + 60 days outside US); **note pins § 7522(b)(3) Letter 525 with Independent Office of Appeals** (Taxpayer First Act of 2019 § 1001); **note pins TBOR 1 origin** (Pub. L. 100-647 § 6233 + IRS Publication 1); **note pins content disclosure layer over collection constellation** (§ 6201 + § 6203 + § 6212 + § 6303 + § 6213(a)); **multiple amount failures stack** (3 violations); **notice type truth table 6 cells** (5 covered + 1 not covered); **all five amounts truth table when liability included**; **inadequate description always engages safe harbor invariant**; **fully compliant notice does not engage safe harbor invariant**; **not covered notice returns compliant without engaging section invariant**.

`traderview-expense::section_7525` is the **IRC § 7525 federally authorized tax practitioner (FATP) confidentiality privilege** module — when do communications between a taxpayer and a CPA / EA / attorney / enrolled actuary / enrolled retirement plan agent receive the same common-law confidentiality protection that applies between taxpayer and attorney? Trader-relevant for protecting practitioner communications on M2M elections (§ 475(f)), straddle (§ 1092) advice, § 1256 60/40 reporting, § 1091 wash sale planning, qualified-trader-status disputes, and international (§ 988, § 1297, § 6038D) advice. Companion to `section_7521` (interview procedure), `section_7811` (Taxpayer Assistance Orders), and `section_7434` (civil-fraud victim damages).

**Six conjunctive elements required for privilege**:

| Element | Source | Failure mode |
|---------|--------|--------------|
| FATP qualification | § 7525(a)(2) + 31 USC § 330 | Non-FATP individual (bookkeeper, paralegal, financial planner without Circular 230 authorization) |
| Noncriminal context | § 7525(a)(3)(A) | Criminal tax matter categorically EXCLUDED (grand jury, indictment, IRS-CI referral) |
| Federal tax matter | § 7525(a)(3)(A) | State or local tax matter (federal-only privilege scope) |
| Tax advice (not return prep) | United States v. Frederick, 182 F.3d 496 (7th Cir. 1999) | Return preparation work (ministerial, not advice) |
| Not written shelter promotion | § 7525(b) + § 6662(d)(2)(C)(ii) | Written FATP communication promoting tax shelter participation |
| Would be privileged with attorney | § 7525(a)(1) gating clause | Communication would not satisfy attorney-client privilege test |

**§ 7525(a)(1) extension clause** — same common-law protections that apply between taxpayer + attorney apply between taxpayer + FATP "to the extent the communication would be considered a privileged communication if it were between a taxpayer and an attorney." Pinned by `note_pins_attorney_client_extension` and `would_not_be_attorney_privileged_voids`.

**§ 7525(a)(3)(A) noncriminal-only limitation** — privilege assertable ONLY in (i) noncriminal tax matter before IRS or (ii) noncriminal federal court tax proceeding brought by or against the United States. Criminal tax matters (grand jury, indictment, IRS-CI referral) categorically void privilege regardless of FATP qualification or communication type. Pinned by `criminal_tax_matter_categorically_voids_privilege` and `criminal_uniquely_categorical_invariant` (criminal voids `noncriminal_context`; state/local voids `federal_tax_matter` instead).

**§ 7525(b) tax-shelter-promotion exception — WRITTEN ONLY**. The exception applies to written communications between FATP and a person in connection with the promotion of direct or indirect participation in any tax shelter as defined in § 6662(d)(2)(C)(ii). Oral tax-shelter advice is NOT swept in by § 7525(b); only written promotional communications. Pinned by `written_tax_shelter_promotion_excluded`, `shelter_exception_uniquely_written_only_invariant` (oral advice still privileged; written shelter promo not), and `written_tax_advice_privileged_distinguishes_from_shelter_promotion`.

**United States v. Frederick return-prep exclusion** — judicial limitation: § 7525 does NOT cover tax-return preparation work because it is ministerial compilation, not legal advice. Distinguishes from oral or written tax-planning advice. Pinned by `return_preparation_work_excluded`, `frederick_citation_pinned_for_return_prep_failure`, and `return_prep_distinguished_from_tax_advice_invariant`.

**FATP qualification under 31 USC § 330 (Circular 230)** — covers CPAs, enrolled agents, attorneys admitted to practice before IRS, enrolled actuaries, and enrolled retirement plan agents. Excludes non-FATP individuals (bookkeepers, paralegals, financial planners without § 330 authorization). Pinned by `fatp_practitioner_types_all_qualify` (5-type sweep), `non_fatp_individual_voids_privilege`, `enrolled_actuary_privileged`, and `enrolled_retirement_plan_agent_privileged`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7525`. Twenty-six tests pin: **CPA noncriminal oral advice privileged** (full compliance path); **EA noncriminal written advice privileged**; **attorney noncriminal federal court privileged**; **criminal tax matter categorically voids privilege** (§ 7525(a)(3)(A)); **state or local tax voids privilege** (federal-only scope); **return preparation work excluded** (Frederick); **written tax shelter promotion excluded** (§ 7525(b) + § 6662(d)(2)(C)(ii)); **non-FATP individual voids privilege** (31 USC § 330); **would not be attorney privileged voids** (§ 7525(a)(1) gating); **multiple disqualifiers stack** (4 failures simultaneous); **FATP practitioner types all qualify** (5-type sweep); **enrolled actuary privileged**; **enrolled retirement plan agent privileged**; **citation pins all relevant subsections** (a)(1) + (a)(2) + (a)(3)(A) + (b) + 31 USC § 330; **note pins attorney-client extension**; **note pins noncriminal-only limitation**; **note pins tax shelter promotion exception**; **proceeding context truth table** (4-cell sweep); **communication type truth table** (4-cell sweep); **criminal uniquely categorical invariant** (criminal voids noncriminal_context; state/local voids federal_tax_matter); **six conjunctive elements all required**; **shelter exception uniquely written only invariant** (oral advice still privileged; written shelter promo not); **written tax advice privileged distinguishes from shelter promotion**; **return prep distinguished from tax advice invariant**; **Frederick citation pinned for return prep failure**; **three failure reasons stack for state local non-FATP return prep**.

`traderview-expense::section_7421` is the **IRC § 7421 Anti-Injunction Act (AIA)** module — when may a trader-taxpayer obtain a TRO or preliminary injunction against an IRS assessment / collection / levy? Default answer = **NEVER**. § 7421(a) general bar: "no suit for the purpose of restraining the assessment or collection of any tax shall be maintained in any court by any person." Three exception pathways (11 statutory + Enochs judicial 2-prong + CIC Services regulatory pre-enforcement). Trader-procedural-companion to `section_7521` (interview procedure), `section_7525` (FATP privilege), `section_7811` (Taxpayer Assistance Orders), and `section_7430` (litigation costs).

**Three exception pathways**:

| Pathway | Source | Trigger |
|---------|--------|---------|
| Statutory exception | § 7421(a) enumerated list | One of 11 sections engages |
| Enochs judicial | Enochs v. Williams Packing, 370 U.S. 1 (1962) | Both prongs satisfied conjunctively |
| CIC Services carve-out | CIC Services v. IRS, 593 U.S. 209 (2021) | Pre-enforcement challenge to reporting requirement / regulation |

**§ 7421(a) eleven statutory exceptions** — verbatim cross-references: §§ 6015(e) (innocent spouse) + 6212(a) + (c) (SNOD) + 6213(a) (pre-assessment Tax Court restraint) + 6232(c) (partnership-level adjustment) + 6330(e)(1) (CDP collection action suspension) + 6331(i) (uniformed-services bond levy) + 6672(c) (TFRP CDP) + 6694(c) (preparer-penalty CDP) + 7426(a) + (b)(1) (third-party wrongful levy) + 7429(b) (jeopardy assessment review) + 7436 (employment-status determination review). Pinned by `statutory_exception_truth_table` (11-exception sweep), `section_6213a_exception_permits_suit`, `section_6330e1_cdp_exception_permits_suit`, `section_7426_wrongful_levy_exception_permits_suit`, `section_7429b_jeopardy_assessment_permits_suit`, `section_6015e_innocent_spouse_permits_suit`, `section_6232c_partnership_adjustment_permits_suit`, and `section_7436_employment_status_permits_suit`.

**Enochs v. Williams Packing two-prong judicial test** — both required conjunctively:
- **Prong 1**: under no circumstances could the Government ultimately prevail
- **Prong 2**: equity jurisdiction otherwise exists (irreparable harm + legal remedy inadequate)

Either prong failing barres the suit. Pinned by `enochs_both_prongs_permits_suit`, `enochs_only_prong_1_barred`, `enochs_only_prong_2_barred`, and `enochs_2x2_truth_table` (4-cell sweep: TT permits, TF / FT / FF all barred).

**CIC Services v. IRS regulatory pre-enforcement carve-out** — 593 U.S. 209 (2021) held that a suit challenging the lawfulness of an IRS reporting requirement is NOT a suit to restrain assessment or collection within the meaning of § 7421(a). Distinction: (1) suits attacking assessment/collection itself versus (2) suits challenging regulatory information-reporting obligations. Pinned by `cic_services_pre_enforcement_permits_suit`, `cic_services_uniquely_permits_pre_enforcement_invariant`, and `note_pins_cic_services_distinction`.

**Default trader procedural pathway** — pay disputed tax + file refund claim under § 6402/§ 7422 + file refund suit. Refund suit after payment is NOT a § 7421(a) bar. Pinned by `refund_after_payment_not_barred`, `refund_path_uniquely_distinct_from_aia_invariant`, and `note_pins_refund_suit_pathway`.

**Three exception pathways are independent** — each can engage independently of the others. Statutory engages without Enochs/CIC; Enochs engages without statutory; CIC engages without either. Pinned by `three_exception_pathways_independent` (verifies each pathway sets its own flag without triggering the others).

**Bare assessment / collection suit stacks 3 failure reasons** — § 7421(a) general bar + Enochs prong 1 not satisfied + Enochs prong 2 not satisfied. Pinned by `multiple_failure_reasons_stack_for_bare_assessment_suit` (exactly 3 reasons), and `statutory_exception_short_circuits_enochs_failure_reasons` (statutory exception engages → 0 failure reasons because suit permitted).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7421`. Twenty-nine tests pin: **restrain assessment no exception barred**; **restrain collection no exception barred**; **§ 6213(a) exception permits suit**; **§ 6330(e)(1) CDP exception permits suit**; **§ 7426 wrongful levy exception permits suit**; **§ 7429(b) jeopardy assessment permits suit**; **§ 6015(e) innocent spouse permits suit**; **Enochs both prongs permits suit**; **Enochs only prong 1 barred**; **Enochs only prong 2 barred**; **CIC Services pre-enforcement permits suit**; **refund after payment not barred**; **citation pins Enochs and CIC Services**; **note pins 11 statutory exceptions**; **note pins Enochs two prong conjunction**; **note pins CIC Services distinction**; **note pins refund suit pathway** (§ 7422); **statutory exception truth table** (11-exception sweep); **suit purpose truth table** (4-cell sweep); **Enochs 2x2 truth table** (4-cell sweep); **Enochs only engages for assessment or collection** (not pre-enforcement); **CIC Services uniquely permits pre-enforcement invariant**; **refund path uniquely distinct from AIA invariant** (4-purpose sweep); **three exception pathways independent**; **multiple failure reasons stack for bare assessment suit** (3 reasons); **statutory exception short-circuits Enochs failure reasons**; **assessment and collection engage AIA bar invariant** (4-purpose sweep); **§ 6232(c) partnership adjustment permits suit**; **§ 7436 employment status permits suit**.

`traderview-expense::section_7422` is the **IRC § 7422 civil actions for refund** module — completes the refund-procedure constellation with `section_7421` (Anti-Injunction Act) + `section_7508A` (disaster postponement of § 6511 refund-claim deadlines) + `section_7430` (litigation costs against IRS) + `section_6511` (refund-claim limitations) + `section_6532` (refund-suit limitations) + `section_7429` (jeopardy review). Trader-relevant when trader has paid tax and wants to sue for refund (rather than file Tax Court petition pre-payment) — refund suits are the default pathway when taxpayer missed § 6213 90-day Tax Court window OR affirmatively chose to pay first to stop § 6601 interest accrual OR issue not amenable to Tax Court (e.g., refund of penalty already paid, recovery of erroneous offset).

**Four pre-suit requirements**:

| Requirement | Source | Trigger |
|-------------|--------|---------|
| 1. **Flora full-payment rule** | Flora v. United States, 362 U.S. 145 (1960) | Taxpayer must have FULLY PAID assessment before suing in district court / Court of Federal Claims |
| 2. Administrative claim filed | § 7422(a) + § 6511 | Form 843 / 1040-X filed within later of 3 years after return filing or 2 years after payment |
| 3. 6-month wait period | § 6532(a) | 180 days from administrative claim filing (unless IRS issues disallowance sooner) |
| 4. 2-year filing window | § 6532(a) | Suit filed within 2 years (730 days) after IRS mails notice of disallowance |

Pinned by `fully_compliant_suit_maintainable`, `flora_partial_payment_violates` (Flora rule blocks suit), `no_administrative_claim_violates`, `under_6_month_wait_violates`, `at_180_day_boundary_compliant`, `at_730_day_two_year_boundary_compliant`, `at_731_days_two_year_window_violates`, `at_179_days_violates_at_180_compliant_precision`, `at_730_compliant_at_731_violates_precision`, `irs_disallowance_short_circuits_6_month_wait` (IRS disallowance permits earlier filing), `six_month_wait_truth_table` (7-cell sweep), and `two_year_window_truth_table` (7-cell sweep).

**Flora full-payment rule** — Flora v. United States, 362 U.S. 145 (1960) holds that taxpayer must have FULLY PAID the assessment before suing for refund in district court or Court of Federal Claims (limited exceptions only). Distinguishes refund jurisdiction from Tax Court pre-payment jurisdiction. Applies uniformly to both district court and Court of Federal Claims. Pinned by `flora_partial_payment_violates`, `flora_rule_uniquely_blocks_district_court_invariant`, and `note_pins_flora_full_payment_rule`.

**§ 7422(e) concurrent jurisdiction limitation** — if Secretary mails notice of deficiency BEFORE hearing in district court / Court of Federal Claims, proceedings must be STAYED during 90-day Tax Court petition window + 60 days additional. If taxpayer files Tax Court petition, district court / Court of Federal Claims LOSES jurisdiction to extent acquired by Tax Court. Pinned by `deficiency_notice_before_hearing_stays_proceedings` (deficiency notice + no Tax Court petition → stay), `tax_court_petition_filed_transfers_jurisdiction` (deficiency notice + Tax Court petition → jurisdiction transfer), `no_deficiency_notice_no_7422e_engagement`, `section_7422e_truth_table` (4-cell sweep: 00 → no engagement; 01 → no engagement; 10 → stay; 11 → transfer), `jurisdiction_transfer_uniquely_engages_when_both_conditions_invariant`, and `note_pins_section_7422e_jurisdiction_transfer`.

**Jurisdiction**: district court (28 USC § 1346(a)(1)) concurrent with United States Court of Federal Claims (28 USC § 1491). Pinned by `court_of_federal_claims_routing` and `note_pins_district_court_court_of_federal_claims_concurrent_jurisdiction`.

**Four-failure stack on bare suit** — Flora partial payment + no administrative claim + 2-year window expired + § 7422(e) stay = 4 simultaneous failures. Pinned by `four_failures_stack_for_bare_suit`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7422`. Twenty-six tests pin: **fully compliant suit maintainable**; **Flora partial payment violates** (362 U.S. 145); **no administrative claim violates** (§ 7422(a) + § 6511); **under 6-month wait violates** (179 days); **at 180-day boundary compliant**; **IRS disallowance short-circuits 6-month wait**; **at 730-day two-year boundary compliant**; **at 731 days two-year window violates**; **deficiency notice before hearing stays proceedings** (§ 7422(e)); **Tax Court petition filed transfers jurisdiction** (§ 7422(e)); **no deficiency notice no § 7422(e) engagement**; **Court of Federal Claims routing**; **citation pins all subsections** (a)-(e) + § 6511 + § 6532(a) + Flora + 28 USC § 1346(a)(1) + 28 USC § 1491 + Pub. 556 + IRM 25.6.2; **note pins administrative claim requirement** (3 years / 2 years + Form 843); **note pins Flora full-payment rule** (362 U.S. 145 (1960)); **note pins 6-month + 2-year § 6532(a)**; **note pins § 7422(e) jurisdiction transfer** (60 days); **note pins district court + Court of Federal Claims concurrent jurisdiction** (28 USC § 1346(a)(1) + § 1491 + AIA-exception pathway); **four failures stack for bare suit**; **six-month wait truth table** (7-cell sweep); **two-year window truth table** (7-cell sweep); **§ 7422(e) truth table** (4-cell sweep); **Flora rule uniquely blocks district court invariant** (district + Court of Federal Claims both blocked); **at 179 days violates at 180 compliant precision**; **at 730 compliant at 731 violates precision**; **jurisdiction transfer uniquely engages when both conditions invariant**.

`traderview-expense::section_7426` is the **IRC § 7426 civil actions by persons other than taxpayers (third-party wrongful levy + surplus proceeds + substituted sales proceeds + § 7426(h) unauthorized collection damages)** module — when IRS levies on property that BELONGS to someone other than the assessed taxpayer (joint accounts + nominee accounts + community-property third-party interests + trader's co-owner / lender / lien-holder rights in seized rental property), what civil remedy is available to the third party? Procedural-companion to `section_7421` (Anti-Injunction Act — § 7426(a)+(b)(1) is a statutory exception), `section_7433` (parallel civil-damages regime for taxpayer), `section_7430` (litigation costs), and `section_6334` (property exempt from levy).

**Four action types under § 7426**:

| Action | Source | Required elements |
|--------|--------|--------------------|
| Wrongful levy | § 7426(a)(1) | Claimant not assessed taxpayer + interest/lien on property + 2-year (or 9-month pre-2017) SOL |
| Surplus proceeds | § 7426(a)(2) | Claimant not assessed taxpayer + interest/lien JUNIOR to United States + entitled to excess sale proceeds |
| Substituted sales proceeds | § 7426(a)(3) | Claimant not assessed taxpayer + interest/lien on substituted fund pursuant to agreement |
| Unauthorized collection damages | § 7426(h) | IRS reckless / intentional / negligent disregard of IRC provision |

Pinned by `action_type_truth_table`, `wrongful_levy_within_2_year_sol_maintainable`, `surplus_proceeds_compliant`, `surplus_proceeds_no_junior_interest_violates`, `surplus_proceeds_uniquely_requires_junior_interest_invariant`, `substituted_sales_proceeds_compliant`, `unauthorized_collection_damages_compliant`, `unauthorized_collection_damages_not_applicable_violates`, and `taxpayer_assessed_blocked_from_action` (§ 7426 actions limited to persons OTHER than taxpayer).

**§ 7426(c) TCJA SOL extension — 2 years from 9 months** (Pub. L. 115-97 § 11071, effective December 22, 2017). Pre-amendment levies retain 9-month (274-day) SOL; post-amendment levies get 2-year (730-day) SOL. Pinned by `wrongful_levy_at_730_day_boundary_compliant` (exactly 730 days), `wrongful_levy_731_days_violates_sol`, `pre_2017_tcja_9_month_sol`, `pre_2017_tcja_at_274_day_boundary_compliant`, `wrongful_levy_pre_2017_275_days_violates`, `sol_truth_table_2017_vs_pre_2017`, `sol_2_year_uniquely_for_post_2017_invariant`, and `sol_only_applies_to_wrongful_levy_not_other_actions` (surplus + substituted have no statutory SOL clock).

**§ 7426(h) civil damages — mirror of § 7433 framework**:

| Culpability | Cap (cents) | Cap (dollars) |
|-------------|-------------|---------------|
| Negligence | $10,000,000,000 | **$100,000** |
| Reckless / Intentional | $100,000,000,000 | **$1,000,000** |

Damages = min(cap, actual + costs). Pinned by `unauthorized_collection_damages_negligence_cap_100k`, `unauthorized_collection_damages_reckless_cap_1m`, `damages_cap_negligence_uniquely_lower_invariant` (reckless 10× negligence), `unauthorized_collection_damages_costs_only_at_zero_actual` (costs alone when actual = 0), `defensive_negative_damages_clamped`, `defensive_negative_costs_clamped`, and `defensive_overflow_damages_saturating` (i64::MAX × 2 → caps at $1M).

**§ 7421(a) Anti-Injunction Act exception engaged for three property actions** — § 7426(a)(1) + (a)(2) + (a)(3) are statutorily excepted from AIA bar on suits to restrain assessment or collection. § 7426(h) damages action does NOT engage AIA exception (different procedural posture). Pinned by `aia_exception_engaged_for_three_property_actions` (3-action sweep) and `aia_exception_not_engaged_for_unauthorized_collection_damages`.

**Wrongful levy three-failure stack** — taxpayer-assessed + no interest/lien + SOL-expired = 3 simultaneous failures. Pinned by `three_failures_stack_for_wrongful_levy` (exactly 3 reasons).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7426`. Thirty-three tests pin: **wrongful levy within 2-year SOL maintainable**; **wrongful levy at 730-day boundary compliant**; **wrongful levy 731 days violates SOL**; **pre-2017 TCJA 9-month SOL** (274-day); **pre-2017 TCJA at 274-day boundary compliant**; **taxpayer-assessed blocked from action**; **no interest or lien blocks wrongful levy**; **surplus proceeds compliant**; **surplus proceeds no junior interest violates** (§ 7426(a)(2)); **substituted sales proceeds compliant**; **unauthorized collection damages compliant**; **unauthorized collection damages negligence cap $100K**; **unauthorized collection damages reckless cap $1M**; **unauthorized collection damages not applicable violates** (§ 7426(h)); **AIA exception engaged for three property actions** (3-action sweep); **AIA exception not engaged for unauthorized collection damages**; **SOL truth table 2017 vs pre-2017**; **SOL 2-year uniquely for post-2017 invariant**; **SOL only applies to wrongful levy not other actions**; **action type truth table** (4-cell sweep); **citation pins all subsections** (a)(1)+(a)(2)+(a)(3)+(b)(1)+(b)(2)+(c)+(g)+(h) + Pub. L. 115-97 § 11071 + IRS Pub. 4528; **note pins three actions**; **note pins TCJA SOL extension** (December 22, 2017 + 9 months → 2 years); **note pins § 7426(h) civil damages caps** ($1M + $100K + § 7433); **note pins AIA exception and Pub. 4528**; **defensive negative damages clamped**; **defensive negative costs clamped**; **defensive overflow damages saturating** (i64::MAX); **three failures stack for wrongful levy**; **surplus proceeds uniquely requires junior interest invariant**; **wrongful levy pre-2017 275 days violates** (precision boundary); **unauthorized collection damages costs only at zero actual**; **damages cap negligence uniquely lower invariant** (10× ratio).

`traderview-expense::section_7429` is the **IRC § 7429 review of jeopardy levy or assessment procedures** module — trader-relevant when IRS believes collection is in jeopardy (taxpayer planning to flee, conceal assets, dispose of assets to evade collection) and invokes jeopardy assessment under § 6861 (income/estate/gift tax) + § 6862 (other taxes) plus immediate collection action. Trader-landlords face § 7429 review when IRS makes jeopardy assessment seizing trading accounts + rental property revenue + bank accounts. Procedural-companion to `section_7421` (Anti-Injunction Act — § 7429(b) is one of 11 statutory exceptions), `section_7426` (third-party wrongful levy), `section_7433` (civil damages for unauthorized collection), `section_7430` (litigation costs), `section_6321`/`section_6323`/`section_6325` (lien constellation), and `section_6334` (exempt property).

**§ 7429(a) administrative review three-step framework**:

| Step | Source | Deadline |
|------|--------|----------|
| 1. IRS provides written statement | § 7429(a)(1) | Within 5 days of jeopardy assessment/levy |
| 2. Taxpayer requests administrative review | § 7429(a)(2) | Within 30 days after written statement furnished |
| 3. IRS responds | § 7429(a)(3) | Within 15 calendar days |

Pinned by `admin_within_30_days_timely`, `admin_at_30_day_boundary_timely` (exactly 30 days compliant), `admin_at_31_days_violates`, `admin_at_zero_days_compliant`, `administrative_truth_table_30_day_boundary` (5-cell sweep: 0/15/30 compliant; 31/60 violate), and `note_pins_administrative_5_30_15_day_framework`.

**§ 7429(b) judicial review framework**:

| Step | Source | Deadline / Trigger |
|------|--------|---------------------|
| Filing window | § 7429(b)(1) | Within 90 days from earlier of (a) district director's notice of determination or (b) **16th day** after administrative review request |
| Jurisdiction | § 7429(b)(2) | **District court has EXCLUSIVE jurisdiction** (no Tax Court alternative) |
| Court determination | § 7429(b)(3) | **20 calendar days** to determine (1) assessment is REASONABLE and (2) amount is APPROPRIATE |
| Extension | § 7429(b)(3) | Up to **40 additional calendar days** for reasonable grounds (**combined 60-day maximum**) |

Pinned by `judicial_within_90_days_timely`, `judicial_at_90_day_boundary_timely` (exactly 90 days compliant), `judicial_91_days_violates`, `judicial_unavailable_without_notification_or_16_days`, `judicial_available_at_16_day_administrative_trigger`, `judicial_path_available_when_determination_notified`, `judicial_path_available_when_administrative_request_at_16th_day`, `judicial_path_unavailable_at_15th_day_without_notification` (precision boundary at 15th day fails; 16th day permits), `court_determination_within_20_days_timely`, `court_determination_at_20_day_boundary_timely`, `court_determination_21_days_without_extension_violates`, `court_extension_up_to_40_additional_days` (55 days OK with full extension), `court_extension_at_60_day_combined_boundary_timely` (exactly 60 days), `court_extension_61_days_violates_combined_cap`, `court_extension_capped_at_40_additional_days` (50-day request capped at 40), `court_extension_30_day_partial_extension`, `court_window_20_day_default_no_extension`, `court_window_60_day_max_with_full_extension`, `court_determination_truth_table_with_and_without_extension` (5-cell sweep), `note_pins_judicial_90_day_16_day_trigger`, and `note_pins_district_court_exclusive_jurisdiction_20_40_court_window`.

**§ 7421(a)(11) Anti-Injunction Act exception** — § 7429(b) judicial review is one of 11 enumerated statutory exceptions to AIA bar on suits to restrain assessment or collection. Without this exception, jeopardy-assessment review would be categorically barred. Pinned by `aia_exception_engaged_for_judicial_review` and `note_pins_aia_exception`.

**Multiple-failure stack** — judicial review past 90-day filing window + court determination past 20-day window = 2 simultaneous failures. Pinned by `three_failures_stack_for_judicial_review`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7429`. Thirty-two tests pin: **admin within 30 days timely**; **admin at 30-day boundary timely**; **admin at 31 days violates**; **admin at zero days compliant**; **judicial within 90 days timely**; **judicial at 90-day boundary timely**; **judicial 91 days violates**; **judicial unavailable without notification or 16 days**; **judicial available at 16-day administrative trigger**; **judicial path available when determination notified**; **judicial path available when administrative request at 16th day**; **judicial path unavailable at 15th day without notification**; **court determination within 20 days timely**; **court determination at 20-day boundary timely**; **court determination 21 days without extension violates**; **court extension up to 40 additional days**; **court extension at 60-day combined boundary timely**; **court extension 61 days violates combined cap**; **court extension capped at 40 additional days**; **court extension 30-day partial extension**; **court window 20-day default no extension**; **court window 60-day max with full extension**; **citation pins all subsections** (a)(1)-(3) + (b)(1)-(3) + (c) + § 301.7429-3 + § 6861 + § 6862 + § 7421(a)(11) + IRM 5.1.4 + IRM 5.17.15 + IRM 8.24.2; **note pins administrative 5/30/15 day framework**; **note pins judicial 90-day + 16-day trigger**; **note pins district court exclusive jurisdiction + 20/40 court window**; **note pins AIA exception** (§ 7421(a)(11) + 11 enumerated); **review stage truth table**; **administrative truth table 30-day boundary** (5-cell sweep); **court determination truth table with and without extension** (5-cell sweep); **AIA exception engaged for judicial review**; **three failures stack for judicial review**.

`traderview-expense::section_7430` is the **IRC § 7430 awarding of costs and certain fees against the IRS** module — when a taxpayer prevails in administrative or judicial proceedings against the IRS, under what conditions may the taxpayer recover litigation costs and attorney fees? Distinct from `section_6402` (refund offsets), `section_6611` (interest on overpayments), and `section_6213` (Tax Court petition timing). Trader-relevant when prevailing against IRS in Tax Court or refund litigation.

**Three gating tests** all must pass for fee award:

| Gate | Authority | Cap |
|------|-----------|-----|
| § 7430(c)(4)(D) net worth | 28 U.S.C. § 2412(d)(2)(B) | Individual ≤ $2M; business entity ≤ $7M |
| § 7430(c)(4)(D) size | 28 U.S.C. § 2412(d)(2)(B) | ≤ 500 employees at filing |
| § 7430(b)(1) exhaustion | § 7430(b)(1) | Administrative remedies within IRS must be exhausted |

**Two prevailing-party paths** (§ 7430(c)(4)(A) vs § 7430(c)(7)):

| Path | Trigger |
|------|---------|
| § 7430(c)(4)(A) Substantively prevailed | Taxpayer substantially prevailed on amount in controversy OR most significant issue AND IRS position NOT substantially justified |
| § 7430(c)(7) Qualified Offer Rule | Taxpayer made § 7430(c)(7) QO during administrative period AND QO liability ≥ eventual judgment liability |

**§ 7430(c)(1)(B)(iii) hourly rate cap (Rev. Proc. 2025-32)**: $260/hour for fees incurred in 2026 (up from $250 in 2025 and $230 in 2024). Base rate $125/hour (1996) inflation-adjusted.

**The Qualified Offer Rule bypasses the substantial-justification defense.** When the QO is engaged (QO ≥ judgment), the IRS cannot defeat prevailing party status by arguing its position was substantially justified — Congress intended QOs to incentivize early IRS settlement. Pinned by `qualified_offer_rule_bypasses_substantial_justification_defense` (IRS substantial justification + QO engaged → taxpayer still prevailing under QO rule), `qualified_offer_engages_when_qo_liability_geq_judgment`, `qualified_offer_does_not_engage_when_qo_liability_less_than_judgment`, `qualified_offer_exactly_at_judgment_engages_rule` (≥ boundary), `qualified_offer_one_cent_below_judgment_does_not_engage` (< boundary).

**Business entity net worth ceiling is 3.5× higher than individual.** Individual taxpayers face a $2M net worth ceiling; unincorporated businesses, corporations, and partnerships face a $7M ceiling — both measured at the time the case was filed. Pinned by `business_entity_7m_uniquely_higher_than_individual_2m_invariant` ($3M individual fails, $3M business satisfies), `individual_net_worth_above_2m_disqualifies`, `individual_net_worth_at_2m_boundary_satisfied`, `business_entity_net_worth_above_7m_disqualifies`, `business_entity_net_worth_7m_ceiling_uniquely_higher`.

**500-employee ceiling applies regardless of net worth.** A multinational with $1M net worth but 501 employees fails the size test. Pinned by `employee_count_above_500_disqualifies_regardless_of_net_worth`, `employee_count_at_500_satisfied` (boundary), `employee_count_at_501_uniquely_disqualifies`.

**IRS substantial justification defense defeats prevailing party status UNLESS QO rule applies.** Per § 7430(c)(4)(B), the IRS can avoid fee-shifting if its position had a "reasonable basis in law and fact." Pinned by `irs_position_substantially_justified_defeats_prevailing_party` (no QO → not prevailing) vs `qualified_offer_rule_bypasses_substantial_justification_defense` (QO engaged → prevailing despite IRS justification).

**Administrative exhaustion is a HARD prerequisite — substantively prevailing doesn't save fee award without exhaustion.** Pinned by `administrative_remedies_not_exhausted_zeros_fee_award` and `substantively_prevailed_but_not_exhausted_zeros_fees` (status = SubstantivelyPrevailed but fees = $0).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7430`. Twenty-seven tests pin: **substantively prevailed individual within net worth awards fees** (2026 $260/hr × 100 hrs = $26K); **IRS position substantially justified defeats prevailing party**; **individual net worth above $2M disqualifies**; **individual net worth at $2M boundary satisfied**; **business entity net worth $7M ceiling uniquely higher** ($5M qualifies); **business entity net worth above $7M disqualifies**; **employee count above 500 disqualifies regardless of net worth**; **employee count at 500 satisfied** (boundary); **administrative remedies not exhausted zeros fee award**; **qualified offer engages when QO liability ≥ judgment**; **qualified offer does not engage when QO liability < judgment**; **qualified offer rule bypasses substantial justification defense**; **hourly cap 2026 is $260**; **hourly cap 2025 is $250**; **hourly cap pre-1996 falls back to $125 base**; **max fees calculation = hourly cap × hours**; **citation pins all subsections + 28 U.S.C. § 2412(d)(2)(B) + § 301.7430-2 + Rev. Proc. 2025-32 + IRM 35.10.1**; **business entity $7M uniquely higher than individual $2M invariant** ($3M test); **note describes 2026 $260/hour cap**; **substantively prevailed but not exhausted zeros fees**; **qualified offer rule only engaged when offer made**; **employee count at 501 uniquely disqualifies**; **negative net worth satisfies test** (defensive); **qualified offer exactly at judgment engages rule** (≥ boundary); **qualified offer one cent below judgment does not engage** (< boundary); **not prevailing zeros fee award**; **three gating tests all must pass for fee award invariant** (net worth + employee count + exhaustion).

`traderview-expense::section_7433` is the **IRC § 7433 civil damages for unauthorized IRS collection actions** module — when IRS officer or employee recklessly OR intentionally OR by reason of negligence disregards any IRC provision or regulation in connection with collection of federal tax, the taxpayer may bring civil action against the United States in district court. Trader-relevant for wrongful levy beyond statutory limits + lien filed without notice + collection during § 6330 CDP appeal + § 6331/§ 6332/§ 6334 violations. Procedural-companion to `section_7421` (Anti-Injunction Act + § 7426 wrongful-levy exception), `section_7430` (litigation costs against IRS), `section_7521` (interview procedure), `section_7525` (FATP privilege), and `section_7811` (Taxpayer Assistance Orders).

**§ 7433 four-gate test** (all required) before judgment for damages may be awarded:

| Gate | Source | Failure mode |
|------|--------|--------------|
| Disregard of IRC provision | § 7433(a) | IRS conduct did not disregard IRC provision or regulation |
| In connection with collection | § 7433(a) | Conduct in assessment / examination / other non-collection context |
| Administrative remedies exhausted | § 7433(d)(1) | Plaintiff did not exhaust administrative remedies within IRS |
| Within 2-year SOL from accrual | § 7433(d)(3) | Action not brought within 2 years; without regard to amount in controversy |

Pinned by `reckless_base_passes_below_cap`, `no_disregard_no_action`, `assessment_context_not_collection_no_action`, `exhaustion_required_blocks_judgment`, `outside_2_year_sol_blocks_judgment`, `all_4_gates_failed_stacks_4_reasons` (exactly 4 reasons), and `exhaustion_failure_zeros_damages`.

**§ 7433(b)(1) culpability-tiered damages cap**:

| Culpability | Cap (cents) | Cap (dollars) |
|-------------|-------------|---------------|
| Negligence (§ 7433(b)(1)(A)) | $10,000,000,000 | **$100,000** |
| Reckless (§ 7433(b)(1)(B)) | $100,000,000,000 | **$1,000,000** |
| Intentional (§ 7433(b)(1)(B)) | $100,000,000,000 | **$1,000,000** |

Damages = min(cap, actual direct economic damages net of mitigable + costs of action). Pinned by `culpability_truth_table` (3-tier sweep), `negligence_cap_uniquely_lower_than_reckless_intentional` (reckless = 10× negligence), `negligence_uniquely_smaller_cap_invariant`, `reckless_and_intentional_same_cap_invariant`, `reckless_cap_1m_engages_high_damages`, `negligence_cap_100k_engages_high_damages`, `intentional_cap_1m_same_as_reckless`, `cap_at_negligence_boundary_engages` (exactly $100K compliant), and `cap_1_cent_over_negligence_boundary_engages` (precision boundary).

**§ 7433(d)(2) mitigation reduction** — damages reduced by amount that could have been reasonably mitigated by plaintiff. Mitigation amount capped at actual damages to prevent negative net (cannot exceed what the plaintiff sustained). Pinned by `mitigation_reduction_applies`, `mitigation_capped_at_actual_damages` (mitigation ≤ actual), `mitigation_full_offset_drives_to_costs_only` (full mitigation leaves only costs of action), and `defensive_negative_mitigable_treated_as_zero`.

**§ 7433A parallel regime for qualified tax collection contractors** — post-2015 ATA reforms extended § 7433 framework to private tax collection contractors operating under qualified tax collection contracts with the IRS. Pinned by `note_pins_7433a_parallel_regime`.

**Defensive arithmetic** — negative damages / costs / mitigable amounts all clamped to 0 via `.max(0)`. Saturating subtraction (`actual - mitigable`) and saturating addition (`net + costs`) prevent overflow. Pinned by `defensive_negative_damages_clamped`, `defensive_negative_costs_clamped`, `defensive_negative_mitigable_treated_as_zero`, and `defensive_overflow_gross_saturating` (i64::MAX × 2 → caps at $1M).

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7433`. Thirty-one tests pin: **reckless base passes below cap**; **reckless cap $1M engages high damages**; **negligence cap $100K engages high damages**; **intentional cap $1M same as reckless**; **no disregard no action**; **assessment context not collection no action**; **exhaustion required blocks judgment**; **outside 2-year SOL blocks judgment**; **mitigation reduction applies**; **mitigation capped at actual damages**; **costs of action added to damages**; **negligence cap uniquely lower than reckless intentional** (reckless 10× negligence); **culpability truth table** (3-tier sweep); **all 4 gates failed stacks 4 reasons**; **cap at negligence boundary engages** ($100K); **cap 1¢ over negligence boundary engages** (precision); **defensive negative damages clamped**; **defensive negative costs clamped**; **defensive negative mitigable treated as zero**; **defensive overflow gross saturating** (i64::MAX); **citation pins all subsections** (a) + (b)(1) + (d)(1) + (d)(2) + (d)(3) + § 7433A + 26 CFR § 301.7433-1 + IRM 25.3.3; **note pins culpability tiers**; **note pins cap amounts** ($1M + $100K + negligence); **note pins exhaustion mitigation SOL**; **note pins § 7433A parallel regime**; **exhaustion failure zeros damages**; **mitigation full offset drives to costs only**; **negligence uniquely smaller cap invariant**; **reckless and intentional same cap invariant**; **precise actual $55,000 + costs $5,000 = $60,000**; **zero damages zero costs yields zero**.

`traderview-expense::section_7434` is the **IRC § 7434 civil damages for fraudulent filing of information returns** module — trader-relevant CIVIL remedy when a third party (employer, broker, payor) willfully files a fraudulent W-2, 1099, or other information return against the taxpayer. Distinct from criminal statutes (`section_7201` evasion, `section_7202` collection failure, `section_7203` failure to file, `section_7206` perjury), civil fraud (`section_6663`), and TFRP (`section_6672`). § 7434 is a VICTIM'S civil cause of action, not a government enforcement tool.

**Trader-relevant scenarios**:
- Broker files incorrect 1099-B inflating proceeds → triggers wash-sale / § 1256 / § 988 false deficiency notices
- Employer files false W-2 inflating wages → triggers SE-tax / FICA-tax false deficiency
- Payor files fake 1099-NEC inflating payments
- Retaliatory false W-2 / 1099 from former employer

**Statutory elements** (preponderance of evidence civil burden):

1. Any PERSON (defendant)
2. WILLFULLY FILED a fraudulent information return
3. Information return was FRAUDULENT
4. With respect to payments purported to be made to any other person (the plaintiff-taxpayer)

All four required. Pinned by `civil_action_authorized_truth_table` (8-cell sweep willful × fraudulent × misstatement).

**§ 7434(b) damages — greater of**:

| Component | Recovery |
|-----------|------------|
| Statutory minimum | $5,000 |
| OR Actual damages | Sum of actual incurred |
| PLUS court costs | Recovered |
| PLUS attorney fees | At court's discretion |

Pinned by `statutory_minimum_5000_when_actual_below`, `actual_damages_when_above_5000_minimum`, `court_costs_recovered_when_authorized`, `attorney_fees_recovered_when_awarded`, `attorney_fees_zero_when_not_awarded`, `total_recovery_sums_three_components`, `statutory_minimum_5000_is_5000`.

**Derolf v. Risinger Bros. misclassification carveout** — most courts hold that misclassification as INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR (1099-MISC / 1099-NEC instead of W-2) does NOT give a § 7434 cause of action when the dollar amount is otherwise correct. Plaintiff must allege **FRAUDULENT AMOUNT MISSTATEMENT**, not merely wrong form classification. Critical scope limitation. Pinned by `derolf_misclassification_carveout_defeats_action` and `amount_misstatement_with_misclassification_still_authorizes` (amount misstatement saves claim even when misclassification also alleged).

**§ 7434(d) statute of limitations — later of**:

- **6 YEARS** after filing of the fraudulent information return, OR
- **1 YEAR** after the date the fraudulent return would have been discovered by exercise of reasonable care

Pinned by `sol_within_6_year_satisfied`, `sol_at_6_year_boundary_expired`, `sol_satisfied_under_1_year_discovery_even_if_over_6_filing`, `sol_at_1_year_discovery_boundary_expired_if_over_6_filing`, `sol_truth_table_filing_x_discovery` (12-cell sweep).

**§ 7434(e) — plaintiff must provide copy of complaint to Secretary** (IRS notice) upon filing civil action.

**Trader-tax-defense leverage** — § 7434 civil judgment provides COLLATERAL-ESTOPPEL leverage in any subsequent Tax Court / refund litigation arising from the fraudulent 1099 / W-2 deficiency notice. The judgment establishes the information return's falsity as a matter of preclusion in tax controversy.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7434`. Thirty-one tests pin: **full claim authorizes civil action**; **statutory minimum $5,000 when actual below**; **actual damages when above $5,000 minimum**; **court costs recovered when authorized**; **attorney fees recovered when awarded**; **attorney fees zero when not awarded**; **total recovery sums three components**; **no willful filing defeats action**; **no fraudulent return defeats action**; **Derolf misclassification carveout defeats action** (1099 instead of W-2 without amount misstatement); **amount misstatement with misclassification still authorizes**; **SOL within 6-year satisfied**; **SOL at 6-year boundary expired**; **SOL satisfied under 1-year discovery even if over 6 filing**; **SOL at 1-year discovery boundary expired if over 6 filing**; **SOL truth table filing × discovery** (12-cell sweep); **expired SOL defeats action**; **citation pins subsections and Derolf** (§§ 7434(a)/(b)/(c)/(d)/(e)/(f) + Derolf v. Risinger Bros.); **return type routed in note** (W-2 + 1099-B + 1099-NEC + 1099-MISC + 1099-DIV + 1099-INT); **cause of action note describes § 7434(a)**; **notice to Secretary note present** (§ 7434(e) IRS notice); **collateral estoppel note present** (Tax Court leverage); **negative actual damages clamped to zero**; **negative court costs clamped to zero**; **negative attorney fees clamped to zero**; **six information return types routed correctly**; **damages note describes greater-of rule** (§ 7434(b)); **SOL note describes later-of rule** (§ 7434(d)); **civil action authorized truth table** (8-cell sweep willful × fraudulent × misstatement); **statutory minimum $5,000 is $5,000**; **full recovery note describes total**.

`traderview-expense::section_7463` is the **IRC § 7463 Disputes Involving $50,000 or Less (Tax Court Small Case Procedure / "S case" election)** module — trader-relevant for traders with smaller audit deficiencies seeking faster + cheaper resolution at Tax Court without formal rules of evidence and without appellate review. Companion to `section_6213` (Tax Court petition deadline), `section_6212` (SNOD), `section_7459` (Tax Court decisions), Tax Court Rules 170-175 (small case procedural rules).

**§ 7463(a) Designation** — petitions filed with Tax Court for redetermination of deficiency where neither the amount of the deficiency placed in dispute nor the amount of any claimed overpayment exceeds **$50,000 per taxable year (income tax)**, **$50,000 per estate (estate tax)**, **$50,000 per calendar year (gift tax)**, or **$50,000 per taxable period or event (excise tax)**. Proceedings conducted at the **option of the taxpayer**, concurred in by the Tax Court or a division thereof, **BEFORE THE HEARING** of the case.

**§ 7463(b) Finality of decisions** — a decision entered in any case conducted under § 7463 **SHALL NOT BE REVIEWED IN ANY OTHER COURT** and **SHALL NOT BE TREATED AS A PRECEDENT** for any other case. Critical procedural tradeoff — faster + cheaper + informal but **NO APPEAL + NO PRECEDENTIAL VALUE**.

**§ 7463(c) Limit on review** — taxpayer or the Secretary may concur to discontinue § 7463 designation BEFORE the case decision becomes final.

**§ 7463(d) Procedure** — proceedings conducted under Tax Court Rules 170-175; "as informally as possible consistent with orderly procedure, and any evidence deemed by the Court to have probative value shall be admissible".

**§ 7463(f) Additional cases** — small case procedure also available for: (1) § 6320 (CDP-lien) + § 6330 (CDP-levy) collection-determination proceedings under $50,000; (2) § 6015 innocent-spouse-relief proceedings under $50,000; (3) § 7436 worker-classification (1099 vs W-2) proceedings under $50,000.

**Procedural tradeoff** — S case **advantages**: faster (typically 6-12 months vs 18-36 months regular); cheaper (no transcript required); informal (relaxed rules of evidence); pro se friendly. S case **disadvantages**: NO APPEAL to Circuit Court + Supreme Court (decision is FINAL); NO PRECEDENTIAL VALUE; informal nature may favor IRS-experienced counsel.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7463`. Twenty-seven tests pin: **income tax $40K within limit designation available**; **at $50,000 boundary compliant**; **$50,001 exceeds limit**; **estate tax in scope**; **gift tax in scope**; **excise tax in scope**; **§ 6320 CDP-lien in scope** (§ 7463(f)); **§ 6330 CDP-levy in scope**; **§ 6015 innocent spouse in scope**; **§ 7436 worker classification in scope**; **no taxpayer election no designation**; **no Tax Court concurrence no designation** (BEFORE the hearing); **discontinued designation appealable**; **S case active decision final no appeal**; **citation pins all authorities** (§ 7463(a)-(f) + Tax Court Rules 170-175 + § 6212 + § 6213 + § 7459 + § 6320 + § 6330 + § 6015 + § 7436 + IRM 35.1.3); **note pins § 7463(a) $50K limit** (estate + gift + excise); **note pins § 7463(a) concurrence before hearing**; **note pins § 7463(b) no review no precedent**; **note pins § 7463(c) discontinue motion**; **note pins § 7463(d) informal rules** (Rules 170-175 + probative value); **note pins § 7463(f) collection + innocent spouse + worker** (§ 6320 + § 6330 + § 6015 + § 7436); **note pins procedural tradeoff** (FASTER + CHEAPER + INFORMAL + NO APPEAL + NO PRECEDENTIAL VALUE); **case type truth table 8 cells** (all 8 case types in scope); **amount boundary invariant** (50K ok, 50K+1 over); **S case active blocks appeal invariant**; **defensive zero amount designation available**; **multiple failures stack** (2 violations).

`traderview-expense::section_7491` is the **IRC § 7491 burden of proof shifts to Secretary** module — civil procedural defense that flips the burden of proof from taxpayer to IRS once taxpayer satisfies threshold requirements. Distinct from `section_6664` (reasonable cause + good faith defense to penalties), § 7454(a) (fraud-burden specific to § 6663 + accumulated earnings), and `section_6663` (civil fraud penalty). Highly relevant for aggressive § 1256 mark-to-market, § 988 currency, § 1202 QSBS holding-period, and § 475(f) trader-tax-status disputes.

**Three burden-shift subsections**:

| Subsection | Trigger | Burden type | Scope |
|------------|---------|---------------|---------|
| § 7491(a)(1) | Credible evidence + 3 thresholds (a)(2) | Burden of proof (full persuasion) | Subtitle A income / B estate/gift/GST |
| § 7491(b) | Statistical reconstruction by IRS | Burden of proof | Individual Subtitle A only |
| § 7491(c) | IRS asserts penalty / addition to tax | Burden of production (lower bar) | All proceedings, all penalties |

**§ 7491(a)(1) — General factual burden shift**. If taxpayer introduces CREDIBLE EVIDENCE on a factual issue relevant to liability under Subtitle A (income, SE, NIIT) or B (estate, gift, GST), the Secretary bears the burden of proof on that issue. "Credible evidence" = quality of evidence the court would find sufficient to base a decision on if no contrary evidence were submitted (post-critical-analysis test).

**§ 7491(a)(2) — Three threshold conditions (conjunctive)**:
- **§ 7491(a)(2)(A) — Substantiation**. Taxpayer complied with substantiation requirements.
- **§ 7491(a)(2)(B) — Records**. Taxpayer maintained all required records.
- **§ 7491(a)(2)(C) — Cooperation**. Taxpayer cooperated with reasonable IRS requests for witnesses, information, documents, meetings, interviews.

All three required; missing any one defeats (a)(1). Pinned by `three_threshold_conjunctive_truth_table` (8-cell sweep across A × B × C).

**§ 7491(a)(2)(C) — Net worth limitation**. Corporations, partnerships, and trusts with net worth EXCEEDING $7,000,000 at time of proceeding are EXCLUDED from (a)(1) burden-shifting. **Individuals and estates not subject to net-worth limitation regardless of size**. Pinned by `corporation_net_worth_above_7m_limitation_engaged`, `corporation_net_worth_at_7m_boundary_no_limitation` (boundary), `individual_unlimited_by_net_worth`, `estate_not_subject_to_7m_limitation`, `net_worth_limitation_only_for_corp_partnership_trust`.

**§ 7491(b) — Statistical reconstruction burden**. In Subtitle A court proceeding involving an INDIVIDUAL, the Secretary bears burden on any income item reconstructed by statistical methods from unrelated taxpayers (BLS surveys, market-segment analysis). Limited to INDIVIDUAL Subtitle A only — corporations + estates + Subtitle B not covered. Pinned by `statistical_reconstruction_individual_subtitle_a_engages_b`, `statistical_reconstruction_corporation_not_engaged`, `statistical_reconstruction_estate_subtitle_b_not_engaged`.

**§ 7491(c) — Penalty production burden (independent)**. Secretary bears BURDEN OF PRODUCTION (not persuasion) for any penalty or addition to tax — § 6651, § 6662, § 6663, § 6672, etc. IRS must come forward with evidence supporting the penalty before taxpayer must rebut. Lower bar than full burden of persuasion. **Independent of (a)(1) — engages even when (a)(1) threshold not met.** Pinned by `penalty_production_burden_independent_of_other_subsections`.

**Scope of § 7491(a)** — limited to Subtitle A (income) and Subtitle B (estate/gift/GST) taxes. **Subtitle C (employment) and Subtitle D (excise) outside scope**. Pinned by `employment_tax_outside_burden_shift_scope` and `excise_tax_outside_burden_shift_scope`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7491`. Thirty tests pin: **individual full compliance burden shifts**; **missing credible evidence no shift**; **missing substantiation no shift** (§ 7491(a)(2)(A)); **missing records no shift** (§ 7491(a)(2)(B)); **missing cooperation no shift** (§ 7491(a)(2)(C)); **three threshold conjunctive truth table** (8-cell sweep A × B × C); **corporation net worth above $7M limitation engaged**; **corporation net worth at $7M boundary no limitation**; **partnership net worth above $7M limitation engaged**; **trust net worth above $7M limitation engaged**; **individual unlimited by net worth** (no limitation regardless of size); **estate not subject to $7M limitation**; **employment tax outside burden shift scope** (Subtitle C); **excise tax outside burden shift scope** (Subtitle D); **estate / gift / GST within scope** (Subtitle B); **statistical reconstruction individual Subtitle A engages (b)** (BLS surveys + market-segment analysis); **statistical reconstruction corporation not engaged**; **statistical reconstruction estate Subtitle B not engaged**; **penalty production burden engaged** (§ 7491(c) BURDEN OF PRODUCTION not persuasion); **penalty production burden not engaged when no penalty**; **penalty production burden independent of other subsections** (engages even without (a)(1)); **any burden shift engaged when any subsection engaged**; **any burden shift not engaged when no subsection**; **credible evidence note describes post-critical-analysis**; **three threshold satisfied note lists (A) substantiation + (B) records + (C) cooperation**; **RRA 1998 enactment note present** (Pub. L. No. 105-206); **citation pins all subsections** (§§ 7491(a)(1) / 7491(a)(2)(A) / 7491(a)(2)(B) / 7491(a)(2)(C) / 7491(b) / 7491(c) + § 7454(a) + § 6664(c) cross-references); **three subsections independent routing** (8-cell sweep credible × statistical × penalty); **net worth limitation only for corp / partnership / trust**; **corp with low net worth can engage (a)(1) shift**.

`traderview-expense::section_7206` is the **IRC § 7206 fraud and false statements (criminal felony — "tax perjury" statute)** module — criminal counterpart to `section_6663` civil fraud 75% penalty. Five enumerated criminal tax offenses with up to 3 years imprisonment + $250K individual / $500K corporation fine under 18 U.S.C. § 3571. Natural sibling to `section_6663` (civil fraud), `section_6664` (reasonable cause defense), `section_6501` (§ 6501(c)(1) unlimited ASED for fraud), and `section_7491` (burden shifts — criminal cases OUTSIDE § 7491 scope).

**Five enumerated offenses**:

| Subsection | Crime | SOL (§ 6531) |
|------------|-------|---------------|
| § 7206(1) | Tax perjury — false return signed under penalty of perjury | 6 years |
| § 7206(2) | Aiding or assisting false document (preparers/advisors/third parties) | 6 years |
| § 7206(3) | Fraudulent bonds, permits, entries | 6 years |
| § 7206(4) | Removal or concealment of taxed goods with intent to defraud | 6 years |
| § 7206(5) | Compromises and closing-agreement fraud (§§ 7121/7122) | 3 years |

**§ 7206(1) five-element test** (government bears BEYOND REASONABLE DOUBT burden):

1. Defendant MADE AND SUBSCRIBED a return / statement / other document
2. Document was FALSE as to a MATERIAL matter
3. Document contained a written DECLARATION UNDER PENALTIES OF PERJURY
4. Defendant DID NOT BELIEVE the document to be true and correct as to every material matter
5. Defendant ACTED WILLFULLY with specific intent to violate the law

All five required; failure of any element defeats prosecution. Pinned by `section_1_five_element_truth_table` (32-cell sweep across all five elements).

**§ 7206(2) reach beyond signing taxpayer**. Aids / assists / procures / counsels / advises preparation of false document. Reaches **return preparers, advisors, and third parties EVEN WHEN the taxpayer-signer is innocent**. Pinned by `section_2_aiding_full_elements_authorizes` and `section_2_aiding_element_note_describes_reach`.

**Cheek defense — Cheek v. United States, 498 U.S. 192 (1991)**. Good-faith subjective MISUNDERSTANDING of the law negates willfulness element (subjective belief test). **NOT** a defense for disagreement with the law (constitutional challenges, tax-protester arguments). Most important taxpayer defense to § 7206(1) prosecution. Pinned by `cheek_defense_successful_defeats_prosecution`, `cheek_defense_unsuccessful_does_not_defeat`, `cheek_only_defeats_when_successful_and_asserted` (4-cell sweep across asserted × successful).

**Penalties**:
- Imprisonment up to 3 YEARS
- Fine $250K individual / $500K corporation (18 U.S.C. § 3571 Criminal Fines Improvement Act supersedes § 7206's original $100K cap)
- BOTH imprisonment and fine permitted, plus costs of prosecution

**§ 6531 criminal statute of limitations** — 6 years for § 7206(1)/(2)/(3)/(4); 3 years for § 7206(5) compromises and closing agreements. Pinned by `sol_truth_table_per_subsection` (5-subsection sweep).

**Spies-Daly doctrine — parallel civil + criminal**. § 7206 criminal prosecution may PROCEED IN PARALLEL with § 6663 civil fraud 75% penalty. Double jeopardy does NOT bar civil penalty after criminal conviction (or acquittal). § 6501(c)(1) UNLIMITED ASED applies once fraud established; § 6501(c)(2) UNLIMITED ASED for willful evasion. **§ 7491 burden shifts do NOT apply to criminal prosecutions** — government bears beyond-reasonable-doubt burden on each element. Pinned by `section_6663_civil_parallel_always_available`, `unlimited_ased_cross_reference_note`, `section_7491_burden_shifts_excluded_note`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7206`. Twenty-nine tests pin: **§ 7206(1) all five elements authorizes prosecution**; **§ 7206(1) missing any element no prosecution**; **§ 7206(1) five-element truth table** (32-cell sweep e1 × e2 × e3 × e4 × e5); **Cheek defense successful defeats prosecution** (498 U.S. 192 (1991)); **Cheek defense unsuccessful does not defeat**; **Cheek not asserted no Cheek engagement**; **Cheek case note describes subjective belief test**; **Cheek only defeats when successful and asserted** (4-cell sweep); **individual max fine $250K** (18 U.S.C. § 3571); **corporation max fine $500K**; **maximum imprisonment always 3 years** (5-subsection invariant); **§ 7206(1)/(2)/(3)/(4) six-year SOL** (§ 6531); **§ 7206(5) three-year SOL**; **SOL truth table per subsection** (5-subsection sweep); **§ 7206(2) aiding full elements authorizes**; **§ 7206(2) aiding missing aid no prosecution**; **§ 7206(2) aiding missing willfulness no prosecution**; **§ 7206(2) Cheek defeats aiding**; **§ 7206(2) aiding element note describes reach** (preparers + advisors + third parties even when taxpayer-signer innocent); **§ 6663 civil parallel always available** (Spies-Daly doctrine); **unlimited ASED cross-reference note** (§ 6501(c)(1)); **§ 7491 burden shifts excluded note** (BEYOND REASONABLE DOUBT); **CFIA 18 U.S.C. § 3571 supersedes note**; **IRM 9.1.3 note present**; **citation pins all subsections** (§§ 7206(1)/(2)/(3)/(4)/(5) + § 6531 + § 6663 + § 6501(c)(1) + § 6501(c)(2) + 18 U.S.C. § 3571 + Cheek + IRM 9.1.3); **five subsections routed correctly**; **elements count zero when no elements**; **elements count increments per element**; **§ 7206(1) five-element note present** (5/5 satisfied note).

`traderview-expense::section_7207` is the **IRC § 7207 fraudulent returns, statements, or other documents (criminal misdemeanor)** module — pairs with `section_7206` (felony perjury alternative when document signed under penalties of perjury) and `section_7434` (civil damages for fraudulent information return). Completes the false-documents criminal statute trio. Criminal misdemeanor 1-year imprisonment + $100K individual / $200K corporation fine under 18 U.S.C. § 3571 (supersedes original $10K / $50K caps).

**Three-element test** (BEYOND REASONABLE DOUBT):

1. DELIVERY OR DISCLOSURE to an IRS officer or employee of any list, return, account, statement, or OTHER DOCUMENT
2. Document was FALSE OR FRAUDULENT as to a MATERIAL MATTER
3. Done WILLFULLY or with knowledge of the falsity or fraud

All three required; failure of any defeats prosecution. Pinned by `three_element_truth_table` (8-cell sweep).

**Distinction from § 7206**:

| Provision | Penalty | Document scope |
|-----------|---------|------------------|
| § 7206(1) FELONY | 3-year imprisonment | Documents signed under penalties of perjury (e.g., tax returns) |
| § 7207 MISDEMEANOR | 1-year imprisonment | ANY false document delivered to IRS regardless of perjury verification |

Government typically charges § 7207 when (a) computed tax deficiency is de minimis, (b) document was NOT signed under penalties of perjury, or (c) prosecutorial discretion favors misdemeanor over felony. Pinned by `de_minimis_deficiency_note_present`, `distinction_from_7206_note_describes_perjury_scope`.

**§ 7206(1) felony alternative available** — when document IS signed under penalties of perjury AND all § 7207 elements satisfied, § 7206(1) felony alternative becomes available (prosecutor's discretion). Pinned by `signed_under_perjury_triggers_7206_alternative`, `felony_alternative_only_when_signed_and_all_three_elements`, `cheek_engaged_disables_felony_alternative`.

**Cheek defense — Cheek v. United States, 498 U.S. 192 (1991)**. Good-faith subjective belief negates willfulness element. Same defense as § 7201 / § 7203 / § 7206. Pinned by `cheek_only_defeats_when_successful_and_asserted` (4-cell sweep) and `cheek_engaged_disables_felony_alternative`.

**§ 6531(2) 6-year criminal SOL** for § 7207 (enumerated 6-year offense). Pinned by `criminal_sol_6_years`.

**Penalties**:
- Imprisonment up to **1 YEAR** (misdemeanor — invariant regardless of perjury verification)
- Fine $100K individual / $200K corporation under 18 U.S.C. § 3571 (supersedes original $10K / $50K)
- BOTH imprisonment and fine permitted
- Costs of prosecution

**Typical § 7207 fact patterns** — fabricated receipts during audit + altered Schedule K-1 + fraudulent supporting documents + false Form 433 collection information statement. Pinned by `typical_fact_patterns_note_present`.

**Spies-Daly parallel civil consequences** — § 7434 civil damages for fraudulent information return + § 6663 civil fraud 75% penalty + § 6501(c)(1) UNLIMITED ASED for fraud. Pinned by `spies_daly_parallel_civil_note_present`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7207`. Twenty-six tests pin: **full three elements authorizes misdemeanor**; **individual max fine $100K** (18 U.S.C. § 3571); **corporation max fine $200K**; **missing delivery element defeats**; **missing falsity element defeats**; **missing willfulness element defeats**; **three-element truth table** (8-cell sweep); **Cheek defense successful defeats prosecution** (498 U.S. 192 (1991)); **Cheek only defeats when successful and asserted** (4-cell sweep); **signed under perjury triggers § 7206 alternative** (felony 3-year + $250K); **not signed under perjury no § 7206 alternative**; **de minimis deficiency note present** (prosecutorial discretion); **criminal SOL 6 years** (§ 6531(2)); **CFIA supersedes fine caps note** (18 U.S.C. § 3571); **Spies-Daly parallel civil note present** (§ 7434 + § 6663 + § 6501(c)(1)); **typical fact patterns note present** (fabricated receipts + altered K-1 + Form 433); **citation pins all authorities** (§§ 7207/6531/7206/7434/7212/6663/6501(c)(1) + 18 U.S.C. § 3571 + Cheek + IRM 9.1.3); **maximum imprisonment always 1 year** (perjury-invariant); **distinction from § 7206 note describes perjury scope**; **three-element count increments per element**; **misdemeanor authorized note describes pathway**; **felony alternative only when signed and all three elements**; **Cheek engaged disables felony alternative**; **IRM 9.1.3 note present**; **Cheek case note describes subjective belief**; **prosecution not authorized when elements missing note**.

`traderview-expense::section_7212` is the **IRC § 7212 attempts to interfere with administration of internal revenue laws (criminal felony)** module — pairs with `section_7201` (5-year evasion felony), `section_7202` (5-year trust-fund-tax felony), `section_7203` (1-year misdemeanor failure to file), `section_7206` (3-year felony perjury), and `section_7434` (civil damages for fraudulent information return). 3-year FELONY for corruptly OR by force/threats impeding IRS administration + $250K individual / $500K corporation fine under 18 U.S.C. § 3571.

**§ 7212(a) two clauses**:

| Clause | Test | Marinello required |
|--------|------|----------------------|
| Officer-specific | Corruptly OR force/threats endeavors to intimidate/impede IRS officer in official capacity | NO |
| Omnibus | Corruptly OR force/threats obstructs due administration of Title 26 | YES |

**Officer-specific clause three-element test** (BEYOND REASONABLE DOUBT):

1. Use of FORCE OR THREATS OF FORCE (or corrupt action)
2. To INTIMIDATE, IMPEDE, OR OBSTRUCT
3. Officer or employee of US acting in OFFICIAL CAPACITY under Title 26

Pinned by `officer_specific_three_element_truth_table` (8-cell sweep).

**Omnibus clause three-element test + Marinello nexus**:

1. CORRUPTLY ACTED (or force/threats)
2. With INTENT TO SECURE UNLAWFUL BENEFIT
3. OBSTRUCTED or impeded DUE ADMINISTRATION of Title 26

PLUS **Marinello v. United States, 138 S. Ct. 1101 (2018) nexus** to known pending OR reasonably foreseeable proceeding. Marinello narrowed omnibus clause to EXCLUDE routine non-compliance with tax code requirements absent the proceeding-nexus. Pinned by `omnibus_without_marinello_nexus_defeats` and `marinello_routine_noncompliance_carveout_note_when_missing`.

**§ 7212(a) threats-only downgrade**. When offense is committed ONLY by threats of force (no actual force + no corrupt act), penalty reduces to: fine up to $3,000 + imprisonment up to **1 YEAR (misdemeanor)**. Otherwise: fine up to $5,000 (superseded by 18 U.S.C. § 3571 to $250K individual / $500K corporation) + imprisonment up to **3 YEARS (felony)**. Pinned by `threats_only_with_intimidation_engages_misdemeanor_path` and `imprisonment_1_year_when_misdemeanor_threats_only`.

**"Corrupt" defined** — judicial gloss: act performed with INTENTION TO SECURE AN UNLAWFUL BENEFIT.

**§ 6531 criminal SOL** — general **3-year** criminal SOL applies to § 7212 (NOT extended 6-year as for § 7201 / § 7203 / § 7206(1)-(4)). Notable shorter window. Pinned by `criminal_sol_3_years_general`.

**Spies-Daly parallel civil consequences**. § 7212 prosecution does not bar § 6663 civil fraud + § 6672 TFRP + other civil penalties arising from underlying conduct. § 6501(c)(1) UNLIMITED ASED for fraud if proven.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7212`. Thirty-two tests pin: **officer-specific full authorizes felony**; **officer-specific missing force defeats**; **officer-specific missing intimidation defeats**; **officer-specific missing official capacity defeats**; **omnibus full with Marinello authorizes felony**; **omnibus without Marinello nexus defeats** (routine non-compliance carveout); **omnibus missing corrupt defeats**; **omnibus missing unlawful benefit defeats**; **omnibus missing obstruction defeats**; **threats-only pathway engages misdemeanor**; **threats-only with intimidation engages misdemeanor path** (1-year + $100K individual); **threats-only individual max fine $100K**; **threats-only corporation max fine $200K**; **felony individual max fine $250K** (18 U.S.C. § 3571); **felony corporation max fine $500K**; **corrupt act alone does not trigger threats-only**; **Marinello nexus satisfied note present when satisfied**; **Marinello routine noncompliance carveout note when missing**; **corrupt definition note present** (INTENTION TO SECURE UNLAWFUL BENEFIT); **criminal SOL 3 years general** (§ 6531); **parallel civil consequences note present** (§ 6663 + § 6672 + § 6501(c)(1)); **citation pins all authorities** (§§ 7212(a)/(b) + 6531 + 6663 + 6672 + 6501(c)(1) + 18 U.S.C. § 3571 + Marinello + IRM 9.1.3); **officer-specific three-element truth table** (8-cell sweep); **omnibus Marinello required alongside three elements**; **imprisonment 3 years when felony**; **imprisonment 1 year when misdemeanor threats-only**; **CFIA 18 U.S.C. § 3571 supersedes note when felony**; **threats-only downgrade note when misdemeanor**; **prosecution authorized note describes pathway** (FELONY vs misdemeanor); **two clauses note always present** (officer-specific + omnibus); **IRM 9.1.3 note present**; **clause target routing distinct** (officer-specific vs omnibus notes).

`traderview-expense::section_7216` is the **IRC § 7216 disclosure or use of return information by preparers (criminal misdemeanor)** module — pairs with **§ 6713 civil penalty** ($250 per prohibited disclosure + $10,000 annual cap) for the same conduct. Criminal misdemeanor 1-year imprisonment + $100K individual / $200K corporation fine under 18 U.S.C. § 3571 (supersedes § 7216's original $1K cap). Distinct trader-relevant: when broker, CPA, or tax preparer discloses trader's return information without authorization for purpose other than preparing the return.

**§ 7216(a) — Criminal misdemeanor**. Preparer who KNOWINGLY OR RECKLESSLY discloses or uses tax return information for any purpose other than preparing the tax return.

**§ 7216(b) two-pathway exceptions**:

| Exception | Authority | Requirement |
|-----------|-----------|---------------|
| Taxpayer consent | 26 CFR § 301.7216-3 + Rev. Proc. 2013-14 | Written + signed BEFORE disclosure + specific recipient + specific purpose + duration + prominent and separate |
| Permissible non-consent | 26 CFR § 301.7216-2 | Court orders + IRS inquiries + peer review + malpractice insurance + payment processing |

Either exception defeats both criminal and civil liability. Pinned by `consent_truth_table` (4-cell sweep) and `either_exception_pathway_defeats_violation`.

**Penalties**:
- Imprisonment up to **1 YEAR** (misdemeanor)
- Fine $100K individual / $200K corporation under 18 U.S.C. § 3571 (supersedes original $1K cap)
- Costs of prosecution
- **Identity-theft enhancement**: monetary penalty up to $100,000 (separate from § 3571) when disclosure or use is in connection with crime involving identity theft

**§ 6713 civil penalty — paired with § 7216 same conduct**:
- $250 per prohibited disclosure or use
- $10,000 ANNUAL CAP per preparer per calendar year
- **NO-FAULT CIVIL** — does NOT require knowing or reckless conduct (lower standard than § 7216 criminal)

Pinned by `civil_vs_criminal_no_fault_distinction`, `civil_penalty_calculation_5_disclosures`, `civil_penalty_capped_at_10000_annual`, `civil_penalty_at_40_disclosures_at_cap`, `civil_penalty_at_39_disclosures_below_cap`, `civil_penalty_proportional_until_cap` (9-disclosure sweep).

**"Tax return information" broadly defined** — includes any information furnished for, or in connection with, the preparation of any tax return. Reaches financial records, brokerage statements, K-1s, payroll information, source documents.

**§ 6531 general 3-year criminal SOL** applies to § 7216 (NOT extended 6-year as for § 7201 / § 7203 / § 7206(1)-(4) / § 7207).

**Consent regulations 26 CFR § 301.7216-3 + Rev. Proc. 2013-14** — taxpayer consent must satisfy: WRITTEN consent (electronic permitted) + SIGNED BEFORE the disclosure + specifies SPECIFIC RECIPIENT + specifies PURPOSE + DURATION of consent + PROMINENT and SEPARATE from other documents. Consent invalid if extracted by inadequate notice. Pinned by `invalid_consent_does_not_defeat`.

Mounted at `POST /api/calc/section-7216`.
